The South Korean Ministry of Defense stated that its navy seized the boat after it crossed the sea demarcation line, which separates North and South Korea.

These events come as tensions between the two countries are rising again. On Wednesday, North Korea launched two medium-range ballistic missiles into the ocean. South Korea believes these missile tests are a response to ongoing military exercises involving the U.S. and South Korea.

North Korea, through its news agency KCNA, strongly criticized South Korea, accusing it of "provocations." North Korea claimed that on March 24, South Korean "puppet military gangsters" fired bullets and shells near five islands, including Paekryong and Big Yonphyong islands. They also accused South Korea of scattering leaflets that "hurt the dignity of the supreme leadership of the DPRK" (North Korea).

original_text: nighttime sky transparency statistics on mauna kea are reported based on data from the canada - france - hawaii telescope skyprobe monitor . we focus on the period beginning with the start of megacam wide - field optical imager operations in 2003 , and continuing for almost three years . skies were clear enough to observe on 76% of those nights ; attenuations were less than 0.2 magnitudes up to 60% of the time . an empirical model of cloud attenuation and duration is presented allowing us to further characterize the photometric conditions . this is a good fit to the skyprobe data , and indicates that mauna kea skies are truly photometric ( without cloud ) an average of 56% of the time , with moderate seasonal variation . continuous monitoring of transparency during the night is necessary to overcome fluctuations in attenuation due to thin cloud .
simplified_kindergarten:
We look at how clear the sky is at night at Mauna Kea. A special camera called Skyprobe is on a big telescope. It tells us.

We looked at a time from 2003. A camera called Megacam started to work then. We looked for almost three years.

The sky was clear enough to see things on 76 out of 100 nights. The sky was very, very clear for 60 out of 100 nights.

We made a way to guess about clouds. It tells us how much light clouds block. It also tells us how long clouds stay. This helps us know how clear the sky is.

Our guess works well with what Skyprobe saw. It shows that Mauna Kea skies are truly clear. This means no clouds. It is clear for 56 out of 100 nights. This changes a little with the seasons.

We need to watch how clear the sky is all night. This helps us see when thin clouds make the sky a little less clear.
simplified_primary_school:
We have facts about how clear the sky is at night on Mauna Kea. These facts come from a special camera called the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope Skyprobe Monitor. We looked at the time starting in 2003, when a big camera called MegaCam began to work. This was for almost three years.

On 76 out of 100 of those nights, the sky was clear enough to see things. For up to 60 out of 100 times, the sky was very clear, meaning light was dimmed by less than 0.2 magnitudes.

We made a way to guess how much clouds dim the light and for how long. This helps us understand even better how clear the sky is for taking good pictures. This guess works well with the Skyprobe camera's information. It shows that the sky above Mauna Kea is truly clear (without clouds) for 56 out of 100 times on average. The amount of clear sky changes a little bit with the seasons.

We need to keep checking how clear the sky is all night long. This is because thin clouds can make the light dim a little bit, and this changes often.
simplified_secondary_school:
We are reporting statistics about how clear the night sky is over Mauna Kea. These statistics come from data collected by the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope's Skyprobe monitor. We focused on the period starting in 2003, when the MegaCam wide-field optical imager began operations, and continuing for almost three years.

During this time, the sky was clear enough for observations on 76% of the nights. Also, the dimming of starlight (called attenuation) was less than 0.2 magnitudes for up to 60% of the time.

We developed a model, based on our observations, that describes how much clouds dim light and how long they last. This model helps us better understand the "photometric conditions" – meaning how often the sky is clear enough for very precise light measurements. This model fits the Skyprobe data well. It shows that Mauna Kea's skies are truly photometric (completely free of clouds) for an average of 56% of the time, with some moderate changes throughout the year.

To account for changes in how much light is dimmed by thin clouds, it is necessary to continuously monitor the sky's transparency throughout the night.

original_text: computer simulations have been employed in recent years to evaluate the configurational entropy changes in model glass - forming liquids . we consider two methods , both of which involve the calculation of the ` intra - basin ' entropy as a means for obtaining the configurational entropy . the first method involves the evaluation of the intra - basin entropy from the vibrational frequencies of inherent structures , by making a harmonic approximation of the local potential energy topography . the second method employs simulations that confine the liquid within a localized region of configuration space by the imposition of constraints ; apart from the choice of the constraints , no further assumptions are made . we compare the configurational entropies estimated for a model liquid ( binary mixture of particles interacting _ via _ the lennard - jones potential ) for a range of temperatures , at fixed density .
simplified_kindergarten:
Computers help us. They look at how things sit. This is in pretend glass liquid.

We use two ways. Both ways look at 'intra-basin entropy'. This helps us find how things sit.

The first way finds 'intra-basin entropy'. It sees how tiny parts wiggle. It makes a simple guess about their wiggles.

The second way uses computers. It keeps the liquid in a small spot. It uses rules to hold it there. We only pick the rules. We do not guess other things.

We compare how things sit. This is for a pretend liquid. It has two kinds of little parts. They push and pull. We look at different warmths. The liquid stays the same size.
simplified_primary_school:
Scientists have used computers to study how 'configurational entropy' changes in liquids that can turn into glass. This 'entropy' is about how the tiny parts inside a liquid are arranged.

They look at two ways to do this. Both ways need them to figure out something called 'intra-basin' entropy. This helps them find the 'configurational entropy'.

The first way finds the 'intra-basin' entropy by looking at how much the tiny parts of the liquid shake in their natural spots. They make a simple guess about the energy around each part to help them.

The second way uses computer simulations that keep the liquid's parts in a small, specific area of how they can be arranged. They do this by setting some rules. They only choose these rules and don't guess anything else.

They compare the 'configurational entropies' they found for a special liquid. This liquid is made of two different kinds of tiny parts that push and pull on each other in a way called 'Lennard-Jones potential'. They do this at different temperatures, but keep the liquid's thickness the same.
simplified_secondary_school: Recently, computer simulations have been used to calculate changes in the configurational entropy of model liquids that form glass. We use two methods, both of which calculate 'intra-basin' entropy to find the configurational entropy. The first method calculates the intra-basin entropy from the vibration rates of inherent structures. It does this by using a harmonic approximation of the local potential energy landscape. The second method uses simulations that keep the liquid in a small area of its possible arrangements (configuration space) by adding constraints. Other than choosing these constraints, no other assumptions are made. We compare the configurational entropies calculated for a model liquid (a mixture of two types of particles that interact using the Lennard-Jones potential). This comparison is done across different temperatures, while keeping the density constant.

original_text: Baco noir (pronounced BA-koh NWAHR) is a hybrid red wine grape variety produced by Francois Baco from a cross of Vitis vinifera var. Folle blanche, a French wine grape, and an unknown variety of Vitis riparia indigenous to North America. Regions In 1951 the variety was brought to the cooler viticulture regions of North America, such as British Columbia, Ontario, Nova Scotia, New York, Michigan, Mississippi, Wisconsin, Pennsylvania and Oregon. In 1955 the variety was brought back to Canada, where the "George" clonal variety is commonly used. Baco noir was the target of a vine-pull program in Canada in the early 1980s, which means that there are few older plots of this variety left in Canada. Oregon's first Baco Noir vines were imported by Philippe Girardet in 1971 for his winery located in the Umpqua Valley. This variety is also grown in certain parts of Colorado as vineyard area expands beyond the traditional AVA's of Colorado and across the Front Range. See also Baco 22A (Baco blanc) References 2. BC Baco Noir wineries Red wine grape varieties Hybrid grape varieties
simplified_kindergarten:
Baco noir is a red grape. It makes red wine.
A man named Francois Baco made it.
He mixed two other grapes.
One grape was Folle blanche from France.
The other grape grew in North America. We do not know its name.

In 1951, Baco noir grapes came to North America.
They went to cool places.
Like British Columbia, Ontario, Nova Scotia.
And New York, Michigan, Mississippi, Wisconsin, Pennsylvania, Oregon.

In 1955, the grapes went back to Canada.
A special kind called "George" is grown there a lot.

In the 1980s, people in Canada took out many Baco noir plants.
So, there are not many old Baco noir plants left there.

In Oregon, the first Baco noir plants came in 1971.
A man named Philippe Girardet brought them.
He made wine in Umpqua Valley.

These grapes also grow in Colorado.
More places in Colorado are growing them now.
simplified_primary_school:
Baco noir (say it like BA-koh NWAHR) is a red grape. It's a special kind called a hybrid. A man named Francois Baco made it. He mixed two other grapes. One was Folle blanche, a French grape. The other was a wild grape from North America that we don't know the name of.

In 1951, this grape came to cooler places in North America where grapes grow. These places include British Columbia, Ontario, Nova Scotia, New York, Michigan, Mississippi, Wisconsin, Pennsylvania, and Oregon.

In 1955, the grape went back to Canada. There, a type called "George" is often grown.

In the early 1980s, Canada had a program to pull out Baco noir vines. This means there are not many old Baco noir plants left in Canada today.

In 1971, a man named Philippe Girardet brought the first Baco Noir vines to Oregon. He brought them for his place that makes wine in the Umpqua Valley.

This grape also grows in some parts of Colorado. People are planting more grapes there, even in new areas beyond the usual grape-growing spots and across the Front Range mountains.
simplified_secondary_school:
Baco noir (pronounced BA-koh NWAHR) is a hybrid red wine grape. It was created by Francois Baco by crossing Folle blanche, a French wine grape, with an unknown type of Vitis riparia, which is native to North America.

In 1951, Baco noir was introduced to cooler grape-growing regions in North America. These included Canadian provinces like British Columbia, Ontario, and Nova Scotia, and U.S. states such as New York, Michigan, Mississippi, Wisconsin, Pennsylvania, and Oregon. In 1955, the grape was brought back to Canada, where a specific type called the "George" clonal variety is widely grown. However, in the early 1980s, Baco noir was targeted by a "vine-pull program" in Canada. This program removed many vines, so few older Baco noir vineyards remain in the country.

In Oregon, the first Baco noir vines were imported by Philippe Girardet in 1971 for his winery located in the Umpqua Valley. This grape is also cultivated in certain parts of Colorado, as vineyard areas expand beyond the state's traditional grape-growing regions (known as AVAs) and across the Front Range.

original_text: The Battle of Burgos, also known as Battle of Gamonal, was fought on November 10, 1808, during the Peninsular War in the village of Gamonal, near Burgos, Spain. A powerful French army under Marshal Bessières overwhelmed and destroyed the outnumbered Spanish troops under General Belveder, opening central Spain to invasion. Battle The French were attacking the Spanish positions in Burgos with a ratio 2:1. Only two divisions of the Army of Extremadura were in Burgos and the so the result of the battle was clear. Arrayed in an open plain at Gamonal, the Spaniards were broken by a massive cavalry charge and fled in panic, leaving the invaders to sack Burgos. Notes References Battle of Burgos Battles of the Peninsular War Battles in Castile and León Battles involving Spain Battle of Burgos November 1808 events History of the province of Burgos Burgos Battles inscribed on the Arc de Triomphe
simplified_kindergarten:
The Battle of Burgos happened. It was also called the Battle of Gamonal. It was on November 10, 1808. It was during the Peninsular War. It was in Gamonal. Gamonal is a town near Burgos, Spain.

A big French army fought. Marshal Bessières was their leader. They beat the Spanish soldiers. General Belveder led the Spanish. There were not many Spanish soldiers. The French won. Now the French could go into the middle of Spain.

The French attacked the Spanish in Burgos. There were two French soldiers for every one Spanish soldier. Only two groups of Spanish soldiers were in Burgos. They were from the Army of Extremadura. So it was clear the French would win.

The Spanish stood in a big, flat field at Gamonal. French horses and riders ran at them. The Spanish could not fight anymore. They ran away fast. They were scared. The French went into Burgos. They took things from Burgos.
simplified_primary_school: The Battle of Burgos happened on November 10, 1808. It was also called the Battle of Gamonal. It was part of the Peninsular War. The fight was in a village called Gamonal, near Burgos in Spain. A strong French army led by Marshal Bessières fought. They beat the Spanish soldiers, who had fewer people. General Belveder led the Spanish. This win meant the French could go into central Spain. The French army had twice as many soldiers as the Spanish army in Burgos. Only two groups of soldiers from the Army of Extremadura were there. So, it was clear what would happen in the battle. The Spanish soldiers stood on a flat field in Gamonal. French soldiers on horses charged at them very hard. The Spanish soldiers broke apart and ran away in fear. This let the French soldiers take things from Burgos.
simplified_secondary_school: The Battle of Burgos, also known as the Battle of Gamonal, was fought on November 10, 1808, during the Peninsular War in the village of Gamonal, near Burgos, Spain. A powerful French army under Marshal Bessières attacked the Spanish positions. The French outnumbered the Spanish troops under General Belveder by a 2:1 ratio, with only two divisions of the Army of Extremadura present. This significant numerical disadvantage meant the battle's outcome was clear. The French overwhelmed and destroyed the Spanish forces, who were arrayed on an open plain at Gamonal. A massive cavalry charge broke the Spanish lines, causing them to flee in panic. This defeat opened central Spain to invasion and allowed the invaders to sack Burgos.

original_text: an interaction between dark matter and dark energy , proportional to the product of their energy densities , results in a scaling behavior of the ratio of these densities with respect to the scale factor of the robertson - walker metric . this gives rise to a class of cosmological models which deviate from the standard model in an analytically tractable way . in particular , it becomes possible to quantify the role of potential dark - energy perturbations . we investigate the impact of this interaction on the structure formation process . using the ( modified ) camb code we obtain the cmb spectrum as well as the linear matter power spectrum . it is shown that the strong degeneracy in the parameter space present in the background analysis is considerably reduced by considering _ planck _ data . our analysis is compatible with the @xmath0cdm model at the @xmath1 confidence level with a slightly preferred direction of the energy flow from dark matter to dark energy .
simplified_kindergarten:
Dark matter and dark energy talk to each other. How much they talk depends on how much of each there is. This changes how much dark matter there is compared to dark energy as the universe grows.

This helps us make new ideas about the universe. These ideas are a bit different from the old ones. We can study them easily. We can see how much dark energy might wiggle.

We look at how this talk changes how stars and galaxies are made. We use a computer program. It helps us see old light from the universe. It also shows us how matter is spread out.

Before, some things were hard to tell apart. But special information from Planck makes it much clearer. Our study agrees with the usual idea about the universe. It agrees very well. It looks like energy goes a little from dark matter to dark energy.
simplified_primary_school: Dark matter and dark energy interact. How much they interact depends on how much of each is around. This makes the amount of dark matter compared to dark energy change in a special way as the universe gets bigger. This leads to new ideas about how the universe works. These ideas are a bit different from the usual way we think the universe works, but we can still figure them out. We can also measure how important any small changes or bumps in dark energy might be. We looked at how this interaction affects how big things like galaxies and stars were made. Using a special computer program, we got a map of the oldest light in the universe and a map of how matter is spread out. When we first looked at the basic information, it was hard to tell the difference between different ideas. But using information from the Planck satellite made things much clearer. Our study agrees with the usual model of the universe, and we are very sure about this. It also seems a little more likely that energy moves from dark matter to dark energy.
simplified_secondary_school:
An interaction between dark matter and dark energy, where the strength of the interaction is proportional to the product of their energy densities, causes the ratio of these densities to change in a predictable way as the universe expands (measured by the scale factor of the Robertson-Walker metric). This leads to a group of cosmological models that differ from the standard model of cosmology, but in a way that can still be studied and solved mathematically. Specifically, it becomes possible to measure the impact of possible fluctuations in dark energy.

We investigated how this interaction affects the process by which large structures like galaxies and galaxy clusters form. Using a modified version of the CAMB computer code, we calculated the cosmic microwave background (CMB) spectrum and also the linear matter power spectrum. Our results show that the strong uncertainty in the values of the model's parameters, which was present when only looking at the overall expansion of the universe, is greatly lessened by including data from the Planck satellite. Our analysis is compatible with the @xmath0cdm model at the @xmath1 confidence level, with a slight preference for energy flowing from dark matter to dark energy.

original_text: voltage - driven 180@xmath0 magnetization switching without electric current provides the possibility for revolutionizing the spintronics . we demonstrated the voltage - driven charge - mediated 180@xmath0 magnetization switching at room temperature by combining first - principles calculations and temperature - dependent magnetization dynamics simulation . the electric field ( @xmath1 ) induced interface charge is found to allow a giant modulation of the magnetic anisotropy ( @xmath2 ) of the nanomagnet . particularly @xmath2 is revealed to vary linearly with respect to @xmath1 and the epitaxial strain . magnetization dynamics simulations using the so - obtained @xmath2 show that both in - plane and perpendicular 180@xmath0 switching can be achieved by @xmath1 pulses . the temperature effect renders the 180@xmath0 switching as probability events . statistical analysis indicates a fast ( around 2 ns ) and deterministic 180@xmath0 switching achievable at room temperature by controlling the magnitude of e and the pulse width . the study inspires the rational design of miniaturized nanoscale spintronic devices where finite temperature effect has a great impact .
simplified_kindergarten:
A battery can make a tiny magnet flip. No electricity flows. This can make new tiny computers.
We showed how a battery makes the magnet flip. Tiny charges help it. This works when it is not too hot or cold. We used math and computer games to see this.
The battery power makes tiny charges. These charges make the tiny magnet much easier to flip.
How easy it is to flip changes with the battery power. It also changes with how much the magnet is stretched.
Our computer games showed we can flip the magnet in different ways. We use quick pushes of battery power.
How hot or cold it is can make the flip not always happen.
Our math showed the magnet can flip very fast. It will always flip. This works when it is not too hot or cold. We just change how strong the push is and how long it lasts.
This helps us make very tiny, smart computers. How hot or cold it is matters a lot for them.
simplified_primary_school: Using electricity's push to flip tiny magnets 180 degrees without electricity flowing could change how we make special electronic devices called spintronics. We showed that we can flip these tiny magnets 180 degrees using electricity's push and tiny electric bits, even at normal room temperature. We did this by using two types of computer models. The electricity's push makes tiny electric bits appear where things meet. These bits can greatly change how easily the tiny magnet stays in its direction. We found that how easily the magnet stays in its direction changes directly with the electricity's push and how much the material is stretched. Our computer models showed that we can flip the magnet 180 degrees both flat and upright by using short bursts of the electricity's push. How hot or cold it is makes flipping the magnet 180 degrees happen by chance. But by looking at the numbers, we found that we can flip the magnet 180 degrees very quickly (in about 2 nanoseconds) and surely at room temperature. We do this by changing how strong the electricity's push is and how long it lasts. This work helps us think about how to make very tiny spintronic devices, especially where how hot or cold it is really matters.
simplified_secondary_school:
Imagine changing a magnet's direction by 180 degrees using only voltage, not an electric current. This method could completely transform spintronics technology. We showed that this 180-degree magnetization switching, driven by voltage and mediated by electrical charges, is possible at room temperature. We achieved this by combining advanced computer modeling (first-principles calculations) with simulations that account for how magnetism changes with temperature.

We found that an electric field (E) creates charges at the surface of the nanomagnet. These charges can significantly change the nanomagnet's magnetic anisotropy (MA), which is its preferred direction of magnetization. Specifically, the magnetic anisotropy (MA) changes directly in proportion to both the electric field (E) and the epitaxial strain (a type of stress applied during manufacturing).

Using the calculated magnetic anisotropy (MA) in our simulations, we showed that applying short pulses of the electric field (E) can achieve 180-degree switching, whether the magnetization is initially in the plane or perpendicular to it. However, temperature affects this process, making the 180-degree switching occur as a matter of probability rather than certainty. Our statistical analysis revealed that fast (around 2 nanoseconds) and reliable (deterministic) 180-degree switching can be achieved at room temperature by carefully controlling the strength of the electric field (E) and how long it is applied (the pulse width).

This research provides a foundation for designing tiny, nanoscale spintronic devices, especially where the effects of temperature are significant.

original_text: (CNN) -- Former Detroit mayor Kwame Kilpatrick is headed back behind bars, but only for a few days. original_text: In telecommunication, the term conditioning equipment has the following meanings: At junctions of circuits, equipment used to obtain desired circuit characteristics, such as matched transmission levels, matched impedances, and equalization between facilities. Corrective networks used to improve data transmission, such as equalization of the insertion loss-vs.-frequency characteristic and the envelope delay distortion over a desired frequency range. References Communication circuits
simplified_kindergarten:

It means special things.
These things are used where wires meet.
They make the wires work well.
They make sounds not too loud or too soft.

It also means parts that fix things.
These parts make sending information better.

They make sounds arrive on time.
This is for the sounds we want.
simplified_primary_school:
When phones and computers talk to each other, "conditioning equipment" means these things:

It is a machine used where different wires meet. It makes sure the wires work just right. For example, it makes sure the sound or information is at the right loudness. It also makes sure the wires fit together well and that everything is balanced between different parts.

It is also special parts that fix problems when sending information. For example, it makes sure all the different parts of the information arrive at the right strength. It also makes sure they all arrive at the same time. This helps the information get where it needs to go clearly.
simplified_secondary_school: In telecommunication, "conditioning equipment" has two main meanings. First, it refers to equipment used where communication circuits connect. This equipment adjusts circuit properties to achieve desired characteristics, such as matching signal strengths (transmission levels), balancing electrical resistance (impedances), and ensuring different parts of the system are compatible (equalization between facilities). Second, it describes corrective networks designed to improve data transmission. These networks fix problems like uneven signal loss across different frequencies (equalization of the insertion loss-vs.-frequency characteristic) and prevent distortion caused by different frequencies arriving at different times (envelope delay distortion) within a specific frequency range.

original_text: The Great Rebellion or Great Revolt is a term that is generally used in English for the following conflicts: First Jewish–Roman War in 66–73 CE, also known as the Great Revolt of Judaea Peasants' Revolt in England in 1381, also called Wat Tyler's Rebellion English Civil War in 1642–1651, also called English Revolution Rebellion of Túpac Amaru II in 1780–83, against Bourbon reforms in the Spanish Viceroyalty of Peru Wars of the Three Kingdoms, an intertwined series of conflicts that took place in England, Ireland and Scotland between 1639 and 1651, including the English Civil War Indian Rebellion of 1857, against the British East India Company In the northern states of the US, an alternate term used in naming the American Civil War (1861–65) East Timorese rebellion of 1911–12 against Portuguese colonial authorities Arab Revolt or Great Arab Revolt of 1917 (; ), against the Ottoman Empire 1936–1939 Arab revolt in Palestine, that would come to be known as "the Great Revolt". simplified_secondary_school:
The terms "Great Rebellion" or "Great Revolt" are commonly used in English to refer to these conflicts:

*   The First Jewish-Roman War (66–73 CE), also known as the Great Revolt of Judaea.
*   The Peasants' Revolt in England (1381), also called Wat Tyler's Rebellion.

*   The Arab Revolt or Great Arab Revolt (1917), against the Ottoman Empire.
*   The Arab Revolt in Palestine (1936–1939), which later became known as "the Great Revolt."

In fiction, the term also refers to:

*   The war against the Evil Horde in She-Ra: Princess of Power.
*   The Butlerian Jihad, an event in Frank Herbert's fictional Dune universe.

original_text: The class Heterotardigrada includes tardigrades (water bears) that have cephalic appendages and legs with four separate but similar digits or claws on each. 444 species have been described. Anatomy The anatomy of the reproductive system is an important defining feature in distinguishing the different groups of tardigrades. Heterotardigrades have gonoducts that open to the outside through a preanal gonopore, rather than opening into the rectum as in the only other confirmed class of tardigrades, the Eutardigrada. The third class, Mesotardigrada, is represented by a single species known from a single specimen that is now lost, and the location from which that specimen was collected has since been destroyed by an earthquake, so its reproductive anatomy has not been studied recently. Ecology Some orders of heterotardigrades are marine, others are terrestrial, but as for all tardigrades, all are aquatic in the sense that they must be surrounded by at least a film of moisture in order to be active – though they can survive in a dormant state if the habitat dries out. References Protostome classes Taxa named by Ernst Marcus (zoologist)
simplified_kindergarten:
Heterotardigrades are water bears. They have little feelers on their heads. They have legs. Each leg has four tiny claws. We know about 444 kinds of them.

How their bodies make babies is important. It helps us tell different water bears apart. Heterotardigrades have a special tube. It opens to the outside of their body. Other water bears, called Eutardigrades, have a tube that opens inside their bottom.

There was a third kind of water bear. It was called Mesotardigrada. We only found one of them. But that one is lost now. The place where it was found is gone. An earthquake broke it. So we cannot study how it made babies.

Some heterotardigrades live in the sea. Some live on land. But all water bears need water around them to move and play. If it gets dry, they can go to sleep. They wake up when water comes back.
simplified_primary_school:
Heterotardigrades are a type of water bear. They have small parts on their heads. Their legs have four small toes or claws. People have found 444 different kinds of them.

How their bodies make babies is a big way to tell them apart and see which group of water bears they belong to. Heterotardigrades have a special tube that opens outside their body, in front of their bottom. Other water bears, called Eutardigrades, have this tube open inside their bottom.

There was a third group, called Mesotardigrades, but we only know about one kind of them. This was from just one animal that is now gone. The place where that animal was found was broken by an earthquake. So, no one has looked at how it made babies for a long time.

Some heterotardigrades live in the sea, and others live on land. But like all water bears, they need to be wet to move around. They need a little bit of water all around them. But if their home gets dry, they can go to sleep and wait for water.
simplified_secondary_school:
The class Heterotardigrada includes tardigrades, also known as water bears, which have appendages on their heads and legs with four distinct, claw-like digits on each. There are 444 described species in this class.

**Anatomy**
The structure of the reproductive system is a crucial feature for telling different groups of tardigrades apart. Heterotardigrades have reproductive ducts (gonoducts) that open directly to the outside through a preanal gonopore, which is an opening located before the anus. This is different from the Eutardigrada, the only other confirmed class of tardigrades, where these ducts open into the rectum. A third class, Mesotardigrada, is known from only a single species, represented by one specimen that is now lost. The place where this specimen was found was later destroyed by an earthquake, so its reproductive anatomy has not been studied recently.

**Ecology**
Some orders of heterotardigrades live in marine environments, while others are found on land. However, like all tardigrades, they are all considered aquatic because they must be surrounded by at least a thin film of moisture to be active. If their habitat dries out, they can survive in a dormant state.

original_text: (CNN) -- Firefighters in Clinton, North Carolina, discovered six bodies inside what remained of a mobile home that burned early Saturday morning. There was no immediate sign of foul play, the Sampson County Sheriff's Office said, but investigators are processing the location as a potential crime scene until the cause of the blaze is determined and autopsies are completed. The single-wide mobile home caught fire around 1 a.m.. The victims have not yet been identified, the sheriff's office said. The sheriff's office, local fire marshal's office, the North Carolina State Bureau of Investigation and the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) are investigating. Clinton is about 25 miles east of Fayetteville.

original_text: in social networks of human individuals , social relationships do not necessarily last forever as they can either fade gradually with time , resulting in `` link aging '' , or terminate abruptly , causing `` link deletion '' , as even old friendships may cease . in this paper , we study a social network formation model where we introduce several ways by which a link termination takes place . if we adopt the link aging , we get a more modular structure with more homogeneously distributed link weights within communities than when link deletion is used . by investigating distributions and relations of various network characteristics , we find that the empirical findings are better reproduced with the link deletion model . this indicates that link deletion plays a more prominent role in organizing social networks than link aging .
simplified_kindergarten:
People are friends with other people. Friends do not stay friends forever. Sometimes friends slowly stop being friends. This is like a friendship getting old. Or friends can stop being friends very fast. This is like a friendship breaking.

We looked at how friendships end.

If friendships just get old, the groups of friends are more clear. And the friends in each group are all good friends. This is not like when friendships break fast.

We looked at how all the friends were connected. We saw that the fast way friends stop being friends looked more like real life.

This means that friends stopping fast is more important for how friends make groups than friends slowly stopping.
simplified_primary_school:
When people are friends online or in groups, these friendships don't always last forever. Sometimes, friendships slowly get weaker over time. We call this 'link aging'. Other times, friendships suddenly stop. We call this 'link deletion'. Even old friends can stop being friends.

In this paper, we looked at how groups of friends are made. We showed different ways friendships can end.

If friendships slowly get weaker (link aging), the groups of friends look more like separate blocks. The connections inside each block are also more even. This is different from when friendships suddenly stop (link deletion).

We looked at how different parts of the friend groups were spread out and connected. We found that what we saw in real life matched better with the idea of friendships suddenly stopping (link deletion).

This means that friendships suddenly stopping (link deletion) is more important for how groups of friends are set up than friendships slowly getting weaker (link aging).
simplified_secondary_school:
In social networks, the connections between people don't always last. These relationships can end in two main ways: they might gradually weaken over time, a process called "link aging," or they might suddenly stop, which is known as "link deletion." Even long-standing friendships can come to an abrupt end.

This paper explores a model of how social networks are formed, specifically looking at these different ways relationships terminate. We found that if we consider "link aging," the network tends to have a more modular structure, meaning it's more clearly divided into distinct groups, and the strength of connections within those groups is more evenly spread.

However, after examining various network features and comparing them to real-world observations, we discovered that the "link deletion" model better matches what we see in actual social networks. This suggests that relationships ending suddenly (link deletion) plays a more significant role in shaping and organizing social networks than relationships gradually fading (link aging).

simplified_kindergarten:

This rule is for "distance-regular graphs".

Another kind of graph is "bipartite".
It is "distance-biregular" if it is "distance-regular" near every dot.
And a special list of numbers for it only cares about the dot's group.

We made a new "spectral excess theorem".
This new rule is for "bipartite distance-biregular graphs".
simplified_primary_school:
The 'spectral excess theorem' for 'distance-regular graphs' says that a regular graph (where all its parts are linked) is a distance-regular graph exactly when its 'spectral excess' is the same as its 'average excess'.

A 'bipartite graph' is called 'distance-biregular' when it acts like a distance-regular graph if you look from any of its points. Also, a special list of numbers for it (called the 'intersection array') only depends on the 'stable set' that point belongs to.

We are creating a new version of the 'spectral excess theorem'. This new version is for 'bipartite distance-biregular graphs'.
simplified_secondary_school: The spectral excess theorem for distance-regular graphs states a key condition: a regular, connected graph is distance-regular if and only if its spectral excess equals its average excess. A bipartite graph is called distance-biregular when it is distance-regular from the perspective of each vertex, and its intersection array (a structural property) depends only on the stable set to which that vertex belongs. This note presents a new version of the spectral excess theorem, specifically for bipartite distance-biregular graphs.

The spectacular Guggenheim Museum provides a breathtaking backdrop for those brave enough to leap into the water from a platform three times higher than those used at the Olympic Games. While Silchenko won the Bilbao event, Hunt finished second as the Briton secured his fourth world title in five years.

original_text: Arensnuphis (in Egyptian: Iryhemesnefer, ỉrỉ-ḥms-nfr, "the good companion") is a deity from the Kingdom of Kush in ancient Nubia, first attested at Musawwarat el-Sufra in the 3rd century BC. His worship spread to the Egyptian-controlled portion of Nubia in the Ptolemaic Period (305–30 BC). His mythological role is unknown; he was depicted as a lion and as a human with a crown of feathers and sometimes a spear. Arensnuphis was worshipped at Philae, where he was called the "companion" of the Egyptian goddess Isis, as well as at Dendur. In the ancient Egyptian religion, the Egyptians syncretized him with their gods Anhur and Shu. References Nubian gods Egyptian gods
simplified_kindergarten:
Arensnuphis is a god. His name means "the good friend".
He was from a place called Kush. Kush was in old Nubia.
People first knew about him a very long time ago. This was at a place called Musawwarat el-Sufra.

More people started to pray to him. They lived in parts of Nubia that Egypt ruled. This was also a very long time ago.

We do not know his special story. He looked like a lion. He also looked like a person. This person wore a crown of feathers. Sometimes, he held a spear.

People prayed to Arensnuphis at Philae. At Philae, he was called a friend of the god Isis. Isis was from Egypt. People also prayed to him at Dendur.

In old Egypt, people had their own gods. They mixed Arensnuphis with their gods Anhur and Shu.

Later, people started to worship him in parts of Nubia that Egypt ruled. This was during the Ptolemaic Period, from 305 to 30 BC.

We don't know his special stories or what his job was in myths. He was shown as a lion. He was also shown as a person wearing a crown of feathers, sometimes holding a spear. People worshipped Arensnuphis at Philae and Dendur. At Philae, they called him the 'friend' of the Egyptian goddess Isis. In the old Egyptian religion, the Egyptians mixed him with their gods Anhur and Shu.
simplified_secondary_school:
Arensnuphis was a god from the ancient Kingdom of Kush in Nubia. His Egyptian name was Iryhemesnefer, which means "the good companion." The earliest records of him come from Musawwarat el-Sufra in the 3rd century BC.

His worship later spread to the parts of Nubia that Egypt controlled during the Ptolemaic Period (305–30 BC). While his specific role in mythology is unknown, Arensnuphis was depicted in two main ways: as a lion, or as a human wearing a crown of feathers and sometimes holding a spear.

People worshipped Arensnuphis at places like Philae and Dendur. At Philae, he was known as the "companion" of the Egyptian goddess Isis. In the ancient Egyptian religion, Egyptians also combined Arensnuphis with their own gods, Anhur and Shu.

original_text: A former Guantanamo Bay detainee from Britain appeared in a London court Saturday, charged with Syria-related terrorism offenses, UK police said. Moazzam Begg, 45, from Birmingham, is accused of providing instruction and training for terrorism and funding terrorism overseas, West Midlands Police said. A 44-year-old woman, Gerrie Tahari, also from Birmingham, is also accused of funding terrorism overseas. Both suspects, who were arrested Tuesday and charged late Friday, denied the charges at Westminster Magistrates' Court. They were remanded in custody to appear at the Old Bailey criminal court in London on March 14. Two other men, aged 20 and 36, who were also arrested on Tuesday remain in custody, the police said. Begg was arrested as a suspected al Qaeda member in Pakistan in February 2002 and was held at the U.S. airbase at Bagram, Afghanistan, before being sent to the U.S. prison camp at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba. Begg spent nearly three years in custody. He was sent back to Britain by the Bush administration in early 2005 and was never prosecuted. He co-authored a book about his experiences at Guantanamo Bay in 2006 and has appeared as a public speaker and commentator in Britain and overseas since his release.

They must stay in jail. They will go to another court in London. It will be on March 14.

West Midlands Police accuse Mr. Begg, from Birmingham, of providing instruction and training for terrorism, and of funding terrorism overseas. A 44-year-old woman, Gerrie Tahari, also from Birmingham, faces a similar charge of funding terrorism overseas.

Both Mr. Begg and Ms. Tahari were arrested on Tuesday and charged late on Friday. They denied the charges when they appeared at Westminster Magistrates' Court. "Client(s)" or "The Client" can refer to:

*   **In business:**
    *   A person or company that uses the services of another business.
    *   A customer, someone who receives goods or services in exchange for money or other valuable payment.
*   **In computing:** Hardware (like a computer) or software (like an app) that connects to and uses a service provided by another computer over a network.
*   **In ancient Rome:** An individual who was protected and supported by a more powerful person called a patron.
*   **Client (band):** A British synthpop music group.
*   **Client (album):** A 2003 album by the band Client.
*   **Clients (album):** A 2005 album by the band The Red Chord.
*   **"The Client" (novel):** A legal thriller book written by John Grisham in 1993.
*   **"The Client" (1994 film):** A movie based on John Grisham's novel.
*   **"The Client" (TV series):** A television show from 1995–1996, also based on the novel.
*   **"The Client" (Star Wars):** A character in the TV series *The Mandalorian*.
*   **"The Client" (2011 film):** A South Korean courtroom thriller movie.
*   **"The Client" (The Office):** An episode of the TV show *The Office*.
*   **Client state:** A country that is weaker and depends on a more powerful country for economic, political, or military support.
*   The term can also refer to a client in the context of prostitution.

original_text: Year 109 (CIX) was a common year starting on Monday (link will display the full calendar) of the Julian calendar. At the time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Palma and Tullus (or, less frequently, year 862 Ab urbe condita). The denomination 109 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Roman Empire June 24 – The Aqua Traiana is inaugurated by Emperor Trajan; the aqueduct channels water from Lake Bracciano, north-west of Rome. The Via Traiana is constructed at the Emperor Trajan's personal expense; the road connects Benevento with Brundisium (Brindisi). The Baths of Trajan, built by the architect Apollodorus of Damascus, are dedicated during the Calends. The thermae are constructed on the platform of the Palace of Nero (Domus Aurea) in Rome. Osroes I of Parthia succeeds his brother Pacorus II, and rules over the western Parthian Empire. Pliny the Younger is legate to Bithynia. By topic Religion The Christian Church proclaims itself to be universal (catholic). References
simplified_kindergarten:
Year 109. It started on a Monday.
Long ago, people called it the year of Palma and Tullus.
Today, we call it year 109. People in Europe started using this name a long, long time ago.

On June 24, Emperor Trajan opened the Aqua Traiana.
It was a big water pipe. It brought water from Lake Bracciano. Lake Bracciano is near Rome.

Emperor Trajan built the Via Traiana road. He paid for it himself.
This road went from Benevento to Brundisium.

The Baths of Trajan were built. A man named Apollodorus built them. They were opened.
The baths were built on top of Nero's old palace. This palace was in Rome.

Osroes I became king. He took over from his brother Pacorus II.
He ruled the western part of Parthia.

Pliny the Younger was a leader in Bithynia.

The Christian Church said it was for everyone.
simplified_primary_school:
Year 109 was a normal year. It started on a Monday. Back then, people called it the year when Palma and Tullus were important leaders. Sometimes, they also called it year 862. People started calling it year 109 much later, in the olden days. That's when the way we count years now became popular in Europe.

In the Roman Empire, on June 24, Emperor Trajan opened the Aqua Traiana. This was a big water pipe that brought water from Lake Bracciano, which is north-west of Rome. Emperor Trajan also paid for a road called the Via Traiana. This road joined Benevento and Brundisium (Brindisi). The Baths of Trajan were also opened. A builder named Apollodorus of Damascus made them. These baths were built in Rome, on top of where Nero's big palace used to be.

simplified_secondary_school:
Year 109 (CIX) was a common year in the Julian calendar, beginning on a Monday. At the time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Palma and Tullus, or less frequently, as year 862 Ab urbe condita. The numerical designation "109" for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years.

Many significant events occurred during 109 AD. In the Roman Empire, on June 24, Emperor Trajan inaugurated the Aqua Traiana, an aqueduct that channels water from Lake Bracciano, located northwest of Rome. The Via Traiana, a road connecting Benevento with Brundisium (Brindisi), was constructed at Emperor Trajan's personal expense. The Baths of Trajan, built by the architect Apollodorus of Damascus, were dedicated during the Calends. These large public baths (thermae) were constructed in Rome on the platform of Nero's Palace (Domus Aurea). Beyond the Roman Empire, Osroes I of Parthia succeeded his brother Pacorus II, becoming ruler of the western Parthian Empire. Pliny the Younger served as legate to Bithynia. In terms of religion, the Christian Church proclaimed itself to be universal (catholic).

original_text: (CNN) -- The 9-year-old boy who sneaked onto a plane for a trip to Las Vegas is back in his hometown of Minneapolis, a family spokesman said Saturday night. Spokesman V. J. Smith would not provide details. He said he would speak with the boy's father Sunday. Crew members aboard a Delta Air Lines flight October 3 became suspicious of their child passenger and then found that he had no ticket or boarding pass. He was placed in a foster home as authorities began an investigation. The Transportation Security Administration has said the boy went through security at Minneapolis-St. Paul International Airport, although it has not explained how he did so without a boarding pass. An airport spokesman said video shows the boy chatted with a Delta agent when he reached the gate and moved toward the plane when the agent became distracted. Father: 'No one stepped up to help' 'He's got a behavioral problem,' dad says . CNN's Janet DiGiacomo contributed to this report.

On October 3, people working on a Delta Air Lines plane thought something was strange about the boy. They found out he did not have a ticket or a special pass to get on the plane. The boy was put in a special home while grown-ups started to look into what happened.

original_text: Year 289 (CCLXXXIX) was a common year starting on Tuesday (link will display the full calendar) of the Julian calendar. At the time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Bassus and Quintianus (or, less frequently, year 1042 Ab urbe condita). The denomination 289 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Roman Empire In this or the following year, Emperor Diocletian campaigns with success against the Sarmatians. The future emperor Galerius may have distinguished himself during this campaign. In this or the following year, Maximian attempts to reconquer Britain from the usurper Carausius but is defeated at sea. Births Flavia Maxima Fausta, Roman empress (d. 326) Yu Liang (or Yuangui), Chinese politician (d. 340) Deaths Alexander of Rome, Christian martyr Kyriaki the Great, Christian martyr Xun Xu (or Gongzeng), Chinese official References

A man named Galerius may have done very well in this fight. He would be emperor later.

In this year or the next, Maximian tried to get Britain back. Carausius had taken Britain. Maximian lost at sea.

Less often, they referred to it as year 1042 *Ab urbe condita*, meaning "from the founding of the city" of Rome. We call it 486 now because people started using this name a long time ago, in the early Middle Ages. That's when the Anno Domini calendar became the main way to name years in Europe.

Things that happened:
In Europe, there was a big fight called the Battle of Soissons. King Clovis I and his Frankish army won against the Gallo-Roman kingdom of Soissons, which was in Gaul. The Romans, led by Syagrius, stopped ruling there. The land between two rivers, the Somme and the Loire, became part of Clovis's Frankish Empire. Syagrius ran away to the Visigoths, who were led by King Alaric II. But King Clovis said he would start a war if they didn't give Syagrius back. So, Syagrius was given to Clovis and was killed. King Clovis I made Soissons his new home. He chose Ragnachar, who was a small Frankish king, to help him rule.

About religion:
Christians from Persia who believed in something called Nestorianism met together in a meeting called the second Council of Seleucia. This place is in modern-day Turkey.

Syagrius, a Roman leader in Gaul. We don't know the exact day he died.

The year 486 was a common year in the Julian calendar. At that time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Basilius and Longinus, or sometimes as year 1239 Ab urbe condita (meaning "from the founding of the city" of Rome). The number 486 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar system became the main way to name years in Europe.

**Events**

**Europe**
The Battle of Soissons took place, where Frankish forces led by King Clovis I defeated the Gallo-Roman kingdom of Soissons in Gaul. This battle marked the end of Roman rule under Syagrius in the region. As a result, the land between the Somme and the Loire rivers became part of the Frankish Empire. Syagrius fled to the Visigoths, who were led by King Alaric II. However, Clovis threatened war, which led to Syagrius being handed over and executed. King Clovis I then established his new residence in Soissons and appointed Ragnachar, a Frankish petty king (known as a regulus), as his deputy ruler.

**Religion**
Persian Christians who followed Nestorianism gathered for the second Council of Seleucia, held in what is now modern Turkey.

**Deaths**
Syagrius, a Roman official in Gaul, died around this approximate date.

original_text: UP Health System - Marquette (formerly Marquette General Hospital) is an American hospital and regional medical center located in Marquette and serving the Upper Peninsula of Michigan. The hospital is a Level II trauma center as verified by the American College of Surgeons. The hospital trains medical students from the Michigan State University College of Human Medicine, as well as residents in the family medicine residency program. Prior to 1973, Marquette had two hospitals: St. Mary's Hospital and St. Luke's Hospital. Marquette General Hospital was formed in 1973 when the two Marquette hospitals merged. In 2012, Michigan Attorney General Bill Schuette approved Marquette General Hospitals purchase by for-profit organization Duke LifePoint. It was the fourth hospital to be acquired by the company, after Maria Parham Medical Center in Henderson, North Carolina, Person Memorial Hospital in Roxboro, North Carolina, and Twin County Regional Healthcare in Galax, Virginia. original_text: Wondul Range is a national park in Queensland, Australia, 209 km west of Brisbane. It is located in the Toowoomba Region local government area of the Darling Downs. The park aims to conserve open woodland species typical of the Western Downs. It lies within the water catchment area of the Macintyre River and Weir Rivers in the Brigalow Belt South bioregion. Whetstone State Forest lies to the south and Bulli State Forest to the west, both adjacent to the national park. A total of five rare or threatened species have been identified within Wondul Range National Park. The park has no facilities. The main recreational activities undertaken in the park are bird watching and wildlife observation. original_text: Deoxycytidine is a deoxyribonucleoside, a component of deoxyribonucleic acid. It is similar to the ribonucleoside cytidine, but with one hydroxyl group removed from the C2' position. Deoxycytidine can be phosphorylated at C5' of the deoxyribose by deoxycytidine kinase, converting it to deoxycytidine monophosphate (dCMP), a DNA precursor. dCMP can be converted to dUMP and dTMP. It can also be used as a precursor for 5-aza-2′-deoxycytidine, a treatment for MDS patients. This compound slows the cell cycle by interfering with the methylation of the P15/INK4B gene, increasing the expression of P15/INK4B protein which subdues the transformation of MDS to leukemia. dCMP can change again. It can become dUMP and dTMP.
It can also help make a medicine. The medicine is called 5-aza-2′-deoxycytidine. This medicine helps sick people. They have MDS.
This medicine makes cells grow slower. It stops something bad from happening to a part inside the cell. This part is called P15/INK4B gene. It makes more P15/INK4B protein. This protein helps stop MDS from becoming worse. It stops it from becoming leukemia.

Deoxycytidine can also help make a medicine called 5-aza-2′-deoxycytidine. This medicine helps people who have a sickness called MDS. This medicine makes cells grow slower. It does this by stopping a special chemical process on a part of our body's instructions called the P15/INK4B gene. This makes more of a helper called P15/INK4B protein. This helper stops MDS from turning into a more serious sickness called leukemia.
simplified_secondary_school: Deoxycytidine is a type of deoxyribonucleoside, which is a building block of deoxyribonucleic acid. It is similar to cytidine (a ribonucleoside), but it's missing one hydroxyl group at the C2' position. An enzyme called deoxycytidine kinase can add a phosphate group to deoxycytidine at the C5' position of its deoxyribose sugar. This changes deoxycytidine into deoxycytidine monophosphate (dCMP), which is a molecule needed to make DNA. dCMP can then be changed into other molecules called dUMP and dTMP. Deoxycytidine can also be used to create 5-aza-2′-deoxycytidine, a medication used to treat patients with MDS. This medication works by slowing down the cell cycle. It does this by interfering with a process called methylation on the P15/INK4B gene. This interference leads to more P15/INK4B protein being produced, which helps to stop MDS from developing into leukemia.

original_text: we provide a quantitative determination of the crystallization onset for two electrons in a parabolic two - dimensional confinement . this system is shown to be well described by a roto - vibrational model , wigner crystallization occurring when the rotational motion gets decoupled from the vibrational one . the wigner molecule thus formed is characterized by its moment of inertia and by the corresponding sequence of rotational excited states . the role of a vertical magnetic field is also considered . additional support to the analysis is given by the hartree - fock phase diagram for the ground state and by the random - phase approximation for the moment of inertia and vibron excitations .

simplified_primary_school:
We find out exactly when two tiny particles, called electrons, start to form a crystal. They are held in a special flat trap. We can understand how these electrons act by thinking about them spinning and shaking. They form a crystal when their spinning stops being connected to their shaking.

The crystal shape they make can be described by how hard it is to make it spin. We also look at the different ways it can spin faster. We also think about what happens if we add a magnet pointing up or down.

Other ways of looking at this help us understand it. We use a special map to see how the electrons act when they are calm. We also use another special way to guess how hard it is to make them spin and shake.
simplified_secondary_school: We precisely determine when two electrons begin to crystallize while confined in a two-dimensional parabolic trap. This system is well explained by a roto-vibrational model, where Wigner crystallization happens when the electrons' spinning motion separates from their jiggling motion. The Wigner molecule that forms is described by its moment of inertia (a measure of its resistance to changes in rotation) and by the different energy levels it can have when it rotates. We also investigate the effect of a vertical magnetic field. Further support for our analysis comes from the Hartree-Fock phase diagram, which maps out the stable arrangements of the electrons at their lowest energy state, and from the random-phase approximation, which helps confirm the moment of inertia and the vibrational energy levels (vibron excitations).

original_text: (CNN) -- A Northern California woman was arrested on suspicion of taking a bite out of two kids in the children's section of a bookstore, police said. Polly Beltramo, 46, of Palo Alto, is accused of biting a 2-year-old girl and a baby boy on the cheek at BookBuyers in Mountain View on Thursday, police said. The incident began when Beltramo allegedly bit the girl, whose mother was in the children's section of the store, witnesses said. Bookstore owner Rammurti Reed said he heard the girl screaming and saw a red welt on her cheek. "Suddenly a little boy, who was just a couple of months old, started screaming," Reed told CNN affiliate KGO-TV. "I went to him and his cheek was bleeding." Beltramo allegedly fled the store and was later found at a nearby coffee shop, police said. Beltramo has been arrested on felony child abuse, police said.

Building on the work of S. Y. Cheng and S.-T. Yau, we found something about a special kind of curved space. This space is called a 'strictly pseudoconvex domain' (let's call it D), and it lives inside a 'complex manifold' (a complex space). We showed that D has a special way to measure distances and curves, called a 'complete Khler-Einstein metric'. This happens only if a special property of D, called its 'canonical bundle', is 'positive'. Being 'positive' means it has a special way to connect points that curve in a positive way.

We looked at a simpler situation. This was when the 'CR structure' (a special way the space is built) on the boundary (let's call it M) was 'normal'. In this simpler situation, D must be a special kind of area. It sits inside a 'resolution' (a way to smooth out rough spots) of a 'Sasaki cone' (another special shape) that sits over M.

We found a rule for a 'normal CR manifold' (a special kind of boundary space). This rule cannot be met if that space is the 'CR infinity' (the edge far away) of a 'Khler-Einstein manifold' (a space with the special distance measuring). We could mostly figure out which 'normal CR 3-manifolds' (special boundary spaces with 3 dimensions) can be 'CR infinities'.

We showed many examples of spaces that have both the 'Khler-Einstein metric' and are 'strictly pseudoconvex'. These examples were found on 'bundles' (spaces made of smaller spaces stacked up) and 'resolutions'. Especially, a special area around the 'zero section' (a central line or surface) of every 'negative holomorphic vector bundle' (a special kind of mathematical structure) that sits on a 'compact complex manifold' (a fancy, closed-off complex space) has a 'complete Khler-Einstein metric'. This is true if the whole space (called the 'total space') meets a special rule.
simplified_secondary_school: Extending the findings of S. Y. Cheng and S.-T. Yau, it is demonstrated that a strictly pseudoconvex domain, denoted @xmath0, within a complex manifold possesses a complete Kähler-Einstein metric if and only if its canonical bundle is positive. This means the canonical bundle has an Hermitian connection with positive curvature. We then focus on a specific situation where the CR structure on @xmath1 is normal. In this specific scenario, @xmath2 must be a domain found within a resolution of the Sasaki cone over @xmath1. We also present a condition that a normal CR manifold cannot satisfy if it serves as a CR infinity of a Kähler-Einstein manifold. We have largely identified which normal CR 3-manifolds can function as CR infinities. We provide numerous examples of Kähler-Einstein strictly pseudoconvex manifolds, specifically those found on bundles and resolutions. Specifically, a complete Kähler-Einstein metric is present in the tubular neighborhood of the zero section for every negative holomorphic vector bundle on a compact complex manifold, provided its total space meets the condition @xmath3.

We think about a change. It is about the time before the Big Bang.
We use ideas to fix big problems in space. These ideas come from a special way to look at space.
We found that special changes can fix a problem. This problem is called the 'graceful exit problem'.
These changes are about how things move. They come from seeing space like tiny chains.
The bends in space do not get too big. A special energy also does not get too big.
This is true all the time. It is true in two ways we look at space.
One way is called 'string frame'. The other is called 'Einstein frame'.

simplified_primary_school:
We are looking at a new way to think about the time before the Big Bang. We use ideas from a special science called loop quantum cosmology. This science helps fix problems where space might break.

We found that important changes from these "loop" ideas can fix a problem called the "graceful exit problem." These changes come from how we understand things at a very tiny level.

This means that how much space bends and a special energy called dilaton energy always stay normal. They don't get too big or break. This is true no matter if we look at the universe in the "string frame" or the "Einstein frame."

When we look at it in the "string frame" way, the dilaton energy becomes steady a long time after the universe "bounces" back.
simplified_secondary_school: This study examines a realistic adjustment to the pre-Big Bang scenario, drawing on ideas from how loop quantum cosmology resolves issues of infinite curvature. We show that deep changes to the universe's dynamics, which arise from the fundamental 'polymer' representation in loop quantum cosmology, can solve the 'graceful exit problem.' Importantly, both the curvature of spacetime and the dilaton energy always remain finite (never infinite), whether described in the 'string frame' or the 'Einstein frame.' In the 'string frame,' specifically, the dilaton approaches a steady, unchanging value long after the universe 'bounces' back from its smallest point.

original_text: Year 269 BC was a year of the pre-Julian Roman calendar. At the time it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Gallus and Pictor (or, less frequently, year 485 Ab urbe condita). The denomination 269 BC for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Sicily The Mamertines, a body of Campanian mercenaries who have been employed by Agathocles, the former tyrant of Syracuse, capture the stronghold of Messana (Messina in north-eastern Sicily), from which they harass the Syracusans. The Syracusan military leader, Hieron, defeats them in a pitched battle at the Longanus River near Mylae, but Carthaginian forces intervene to prevent him from capturing Messana. His grateful countrymen then choose Hieron as their king and tyrant, to be known as Hieron II. Births Attalus I Soter, ruler of Pergamon, from 241 to 197 BC. He will be the first of the Attalid dynasty to assume the title of king (d. 197 BC). Deaths References

Some men were called Mamertines. They were soldiers. A bad leader named Agathocles hired them. They took a strong town called Messana. It is in Sicily. From there, they bothered the people of Syracuse.

A leader from Syracuse was named Hieron. He fought the Mamertines. Hieron won the fight. It was by the Longanus River. But other soldiers came. They were from Carthage. They stopped Hieron from taking Messana.

His people were happy. They made Hieron their king. He was also a strong ruler. They called him Hieron II.

A boy was born. His name was Attalus I Soter. He would rule a place called Pergamon. He ruled from 241 BC to 197 BC. He was the first in his family to be called king. He died in 197 BC.

Sometimes, they called it year 485 from when the city of Rome was made. We started calling it 269 BC much later, when people in Europe began using the calendar we mostly use today.

In Sicily, a group of soldiers called the Mamertines took over a strong town named Messana. These soldiers used to work for Agathocles, who was a ruler in Syracuse. From Messana, the Mamertines bothered the people of Syracuse.

A leader from Syracuse named Hieron fought the Mamertines. He won a big battle against them near the Longanus River. But soldiers from Carthage stepped in and stopped Hieron from taking Messana. Because Hieron was so brave, the people of Syracuse chose him to be their king. He was then known as Hieron II.

A boy named Attalus I Soter was born. He would later rule a place called Pergamon from 241 to 197 BC. He was the first person in his family to be called a king. He died in 197 BC.
simplified_secondary_school:
The year 269 BC was recorded using the early Roman calendar. At that time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Gallus and Pictor, or sometimes as year 485 Ab urbe condita (from the founding of the city of Rome). The name "269 BC" for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar became the main way to name years in Europe.

In Sicily, a group of Campanian mercenaries called the Mamertines, who had previously worked for Agathocles, the former tyrant of Syracuse, captured the strong city of Messana (now called Messina) in northeastern Sicily. From Messana, they frequently attacked the people of Syracuse.

Hieron, a military leader from Syracuse, defeated the Mamertines in a major battle at the Longanus River, near Mylae. However, Carthaginian forces intervened, stopping Hieron from capturing Messana. Because of his victory, Hieron's grateful people then chose him to be their king and tyrant, and he became known as Hieron II.

Attalus I Soter, who would later rule Pergamon from 241 to 197 BC, was born this year. He was the first ruler of the Attalid family to take the title of king, and he died in 197 BC.

The Army said Altaie was 41 years old. He was a translator for the American Army. He was visiting his family when he was taken.

At the time of his abduction, Altaie was 41 years old and working as a translator for the U.S. military. He was visiting family members when he was taken. original_text: Hassell National Park is a national park in the Great Southern region of Western Australia, southeast of Perth and north east of Albany. Named after John Hassell, a retired sea captain, who was responsible for opening up extensive areas in the south of the state during the 1850s as he extended his pastoral leases east from Frankland as far as Jerramungup. The park straddles the South Coast Highway between Manypeaks and Wellstead. The site is regarded as an important ecological area as a result of it being a largely intact vegetation and wildlife corridor that serves to link the Mount Manypeaks and Waychinicup areas with the Cheynes Baech and Pallinup areas. The park has no entry fee and has no facilities provided for visitors. A population of the very rare Brown's Banksia, composed of 100 to 200 plants, can be found within the confines of the park. See also Protected areas of Western Australia References National parks of Western Australia Great Southern (Western Australia) Protected areas established in 1971 1971 establishments in Australia

The park is on a road. The road is called South Coast Highway. It is between Manypeaks and Wellstead.

This park is very important. It has many plants and animals. It is like a path for them. It connects different places. It connects Mount Manypeaks and Waychinicup. It connects Cheynes Beach and Pallinup.

You do not pay to go into the park. There are no things for visitors.

A special plant lives in the park. It is called Brown's Banksia. It is very rare. There are 100 to 200 of these plants.

original_text: Nederlek () is a former municipality in the western Netherlands, in the province of South Holland. Since 2015 it has been a part of the municipality of Krimpenerwaard. The former municipality covered an area of of which was covered by water. It was formed on 1 January 1985, by the amalgamation of the municipalities Krimpen aan de Lek and Lekkerkerk. Its name means "Lower Lek", a reference to its location on the Lek River. There is no town called Nederlek; the former municipality consisted of the population centres Krimpen aan de Lek and Lekkerkerk. The last town gained notoriety in 1980 when a large amount of toxic waste was found underneath a residential area built in the 1970s. The cost of cleaning up the mess ran up to 188 million guilders (approximately 90 million US dollars). original_text: In type theory and programming languages, a type variable is a mathematical variable ranging over types. Even in programming languages that allow mutable variables, a type variable remains an abstraction, in the sense that it does not correspond to some memory locations. Programming languages that support parametric polymorphism make use of universally quantified type variables. Languages that support existential types make use of existentially quantified type variables. For example, the following OCaml code defines a polymorphic identity function that has a universally quantified type, which is printed by the interpreter on the second line: # let id x = x;; val id : 'a -> 'a = <fun> In mathematical notation, the type of the function id is , where is a type variable. See also System F Type theory Functional programming Dependently typed programming
simplified_kindergarten:
A type variable is a special name. It can stand for any kind of thing.

Other names in computers can change. But a type variable is just an idea. It does not live inside the computer.

Some computer programs use type variables. These variables mean "for all kinds of things."
Other computer programs use type variables. These variables mean "for some kind of thing."

Look at this. A computer program makes a helper. This helper works for all kinds of things. The computer shows its type. It says 'a' goes to 'a'. Here, 'a' is a type variable.

In math, the helper's type is 'a' arrow 'a'. The 'a' is a type variable.
simplified_primary_school:
In computer programming and its rules, a 'type variable' is like a special letter that can stand for different kinds of types. Even in computer codes where some letters can change their value, a type variable is still just an idea. It doesn't have a real spot in the computer's memory.

Some computer codes that can work with many different types in a general way use type variables that can stand for *any* type. Other computer codes that have types that are hidden use type variables that stand for *some* type, even if we don't know exactly what it is.

For example, here is some OCaml code. It makes a special helper called 'id' that works for any type and just gives you back what you put in. The computer shows its type on the next line:
# let id x = x;;
val id : 'a -> 'a = <fun>

In math writing, the type of the 'id' helper is written like this: `∀α. α → α`. Here, `α` is a type variable.

You can also look at: System F, Type theory, Functional programming, Dependently typed programming.
simplified_secondary_school:
In type theory and programming languages, a type variable is a placeholder for a type. Unlike regular variables that hold values in memory, a type variable is an abstract concept; it doesn't refer to a specific memory location.

Programming languages that support *parametric polymorphism* use type variables that are *universally quantified*. This means the code works "for all types," allowing you to write functions that can take any type of input and return the same type of output. Languages that support *existential types* use type variables that are *existentially quantified*. This means the code works "for some specific type," often when that type is not known or is hidden from the user.

For example, in the OCaml programming language, you can define a simple *identity function* called `id` that just returns whatever input it receives:
`# let id x = x;;`
The computer then shows its type:
`val id : 'a -> 'a = <fun>`
Here, `'a` is a type variable. It means the `id` function takes an input of *any* type (represented by `'a`) and returns an output of the *same* type (`'a`). So, if you give it a number, it returns a number; if you give it text, it returns text. In a more formal way, the type of the `id` function could be described as "for all types 'a, 'a goes to 'a," where 'a is the type variable.

original_text: the @xmath0 liouville theory has received some attention recently as the euclidean version of an exact rolling tachyon background . in an earlier paper it was shown that the bulk theory can be identified with the interacting @xmath0 limit of unitary minimal models . here we extend the analysis of the @xmath0-limit to the boundary problem . most importantly , we show that the fzzt branes of liouville theory give rise to a new 1-parameter family of boundary theories at @xmath0 . these models share many features with the boundary sine - gordon theory , in particular they possess an open string spectrum with band - gaps of finite width . we propose explicit formulas for the boundary 2-point function and for the bulk - boundary operator product expansion in the @xmath0 boundary liouville model . as a by - product of our analysis we also provide a nice geometric interpretation for zz branes and their relation with fzzt branes in the @xmath0 theory .

Before, we saw that this theory is like a special part of unitary minimal models.

Now we look at this special part. We look at its edges.

This is important. We found that FZzt branes in Liouville theory make new boundary theories. There are many of these new theories.

These new theories are like boundary sine-gordon theory. They have an open string spectrum. This spectrum has band-gaps. The band-gaps are not too wide.

We wrote math rules for the boundary 2-point function. We also wrote rules for the bulk-boundary operator product expansion. These rules are for the boundary Liouville model.

We also found something else. We showed what ZZ branes look like. We showed how they connect to FZzt branes. This is in this theory.
