In an older paper, we found that the main part of this idea is like a special version of other ideas called 'unitary minimal models'. Now, we are looking at this special idea more closely, especially what happens at its edges.

The most important thing we found is that special parts of the Liouville theory, called 'FZzt branes', make a new group of ideas for the edges. These new ideas can change in one special way.

These new ideas are like another idea called 'boundary sine-gordon theory'. They have special energy levels, like a ladder with some missing steps that are not too big.

We also suggest clear math rules for how things work at the edges. These rules are for things like the 'boundary 2-point function' and the 'bulk-boundary operator product expansion' in the @xmath0 boundary Liouville model.

While we were figuring things out, we also found a clear picture of what 'ZZ branes' look like and how they are connected to 'FZzt branes' in the @xmath0 theory.
simplified_secondary_school: The @xmath0 Liouville theory has recently received attention as the Euclidean version of an exact rolling tachyon background. An earlier paper showed that the bulk theory can be identified with the interacting @xmath0 limit of unitary minimal models. In this work, we extend the analysis of the @xmath0-limit to the boundary problem. Most importantly, we demonstrate that the FZzt branes of Liouville theory give rise to a new 1-parameter family of boundary theories at @xmath0. These models share many features with the boundary sine-Gordon theory, particularly possessing an open string spectrum with band-gaps of finite width. We propose explicit formulas for the boundary 2-point function and for the bulk-boundary operator product expansion in the @xmath0 boundary Liouville model. As a by-product of our analysis, we also provide a geometric interpretation for ZZ branes and their relation with FZzt branes in the @xmath0 theory.

original_text: (CNN) -- A 30-year-old dolphin at Sea World has died after colliding with another dolphin while performing aerial tricks, the Orlando, Florida, amusement park said Monday. The incident occurred about 4:30 p.m. Saturday at the Discovery Cove area of the park, according to a statement by Sea World spokeswoman Becca Bides. The animals were in the center of the lagoon and not near guests, she said. The dolphin who died was named Sharky. The other dolphin, Tyler, is being watched by veterinarians but appears to be fine, Bides said. "This is an unfortunate, random incident," Bides said in a written statement. "While it is not unusual to have two animals performing aerial behaviors at the same time, we are reviewing the situation to ensure even such a random incident does not occur again." E-mail to a friend .

original_text: TAIPEI, Taiwan (CNN) -- More than 90 percent of eligible Taiwan residents took up the government's offer of 3600 New Taiwan Dollars (USD $108) to go shopping, government officials said Monday. People line up to receive $108 U.S. dollars worth of shopping vouchers in Taipei, Taiwan, on Sunday. Any of the island's 23 million men, women or children qualify for the money, a combination of a lunar New Year gift and economic stimulus package designed to pump about 86 billion ($2.6 billion U.S.) into the economy. By Sunday night, 91.3 percent of eligible citizens had picked up their vouchers, and malls were flooded with shoppers, Interior Minister Liao Liou-yi said. The vouchers are good through September. But one major retailer, the Carrefour chain, said its Sunday receipts were up about 30 percent from normal -- and with Chinese New Year just around the corner, many appeared ready to use the money for holiday groceries. Even President Ma Ying-jeou picked up vouchers for all four members of his family, encouraging people to spread the use of the vouchers over days, Taiwan's Central News Agency reported. Journalist Jonathan Lai contributed to this report.

By Sunday night, many people got their tickets. Stores were full of people. A man named Liao Liou-yi said this. He works for the government.

People can use these tickets until September.

A big store called Carrefour sold much more on Sunday. The Chinese New Year is coming soon. Many people will buy food for the holiday.

Even the President, Ma Ying-jeou, got tickets for his family. He told people to use the tickets over many days. A news group in Taiwan said this.

All 23 million people living on the island, including men, women, and children, were eligible for the money. This initiative served two purposes: it was a gift for the Lunar New Year and an economic stimulus package. The government hoped it would inject about 86 billion New Taiwan Dollars (around $2.6 billion US dollars) into the economy.

By Sunday night, 91.3% of eligible citizens had collected their vouchers, leading to crowded malls, according to Interior Minister Liao Liou-yi. People lined up in places like Taipei on Sunday to receive these vouchers.

These vouchers can be used until September. One large retailer, the Carrefour chain, reported that its sales on Sunday were about 30% higher than usual. With Chinese New Year approaching, many people seemed ready to spend their vouchers on groceries for the holiday.

Even President Ma Ying-jeou collected vouchers for his family of four. He encouraged people to use the vouchers over several days, as reported by Taiwan's Central News Agency.

original_text: (CNN) -- At least 15 people were killed and 45 others wounded in a bombing and gunfire near Iraq's central bank in the capital on Sunday, according to the Interior Ministry. After the initial blast, clashes broke out between security forces and armed men trying to rob the bank, the official said. There were conflicting reports about what the blast was, which hit at around 3 p.m. (8 a.m. ET). One Interior Ministry official said at least three roadside bombs exploded outside the bank, while another official said it was a suicide bomber. The spokesman for the Baghdad military command, Maj. Gen. Qassi, al-Moussawi, told state television that it was one roadside bomb. The casualties figures offered by the Interior Ministry only include civilians and security forces. The power generator for the bank caught fire and the burning fuel caused a large black plume of smoke seen over the capital, the first Interior Ministry official said.The bank is located in central Baghdad's Rasheed street. The bombing comes amid political uncertainty, a day before Iraq's parliament is set to convene for it's first session three months after national elections. CNN's Jomana Karadsheh contributed to this report.

People did not agree about the bomb. It happened at 3 in the afternoon.

One person said three bombs blew up on the road near the bank. Another person said a bad man blew himself up. A general named Qassi said it was one bomb on the road.

The numbers of dead and hurt people are for regular people and police.

The bank's power box caught fire. Fire made big black smoke. People saw the smoke over the big city.

The bank is on Rasheed street in the big city.

The country had problems with its leaders. The next day, leaders were to meet. They had picked new leaders three months ago.

Another official said a person blew themselves up with a bomb. A general from the army, named Qassi al-Moussawi, told the TV that it was just one bomb on the road. The people who died or got hurt were regular people and security forces.

A machine that makes power for the bank caught fire. The burning fuel made a big black cloud of smoke that could be seen over the city. The bank is on Rasheed street, in the middle of Baghdad.

This bombing happened when the country's leaders were not sure what would happen next. It was one day before Iraq's parliament was supposed to meet for the first time. This meeting was three months after people voted for new leaders. A reporter named Jomana Karadsheh helped with this news story.

According to the Interior Ministry, after an initial explosion, security forces clashed with armed men who were attempting to rob the bank.

There were conflicting reports about what caused the blast, which occurred around 3 p.m. local time. One Interior Ministry official stated that at least three roadside bombs exploded outside the bank, while another official suggested it was a suicide bomber. Major General Qassi al-Moussawi, spokesman for the Baghdad military command, told state television that only one roadside bomb was involved.

The casualty figures provided by the Interior Ministry included both civilians and security forces. The bank's power generator caught fire, and the burning fuel created a large plume of black smoke visible across the capital.

The bank is located on Rasheed Street in central Baghdad. This attack happened during a period of political uncertainty, just one day before Iraq's parliament was scheduled to hold its first session, three months after national elections.

original_text: In Finnish mythology and lore, a menninkäinen is believed to be a leprechaun-like inhabitant of the forests. Fairy tale depictions often involve riddling, dominance struggles and favors elicited. Menninkäinen were probably originally thought to be spirits of dead people, but folklore about them has changed during time, and they turned to be something else. In modern usage, the word is usually used to mean goblins, hobgoblins and gnomes. Not all Finnish words for the little folk have an English equivalent, and vice versa, so confusion in the translation of these terms is quite common. Cultural References Menninkäinen are mentioned in the Milo Murphy's Law episode "Star Struck", in which Tobias Trollhammer believes Milo Murphy to be a menninkäinen. tobias also states that menninkainen eat your souls. in that same episode, milo mentions the children's show called "milly and the menninkäinen" that he supposedly watched as a child. melissa however states that she did not watch this show and does not know what a menninkäinen is. this show is seen in the "Phineas and Ferb the Movie: Candace Against the Universe" movie, via a background tv screen. Finnish mythology Finnish legendary creatures Leprechaun

In old stories, they like riddles. They like to be the boss. They ask for help.
Long ago, people thought menninkäinen were ghosts. But stories changed. Now they are different.
Today, the word means goblins. It also means hobgoblins and gnomes.
Some Finnish words for little folk do not have an English word. Some English words do not have a Finnish word. So it can be hard to know what they mean.
Menninkäinen are in a TV show. It is called Milo Murphy's Law. A boy named Tobias thinks Milo is a menninkäinen.
Tobias also says menninkäinen eat souls.
In that show, Milo talks about another show. It is for kids. It is called 'Milly and the Menninkäinen'. He says he watched it when he was little.
But Melissa says she did not watch it. She does not know what a menninkäinen is.
You can see this show in a movie. The movie is called 'Phineas and Ferb the Movie: Candace Against the Universe'. It is on a TV screen in the background.
simplified_primary_school:
In old Finnish stories, people thought a menninkäinen was like a tiny forest person, a bit like a leprechaun. In fairy tales, menninkäinen often ask riddles, try to be in charge, and ask for help. At first, people might have thought menninkäinen were the ghosts of people who had died. But over time, the stories changed, and they became different kinds of creatures. Today, the word menninkäinen often means creatures like goblins, hobgoblins, or gnomes. It's hard to find exact English words for all the Finnish names for these little folk, and it's also hard to find Finnish words for all the English ones. So, people often get confused when they try to translate these names.

Menninkäinen are in a TV show called "Milo Murphy's Law." In one episode, "Star Struck," a character named Tobias Trollhammer thinks Milo Murphy is a menninkäinen. Tobias also says that menninkäinen eat people's souls. In the same show, Milo talks about a children's TV show called "Milly and the Menninkäinen." He says he watched it when he was a child. But another character, Melissa, says she never watched that show and doesn't know what a menninkäinen is. You can also see this show on a TV screen in the background of the movie "Phineas and Ferb the Movie: Candace Against the Universe."
simplified_secondary_school:
In Finnish mythology and folklore, a menninkäinen is believed to be a leprechaun-like creature that lives in forests. Fairy tales often show them posing riddles, engaging in power struggles, and asking for favors. Menninkäinen were probably first thought to be spirits of dead people, but over time, the folklore about them changed, and they became something different. In modern use, the word usually refers to goblins, hobgoblins, and gnomes. Not all Finnish words for these "little folk" have an exact English equivalent, and vice versa, so confusion when translating these terms is quite common.

Menninkäinen are mentioned in popular culture. In the *Milo Murphy's Law* episode "Star Struck," Tobias Trollhammer believes Milo Murphy is a menninkäinen and states that menninkäinen eat souls. In that same episode, Milo mentions a children's show he supposedly watched as a child called "Milly and the Menninkäinen." However, Melissa says she did not watch this show and does not know what a menninkäinen is. This "Milly and the Menninkäinen" show is also seen on a background TV screen in *Phineas and Ferb the Movie: Candace Against the Universe*.

original_text: The Blue Hawaiian or Swimming Pool is a Piña Colada with an added splash of blue curaçao to give it an orange flavor. It is made with light rum (e.g. Pineapple Malibu rum or white rum), blue curaçao, pineapple juice, cream of coconut, and lemon juice. It is garnished with a pineapple wedge, maraschino cherry and cocktail umbrella and served in a hurricane glass with pebble ice, or in a Collins glass. A frozen Blue Hawaiian can be made by putting all the ingredients in a blender with some crushed ice and pulsing them to a creamy consistency. The use of light rum allows the blue color from the curaçao to come through. Adding more pineapple juice results in a greenish blue color. Champagne or prosecco can be added to make a sparkling Blue Hawaiian. Difford's Guide notes, "Probably created by Don the Beachcomber in Los Angeles, USA, this is a riff on the Blue Hawaii." References Fruity cocktails Creamy cocktails Sweet cocktails Cocktails with rum Tiki drinks Cocktails with coconut Cocktails with lemon juice Cocktails with pineapple juice
simplified_kindergarten:
This drink is called Blue Hawaiian. You can also call it Swimming Pool. It is like a Piña Colada drink. It has blue curaçao. This blue curaçao tastes like orange.

You make it with light rum. You can use Pineapple Malibu rum or white rum. It also has blue curaçao. And pineapple juice. And cream of coconut. And lemon juice.

It has a pineapple piece on top. It has a red cherry on top. It has a tiny umbrella on top. You drink it from a tall glass. This glass is called a hurricane glass. It has tiny ice. Or you can drink it from a Collins glass.

You can make it frozen. Put all the things in a blender. Add crushed ice. Blend it until it is smooth and creamy.

Light rum helps the drink stay blue. The blue color comes from the curaçao.

If you add more pineapple juice, it will look green-blue.

You can add champagne. Or you can add prosecco. This makes the drink bubbly.

Some people say a man made it. His name was Don. He made it in Los Angeles, USA. It is like another drink called Blue Hawaii.

It is a fruity drink. It is a creamy drink. It is a sweet drink. It has rum. It is a Tiki drink. It has coconut. It has lemon juice. It has pineapple juice.
simplified_primary_school:
The Blue Hawaiian is also called the Swimming Pool. It is like a Piña Colada drink, but it has a little blue liquid called curaçao. This blue liquid makes it taste like oranges.

You make it with light rum, blue curaçao, pineapple juice, creamy coconut, and lemon juice.

It has a piece of pineapple, a red cherry, and a tiny umbrella on top. You drink it from a tall glass called a hurricane glass with tiny ice, or from a Collins glass.

You can make a frozen Blue Hawaiian. To do this, you put all the things you need into a blender with some crushed ice. Then you mix it until it is smooth and creamy.

The light rum helps the blue color from the curaçao show up. If you add more pineapple juice, it turns a greenish-blue color.

You can add champagne or prosecco to make a fizzy Blue Hawaiian.

A book called Difford's Guide says that a person named Don the Beachcomber in Los Angeles, USA, probably made this drink. It is like another drink called the Blue Hawaii.
simplified_secondary_school:
The Blue Hawaiian, also known as the Swimming Pool, is a cocktail similar to a Piña Colada, but with an added splash of blue curaçao. This ingredient gives the drink its distinctive blue color and contributes an orange flavor.

To make it, you'll need light rum (such as Pineapple Malibu rum or white rum), blue curaçao, pineapple juice, cream of coconut, and lemon juice. The use of light rum is important because it allows the blue color from the curaçao to be clearly visible. If you add more pineapple juice, the drink will turn a greenish-blue color.

The Blue Hawaiian is typically served in a hurricane glass with pebble ice, or in a Collins glass. It's garnished with a pineapple wedge, a maraschino cherry, and a cocktail umbrella.

For a frozen version, all the ingredients can be blended with some crushed ice until they reach a creamy consistency. You can also make a sparkling Blue Hawaiian by adding Champagne or Prosecco.

Difford's Guide notes that this drink was "Probably created by Don the Beachcomber in Los Angeles, USA," and describes it as "a riff on the Blue Hawaii." It is considered a fruity, creamy, and sweet Tiki drink, featuring rum, coconut, lemon juice, and pineapple juice.

original_text: long time coverage and high radial velocity precision have allowed for the discovery of additional objects in known planetary systems . many of the extrasolar planets detected have highly eccentric orbits , which raises the question of how likely those systems are to host additional planets . we investigate six systems which contain a very eccentric ( @xmath0 ) planet : hd 3651 , hd 37605 , hd 45350 , hd 80606 , hd 89744 , and 16 cyg b. we present updated radial - velocity observations and orbital solutions , search for additional planets , and perform test particle simulations to find regions of dynamical stability . the dynamical simulations show that short - period planets could exist in the hd 45350 and 16 cyg b systems , and we use the observational data to set tight detection limits , which rule out additional planets down to a few neptune masses in the hd 3651 , hd 45350 , and 16 cyg b systems .
simplified_kindergarten:
We watched for a long time. We looked very carefully at how fast things moved. This helped us find more things in places with planets.

Many planets we found go around their star in a very stretched-out path. This makes us wonder if these places can have more planets.

We looked at six places. Each place has one planet that goes in a very stretched-out path. The places are HD 3651, HD 37605, HD 45350, HD 80606, HD 89744, and 16 Cyg B.

We show new ways we watched how fast things moved. We show how the planets move around their stars. We looked for more planets. We used computers to see where other planets could safely stay.

The computer games showed us that small, fast planets could be in HD 45350 and 16 Cyg B. We used what we saw to say how small a planet we could find. We know there are no more planets as big as a few Neptunes in HD 3651, HD 45350, and 16 Cyg B.
simplified_primary_school:
We have looked at star systems for a long time and measured how stars move very carefully. This helped us find more planets in systems we already knew. Many planets we found outside our solar system travel in very stretched-out paths around their star. This makes us wonder if these systems can have more planets.

We studied six star systems. Each of these systems has a planet that travels in a very stretched-out path. The systems are called hd 3651, hd 37605, hd 45350, hd 80606, hd 89744, and 16 cyg b.

We shared new measurements of how fast stars move and how planets orbit them. We looked for more planets. We also used computer models to find places where other planets could safely orbit.

Our computer models showed that small planets that orbit quickly could be in the hd 45350 and 16 cyg b systems. We used our observations to set strong limits. This means there are no more planets as big as a few Neptunes in the hd 3651, hd 45350, and 16 cyg b systems.
simplified_secondary_school:
Observing planetary systems for a long time with very accurate measurements of a star's wobble has helped us find more planets in systems we already knew about. Many planets found outside our solar system have orbits that are very stretched out, not circular. This makes us wonder if these systems are likely to have even more planets.

We studied six specific planetary systems that each contain a planet with a very stretched-out orbit: HD 3651, HD 37605, HD 45350, HD 80606, HD 89744, and 16 Cyg b. We provided new measurements of how these stars wobble and updated calculations of their planets' orbits. We looked for more planets and used computer simulations to find stable areas where other planets could exist.

Our computer simulations showed that planets orbiting close to their star could exist in the HD 45350 and 16 Cyg b systems. Using our observations, we were able to set strict limits, confirming that there are no additional planets larger than a few times the mass of Neptune in the HD 3651, HD 45350, and 16 Cyg b systems.

original_text: A microgyrus is an area of the cerebral cortex that includes only four cortical layers instead of six. Microgyria are believed by some to be part of the genetic lack of prenatal development which is a cause of, or one of the causes of, dyslexia. Albert Galaburda of Harvard Medical School noticed that language centers in dyslexic brains showed microscopic flaws known as ectopias and microgyria (Galaburda et al., 2006, Nature Neuroscience 9(10): 1213-1217). Both affect the normal six-layer structure of the cortex. These flaws affect connectivity and functionality of the cortex in critical areas related to sound and visual processing. These and similar structural abnormalities may be the basis of the inevitable and hard to overcome difficulty in reading. References External links The neurological basis of developmental dyslexia Another article on the subject Birthdates of neurons in induced microgyria Cerebrum Congenital disorders of nervous system Dyslexia
simplified_kindergarten:
A microgyrus is a small part of the brain. It has four layers. Most brain parts have six layers.

Some people think these small brain parts happen before a baby is born. They think this can make it hard for some kids to read.

A man named Albert Galaburda looked at brains. He saw tiny problems in brains of kids who find reading hard. He called these problems ectopias and microgyria.

These problems change the usual six layers of the brain.

These problems change how the brain works. They change parts that help us hear and see.

These problems might be why it is very hard for some kids to learn to read.
simplified_primary_school:
A microgyrus is a small part of the brain's outer layer. It has only four layers, but it should have six. Some people think these microgyria happen because a baby's brain didn't grow quite right before birth. This can be due to genes. They believe this is one reason why some people have dyslexia.

A doctor named Albert Galaburda from Harvard Medical School looked at the brains of people with dyslexia. He saw that the parts of the brain that help with language had tiny problems. These problems are called ectopias and microgyria. He wrote about this in a science paper in 2006.

Both ectopias and microgyria change how the brain's outer layer should normally have six layers. These problems make it hard for different parts of the brain's outer layer to work together and do their jobs, especially in parts that help us hear sounds and see things. These problems and other similar ones in the brain's structure might be why it is so hard for some people to learn to read, and why it's hard to get over this problem.
simplified_secondary_school: A microgyrus is a part of the brain's outer layer, called the cerebral cortex, that has only four layers instead of the usual six. Some scientists believe that microgyria develop before birth due to genetic factors and are one cause of dyslexia. Dr. Albert Galaburda of Harvard Medical School observed that the language centers in the brains of people with dyslexia showed tiny flaws known as ectopias and microgyria. Both of these flaws affect the normal six-layer structure of the cortex. These defects impact how different parts of the cortex connect and function, especially in important areas for processing sounds and visual information. These and similar differences in brain structure may be why reading can be so difficult and challenging to overcome for some individuals.

original_text: (CNN) -- President Obama issued a presidential order on Friday authorizing emergency funds to Pakistan's flood victims, the White House announced Friday. The president said the use of the Emergency Refugee and Migration Assistance Fund was in the United States' interest. Up to $33 million can go toward the country's crisis. Pakistani disaster officials said Wednesday that recent floods have killed about 1,677 people and 17 million have been affected nationwide. The United Nations, meanwhile, warned that a crisis is building in the eastern province of Balochistan, where nearly 2 million people are affected. Mengeshe Kebede, the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees' representative to Pakistan, said there is a "humanitarian tragedy" taking place in Balochistan, with imminent threats of water-borne diseases and food shortages. "We need to scale up our activities in the province, or else I think we are heading for a major humanitarian disaster there," Kebede said. "I have worked in humanitarian situations globally, and worked in refugee camps in Africa during emergencies, but to be honest I had never seen a situation as devastating as I saw in Balochistan," he said.

original_text: open quantum systems are studied from the thermodynamical point of view unifying the principle of maximum informational entropy and the hypothesis of relaxation times hierarchy . the result of the unification is a non - markovian and local in time master equation that provides a direct connection of dynamical and thermodynamical properties of open quantum systems . the power of the approach is illustrated with the application to the damped harmonic oscillator and the damped driven two - level system resulting in analytical expressions for the non - markovian and non - equilibrium entropy and inverse temperature .
simplified_kindergarten:
We look at open quantum systems. We study how hot or cold they are. We put two ideas together. One idea is about maximum informational entropy. The other idea is about how fast things calm down.

We get a special math rule. It is a non-Markovian and local in time master equation. It shows how open quantum systems move. It also shows how hot or cold they are.

We show how well this works. We use it on a damped harmonic oscillator. We also use it on a damped driven two-level system. We get math answers. These answers are for non-Markovian and non-equilibrium entropy. And for inverse temperature.
simplified_primary_school:
We study special tiny systems that are open. We look at how heat and energy work in them. We do this by bringing together two ideas: a rule about how much information can be spread out, and an idea about how things settle down at different speeds.

This joining of ideas gives us a special math rule. This rule remembers what happened before and only looks at what is happening right now. It directly connects how things move and change with how heat and energy work in these special tiny systems that are open.

We show how well this method works by using it on two examples. One is like a bouncing spring that slows down. The other is a tiny system with two energy levels that is pushed and slows down. This helps us write down exact math formulas for three things: the messiness (entropy) that remembers what happened before, the messiness when things are not settled, and a special way to measure how hot or cold something is.
simplified_secondary_school: Open quantum systems are studied using thermodynamics. This approach brings together two key ideas: the principle of maximum informational entropy and the hypothesis of relaxation times hierarchy. This combination leads to a special mathematical description called a master equation. This equation is 'non-Markovian' (meaning it considers the system's past) and 'local in time'. It directly links how open quantum systems behave over time (their dynamics) with their energy and heat properties (thermodynamics). The effectiveness of this approach is demonstrated by applying it to specific examples: a damped harmonic oscillator and a damped driven two-level system. This application provides exact mathematical formulas (analytical expressions) for the system's non-Markovian and non-equilibrium entropy, as well as its inverse temperature.

original_text: we consider anisotropies in the cosmic microwave background ( cmb ) generated by spatially limited seeds ; these objects could correspond to relics of high energy symmetry breaking in the early universe . it is shown how the cmb perturbation propagate beyond the size of the seed in the form of waves traveling with the cmb sound velocity . moreover , these waves are the substantial part of the signal , both for polarization and temperature . the explanation of this phenomenology in terms of the cmb equations is given . observationally , this effect is threefold promising . first , it enlarges the signal from a seed intersecting the last scattering surface to the scale of the cmb sound horizon at decoupling , that is roughly one degree in the sky . second , it offers cross correlation possibilities between the polarization and temperature signals . third , it allows to unambiguously distinguish these structures from point - like astrophysical sources .

Tiny seeds make these spots.
These seeds are old bits from when space was very new.

The spots spread out from the seeds.
They move like waves.
They move as fast as sound in the CMB.

These waves are the main part of what we see.
They show up in two ways.

Math helps us understand this.

We can see this.
It helps us in three ways.

First, it makes the spot from a seed bigger.
This happens when a seed is in the old light.
It makes the spot as big as a sound wave in the CMB.
That is like a small circle in the sky.

Second, we can compare the bright spots and the special light spots.

Third, we can tell these spots apart.
We can tell them from other tiny lights in space.
simplified_primary_school: We look at tiny differences in the CMB light, which is light from the early universe. These differences are made by small, special seeds. These seeds might be leftovers from big changes when the universe was very, very young. We see how these changes in the CMB light spread out from the seeds. They spread like waves, moving at the speed of sound in the early universe. These waves are the most important part of what we see, both in how the light wiggles and how hot it is. We can explain how this happens using special math rules for the CMB. This is good for three reasons. First, it makes the signal from a seed that lines up with the baby picture of the universe look bigger. It makes it as big as how far sound could travel in the early universe, right when the light became free. This is about one degree in the sky. Second, we can compare the wiggles of the light with its temperature. Third, it helps us clearly tell these things apart from other tiny, bright things in space like stars.
simplified_secondary_school:
We are studying differences (anisotropies) in the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB), which is the oldest light in the universe. These differences are caused by small, localized "seeds." These seeds might be remnants from a very early, energetic period in the universe's history when fundamental forces were separating.

We show how disturbances in the CMB, caused by these seeds, spread out much further than the original size of the seed. They travel like waves at the speed of sound within the early universe's hot, dense plasma (the CMB sound velocity). Importantly, these waves make up most of the signal we observe, affecting both the temperature and the polarization (the orientation of the light waves) of the CMB. The scientific explanation for these observed effects is provided using the mathematical equations that describe the CMB.

From an observational perspective, this effect is promising for three main reasons:
1. It makes the signal from a seed much larger. If a seed was present when the CMB light was released (at the "last scattering surface"), its signal expands to the size of the CMB sound horizon at that time, which is about one degree across the sky. This larger size makes it easier to detect.
2. It allows us to compare and find connections (cross-correlate) between the polarization and temperature signals of the CMB.
3. It helps us clearly tell these structures apart from small, point-like objects like distant stars or galaxies that we see in space.

original_text: we use maximal entropy random walk ( merw ) to study the trapping problem in dendrimers modeled by cayley trees with a deep trap fixed at the central node . we derive an explicit expression for the mean first passage time from any node to the trap , as well as an exact formula for the average trapping time ( att ) , which is the average of the source - to - trap mean first passage time over all non - trap starting nodes . based on the obtained closed - form solution for att , we further deduce an upper bound for the leading behavior of att , which is the fourth power of @xmath0 , where @xmath1 is the system size . this upper bound is much smaller than the att of trapping depicted by unbiased random walk in cayley trees , the leading scaling of which is a linear function of @xmath1 . these results show that merw can substantially enhance the efficiency of trapping performed in dendrimers .

We use MERW. We look at a trap problem. It is in special trees. There is a big trap in the middle.
We find how long it takes to get to the trap. From any spot. We also find the ATT. ATT is how long it takes on average. From all other spots to the trap.
We used the ATT answer. We found a top number for ATT. It is like @xmath0 times itself four times. @xmath1 is how big the tree is.
This top number is much smaller. It is smaller than ATT from another walk. That walk is called unbiased random walk. In the special trees. That walk's ATT is just like @xmath1.
This means MERW makes trapping much better. In the special trees.
simplified_primary_school:
We use a special kind of walk, called MERW, to look at how things get caught in tree-like shapes. These shapes have a strong trap right in the middle. We found a clear way to figure out how long it takes, on average, to get from any spot to the trap for the first time. We also found a precise way to calculate the "average trapping time" (ATT). This ATT is the average of how long it takes to get to the trap from all the different starting spots.

Using our complete answer for ATT, we also found a top limit for how big ATT can get. This limit is like the system's size multiplied by itself four times, where the system's size is how big the tree-like shape is. This top limit is much, much smaller than the average trapping time if things just walked around randomly in the same tree shapes. When things walk randomly, the time grows simply with how big the tree shape is. These findings show that using the MERW walk makes catching things in these tree-like shapes much, much better.
simplified_secondary_school:
We are studying how things get trapped in structures called dendrimers, which we model as Cayley trees. In these models, a very strong trap is placed at the center. To understand this trapping, we use a method called maximal entropy random walk (MERW).

We developed a clear formula to calculate the 'mean first passage time' – that is, the average time it takes to reach the trap from any starting point. We also found an exact formula for the 'average trapping time' (ATT). ATT represents the average of these mean first passage times, calculated from all possible starting points that are not the trap itself.

Using this direct formula for ATT, we then figured out an upper limit for how ATT grows. This upper limit is the fourth power of a value that depends on the system's size. This upper limit for ATT is significantly smaller than what you would find if you used a simpler method called 'unbiased random walk' in the same Cayley trees. With the unbiased random walk, the ATT grows much faster, increasing directly with the system's size.

These findings demonstrate that using MERW significantly improves how efficiently trapping occurs in dendrimers.

original_text: we study the ground - state properties of one - dimensional mixtures of bosonic and fermionic atoms resonantly coupled to fermionic feshbach molecules . when the particle densities of fermionic atoms and feshbach molecules differ , the system undergoes various depletion transitions between binary and ternary mixtures , as a function of the detuning parameter . however , when the particle densities of fermionic atoms and feshbach molecules are identical , the molecular conversion and disassociation processes induce a gap in a sector of low - energy excitations , and the remaining system can be described by a two - component tomonaga - luttinger liquid . using a bosonization scheme , we derive the effective low - energy hamiltonian for the system , which has a similar form as that of the two - chain problem of coupled tomonaga - luttinger liquids . with the help of improved perturbative renormalization group analysis of the latter problem , we determine the ground - state phase diagram and find that it contains a phase dominated by composite superfluid or pairing correlations between the open and closed resonant channels .
simplified_kindergarten:
We look at tiny things. They are mixed up in a line. There are two kinds of tiny things. They stick to other tiny things.

If there are not the same number of the tiny things and the other tiny things, things change a lot. They go from two kinds of mixes to three kinds of mixes. This happens when we turn a special knob.

But if there are the same number of the tiny things and the other tiny things, when they change or break apart, they make a space where some small wiggles cannot happen. What is left acts like two special kinds of liquid.

We use a special math trick. We find a simple rule for how the quiet things work. This rule is like a rule for two lines of special liquids stuck together.

We use another special math trick to look at this rule. We draw a map of how the quiet things can be. On this map, we see a part where tiny things stick together very well. They act like a special flowing goo. This happens in two ways they can connect.
simplified_primary_school:
We look at what happens when special tiny bits (bosonic and fermionic atoms) are mixed in a line. These bits are strongly linked to other tiny bits called Feshbach molecules. We study them when they are very still and have the least energy.

If there are different amounts of fermionic atoms and Feshbach molecules, the mix changes. It can go from having two main parts to three main parts. This depends on a special number called the detuning parameter.

But if there are the same amounts of fermionic atoms and Feshbach molecules, the way they change into each other makes a gap in how they can move with very little energy. What is left of the mix can then be described as a special kind of liquid called a two-component Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid.

We use a special math method to find a simple rule for how the mix acts when it has very little energy. This rule looks like the rule for a problem with two linked Tomonaga-Luttinger liquids.

By studying that other problem in a better way, we made a map of what the mix is like when it's very still. This map shows a part where the mix acts like a special liquid that flows easily, or where tiny bits link up in special ways between two different connections.
simplified_secondary_school:
We investigate the properties of mixtures of bosonic and fermionic atoms when they are in their lowest energy state and arranged in a single line. These atoms are strongly linked, or "resonantly coupled," to a specific type of fermionic molecule called Feshbach molecules.

When the number of fermionic atoms and Feshbach molecules in a given space is not equal, the system changes its composition. Depending on a control setting called the "detuning parameter," it can switch between mixtures that have two main components (binary) and those with three (ternary), as some components become depleted.

However, if the number of fermionic atoms and Feshbach molecules is exactly the same, the processes of molecules forming and breaking apart create an energy "gap" for certain low-energy changes. What's left of the system can then be understood using a model called a "two-component Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid."

Using a mathematical technique called "bosonization," we developed an effective low-energy "Hamiltonian" (a mathematical description of the system's energy) for this system. This Hamiltonian looks similar to the one used for a "two-chain problem" involving two connected Tomonaga-Luttinger liquids.

By using an improved "perturbative renormalization group analysis" (a detailed theoretical method) on this related two-chain problem, we mapped out the system's "ground-state phase diagram." This diagram shows that one of the stable states is dominated by "composite superfluid" behavior, or by "pairing correlations" between the "open" and "closed resonant channels" (different ways the particles can interact).

original_text: (CNN) -- Pirates have commandeered a Taiwanese-flagged fishing boat off the coast of Africa, the European Union's Naval Force in Somalia announced on Friday. The naval force said the Feng Guo No. 168 departed from Port Louis, Mauritius, on October 1, with 14 crew members onboard, including a Taiwanese captain and sailors from Vietnam, China and Indonesia. The vessel was hijacked in the Gulf of Aden, about 250 nautical miles from Madagascar. That location makes the incident the southernmost act of piracy since the European Union began its first-ever naval response, Operation ATALANTA, in December 2008, according to the naval force. The force said the operation exists to combat piracy and protect vulnerable ships in the Gulf of Aden and the Indian Ocean, especially merchant vessels carrying humanitarian aid from the World Food Program and the African Union Mission in Somalia. Somali pirates now hold 18 ships and 383 hostages after snatching the Feng Guo No. 168, the naval force said.

original_text: West Rutland is the central village and a census-designated place (CDP) in the town of West Rutland, Rutland County, Vermont, United States. As of the 2020 census, it had a population of 1,898, out of 2,214 in the entire town of West Rutland. The CDP is in central Rutland County, in the southern part of the town. It sits in a valley on the east side of the Taconic Mountains, drained to the east by the Clarendon River, a tributary of Otter Creek, and to the west by the Castleton River, a tributary of the Poultney River. Both sets of rivers eventually flow to Lake Champlain. U.S. Route 4, a four-lane expressway, passes south of the village center, with access from Exit 6. Route 4 leads east to U.S. Route 7 on the south side of Rutland and west to Fair Haven. Vermont Route 4A, former US 4, is Main Street through West Rutland; it leads east to downtown Rutland and west to Castleton. Vermont Route 133 (Clarendon Avenue) leads south from the center of West Rutland to Middletown Springs. original_text: we investigate the quasinormal modes for gravitational perturbations of rotating black holes in four dimensional anti - de sitter ( ads ) spacetime . the study of the quasinormal frequencies related to these modes is relevant to the ads / cft correspondence . although results have been obtained for schwarzschild and reissner - nordstrom ads black holes , quasinormal frequencies of kerr - ads black holes are computed for the first time . we solve the teukolsky equations in ads spacetime , providing a second order and a pade approximation for the angular eigenvalues associated to the teukolsky angular equation . the transformation theory and the regge - wheeler - zerilli equations for kerr - ads are obtained .

We look at how spinning black holes shake. These shakes are called quasinormal modes. They are in a place called Ads space.

We look at how fast these shakes happen. This helps us understand a big idea called Ads/CFT.

We already know about Schwarzschild black holes. We also know about Reissner-Nordstrom black holes. But we are finding how fast Kerr-Ads black holes shake. This is the first time.

We solve special math problems. They are called Teukolsky equations. We do this in Ads space. We use two ways to find answers that are almost right. One way is called second order. The other way is called Pade. These help us find special numbers for the Teukolsky angular equation.

We also get a special way to change things. It is called transformation theory. We also get other special math problems. They are called Regge-Wheeler-Zerilli equations. These are for Kerr-Ads black holes.
simplified_primary_school:
We look at the special ways spinning black holes wobble. These black holes are in a special kind of space called anti-de Sitter (or AdS) space. Learning about how fast these wobbles happen is important for something called the AdS/CFT idea.

People have already found answers for Schwarzschild and Reissner-Nordstrom AdS black holes. But we are finding how fast Kerr-AdS black holes wobble for the very first time.

We solve special math problems called Teukolsky equations in AdS space. We use two ways to guess the answers for special numbers in the Teukolsky angular math problem. We also figure out a special way to change how we look at the math, called transformation theory. And we find the Regge-Wheeler-Zerilli equations for Kerr-AdS black holes.
simplified_secondary_school: We are studying the quasinormal modes that occur when rotating black holes in a four-dimensional Anti-de Sitter (AdS) spacetime are disturbed by gravity. Understanding the frequencies of these quasinormal modes is important for the AdS/CFT correspondence. While results for Schwarzschild and Reissner-Nordström AdS black holes have been found before, this work calculates the quasinormal frequencies for Kerr-AdS black holes for the very first time. To achieve this, we solve the Teukolsky equations in AdS spacetime. We then use two methods, a second-order approximation and a Padé approximation, to find the angular eigenvalues linked to the Teukolsky angular equation. Additionally, we develop the transformation theory and the Regge-Wheeler-Zerilli equations specifically for Kerr-AdS black holes.

"Jurassic World" opens June 12.

We are looking at how safe power systems are. This is about when bad people try to mess with the power. They change how much power is made and used.

The people who run the power system use special tools called PMUs to find these attacks. But the bad people make attacks that are hidden, so the PMUs cannot see them.

We found that if we know where the PMUs are, it's usually simple to find the smallest number of hidden attacks. This is like finding how connected the weak spots are in a special map of the power system. The way we showed this helps us find all the smallest hidden attacks very quickly.

We also talked about how much harm these hidden attacks could do.

When PMUs are put in the best places, we checked how the smallest hidden attacks and their possible harm change. This depends on how many PMUs are used. We checked this for different power systems, like the IEEE 30, 57, 118 and 300-bus systems and the Polish 2383, 2737 and 3012-bus systems.

simplified_secondary_school:
We are studying the physical security of power networks when they are subjected to "power injection attacks" that alter how much electricity is generated and consumed. The power grid operators use special sensors called Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) to detect these attacks. However, attackers design their attacks to be "unobservable," meaning the PMU networks cannot detect them.

We show that if we know where the PMUs are placed, finding the "sparsest" unobservable attacks (those that cause the least disruption while remaining hidden) usually has a simple solution. This solution is related to what we call the "vulnerable vertex connectivity" of a modified version of the power network's graph. Our method of proof also allows us to find all possible "sparsest" unobservable attacks relatively quickly, even in large power systems.

We also introduce a way to measure the "potential impact" that these unobservable attacks could have. We then numerically evaluated the "sparsest" unobservable attacks and their potential impact, considering how these change based on the number of PMUs and their optimal placement. This was tested on several standard power grid models, including IEEE systems (with 30, 57, 118, and 300 connection points) and larger Polish systems (with 2383, 2737, and 3012 connection points).

We observed that as more PMUs are added to the system, the maximum potential impact of these "sparsest" unobservable attacks quickly decreases. This reduction continues until the attacks themselves reach their minimum possible "sparsity" (meaning they involve the fewest possible changes).

original_text: WASHINGTON (CNN) -- Timothy Joseph Russert Sr., the father of the late Tim Russert of NBC's "Meet the Press," died from natural causes Thursday, according to his family. He was 85. The elder Russert was the subject of his son's 2004 book "Big Russ and Me." The New York Times bestseller captured the lessons passed down from father to son. "While he was affectionately known to the world as 'Big Russ,' he carried no more important nor meaningful titles than those of father, grandfather, great-grandfather, patriot and friend," the family said in a statement. The younger Russert, who became one of the mainstays of television journalism's political talk as the host of "Meet the Press," died from a heart attack in June 2008 at age 58.

original_text: Frank Edgar Evans (19 November 1876 – 25 November 1941) served as an infantryman in the Spanish–American War, and was commissioned in the United States Marine Corps on 15 February 1900. Biography He was born in Franklin, Pennsylvania on 19 November 1876. He served in the Philippines and in the United States prior to World War I, during which he won the Navy Cross and other awards for the distinction of his service in the Marine Brigade of the American Expeditionary Force in France. His postwar service included duty in Haiti, where from 1927 to 1930 he commanded the Constabulary Detachment and was Chief of the Gendarmerle d'Haiti. Brigadier General Evans also was District Marine Officer of several Naval Districts. Retired 1 December 1940, he made his home in Honolulu, Hawaii, where he died 25 November 1941. Legacy The destroyer USS Frank E. Evans (DD-754) was named in his honor. Frank Edgar Evans, born in Franklin, Pennsylvania, on November 19, 1876, first served as an infantryman in the Spanish–American War. He was commissioned as an officer in the United States Marine Corps on February 15, 1900.

Before World War I, Evans served in the Philippines and the United States. During World War I, he earned the Navy Cross and other awards for his outstanding service in the Marine Brigade of the American Expeditionary Force in France.

After the war, Brigadier General Evans's service included duty in Haiti. From 1927 to 1930, he commanded the Constabulary Detachment and was Chief of the Gendarmerle d'Haiti there. He also served as a District Marine Officer for several Naval Districts.

original_text: (CNN) -- Four people were killed and one seriously injured in an explosion at a Chevron Corp. refinery in western Wales, the refinery's general manager said early Friday. original_text: Île Jésus (, Jesus Island) is a river island in southwestern Quebec, separated from the mainland to the north by the Rivière des Mille Îles, and from the Island of Montreal to the south by the Rivière des Prairies. The second-largest () island in the Hochelaga Archipelago (after the Island of Montreal), Île Jésus is the major component of the City of Laval, along with the Îles Laval and several other islands. Île Jésus (Jesus Island) is a river island located in southwestern Quebec. It is the second-largest island in the Hochelaga Archipelago, after the Island of Montreal. Île Jésus is the major component of the City of Laval, alongside the Îles Laval and several other islands.

The island still has a considerable rural portion, with most of its urban areas concentrated in the central region and along the southern and western river banks. The man was then flown by the Coastguard helicopter to the Queen Alexandra Hospital in Cosham, Portsmouth, where he was pronounced dead. The man's name is yet to be released. A spokeswoman for the Maritime and Coastguard Agency said very little was known about the circumstances leading to the man's death, other than the fact he was windsurfing with a friend at the time. She said the conditions were not unlikely to be a factor in the death as wind gusts had been mild in the area.

A small boat saw his board and sail. It was noon. They called for help.

Many helpers came. A big helicopter came. Three rescue boats came. Police and doctors came too.

They found the man in the water. He was face down. They put him on a boat. They took him to the land.

The helicopter took the man to a hospital. Doctors said he was not alive.

We do not know his name yet.

A helper lady said we do not know why he died. He was with a friend. They were on their boards.

The helper lady said the weather was not bad. The wind was not strong.

A man was found floating face down in the water. Helpers pulled him onto a boat and took him to the land. The Coastguard helicopter flew the man to a hospital called Queen Alexandra Hospital in Cosham, Portsmouth. At the hospital, doctors said he had died. We do not know the man's name yet. Someone from the Coastguard said they do not know much about why the man died. They only know he was windsurfing with a friend. She also said the weather was probably not the reason he died, because the wind was not very strong.

A windsurfer was found dead today after his equipment was spotted in the sea near Portsmouth.

The alarm was raised at 12:15 pm when a dinghy saw windsurfing gear just outside Langstone Harbour. Emergency services, including the Hayling Island Coastguard Rescue Team, a Coastguard helicopter from Lee-on-the-Solent, three RNLI lifeboats, police, and ambulance crews, were sent to the scene.

Rescuers found a man face down in the water. He was pulled onto a boat and taken to shore. The Coastguard helicopter then flew him to Queen Alexandra Hospital in Cosham, Portsmouth, where he was pronounced dead.

The man's name has not yet been released. A spokeswoman for the Maritime and Coastguard Agency stated that little was known about the circumstances of his death, beyond the fact he was windsurfing with a friend. She added that conditions were not unlikely to be a factor in the death, noting that wind gusts in the area had been mild.

simplified_secondary_school:
Progeny Linux Systems was a company based in Indianapolis that provided technology for Linux platforms. Their Platform Services technology supported Linux distributions based on both Debian and RPM.

The company was founded by Ian Murdock, who was also the founder of Debian and served as Progeny's chairman of the board. John H. Hartman was the Chief Technology Officer (CTO), and Bruce Byfield was the director for marketing and communications.

Progeny developed a free operating system called Progeny Componentized Linux, which was commonly known as Progeny Debian. This system was an alternative to Debian 3.1. It was built upon the Linux Standard Base (LSB) 3.0 and incorporated technologies such as the Anaconda installer (originally ported from Red Hat), the Advanced Packaging Tool, and Discover. Progeny Debian aimed to serve as a model for developing Linux systems based on individual components.

On May 1, 2007, Progeny announced through posts to its mailing lists that it was ceasing operations. The company subsequently shut down its website, making Progeny Debian a defunct operating system.

original_text: (CNN) -- A Texas inmate escaped from a prison van near Houston after pulling a gun on two guards who were transporting him between prisons, according to a Texas prison statement Monday. Arcade Joseph Comeaux Jr., 49, ran away with the guards' guns and wearing one of their gray correctional officer uniforms, the statement from the Texas Department of Criminal Justice said. Comeaux was serving a life sentence for indecency with a child, aggravated sexual assault and two counts of aggravated assault with a deadly weapon, it said. The escape happened near Baytown, Texas, about 25 miles east of Houston, it said. He was being taken from the Estelle prison in Huntsville to the Stiles prison in Beaumont, a 150-mile drive, when he "brandished a firearm and directed the officers to stop the vehicle," the statement said. "He took the officers' weapons and apparently handcuffed them together in the back of the transport vehicle," it said. Police found the handcuffed guards unharmed in the vehicle at 10 a.m. CT, the statement said.

Robertsfors Municipality is located in Västerbotten County in northern Sweden. Its administrative center, or seat, is the town of Robertsfors.

In 1759, the small village of Edfastmark was transformed into Robertsfors Bruk, an ironworks. The new ironworks was founded by Irishman John Jennings and his brother-in-law, Scotsman Robert Finlay. The name Robertsfors was given to the ironworks, and later the town, after Robert Finlay.

Robertsfors Municipality contains three localities, or urban areas. Robertsfors itself serves as the municipal seat.

Notable individuals from Robertsfors include Carl Olof Rosenius, a revivalist preacher; Frida Hyvönen, an artist; Maud Olofsson, a politician; and the rock group Sahara Hotnights.

simplified_primary_school:
In group math, the 'normal closure' of a small part (a 'subset') of a group is the smallest special group (a 'normal subgroup') that holds that small part.

To be exact, if you have a group and a subset, the normal closure is what all the special groups that hold your subset have in common. It is the smallest special group because it is a part of every other special group that also holds your subset.

You can also make this special group by taking all the different 'versions' (called 'conjugates') of the things in your subset.

If a group is already a special group, then its normal closure is just that same group.

If your subset is empty (has nothing in it), then the normal closure is a very small group with only one thing.

People use different symbols to write down 'normal closure' in math books.

There is also an opposite idea called 'normal interior.' This is the biggest special group that is completely inside your subset.

When groups are described in a special way, a certain symbol means you are making a new group by dividing it.
simplified_secondary_school:
In group theory, the **normal closure** of a subset within a group is the smallest normal subgroup of that group which contains the given subset.

To clarify what "smallest" means: if you have a subset `S` of a group `G`, its normal closure `N(S)` is a normal subgroup of `G` that includes `S`. It is considered the "smallest" because `N(S)` is a subset of *every other* normal subgroup of `G` that also contains `S`.

You can understand the normal closure in two main ways:
1.  **By intersection:** It is the intersection of all normal subgroups of `G` that contain `S`. This means it consists of all elements that are common to every single normal subgroup that includes `S`.
2.  **By generation:** It is the subgroup generated by the set of all "conjugates" of elements from `S` within `G`. A conjugate of an element `x` by another element `g` in the group is `gxg⁻¹`. The normal closure is formed by taking all elements from `S` and all their conjugates, and then combining them through multiplication and inverses to create all possible elements of the subgroup.

Here are some properties:
*   Any normal subgroup is equal to its own normal closure.
*   The normal closure of an empty set is the trivial subgroup (the subgroup containing only the identity element).

Other notations used for the normal closure in mathematical literature include `S^G` and `⟨S⟩^G`.

Related to the normal closure is the concept of the **normal core** (also written as `core_G(H)` or `H_G`). This is the opposite idea. For a subgroup `H`, its normal core is the largest normal subgroup of `G` that is *contained within* `H`. It is defined as the "join" (the smallest subgroup that contains all of them) of all normal subgroups of `G` that are themselves subsets of `H`.

When groups are defined using a "presentation" – a way of describing them with "generators" (basic elements) and "relators" (rules or equations these elements must satisfy) – the normal closure plays a role. For example, in the notation `G = <X | R>`, the group `G` is formed by taking a "free group" on the generators `X` and then applying the rules `R` by taking the normal closure of `R`. This ensures that the rules `R` are consistently enforced throughout the group.

original_text: thomson s theorem states that static charge distributions in conductors only exist at the conducting surfaces in an equipotential configuration , yielding a minimal electrostatic energy . in this work we present a proof for this theorem based on the variational principle . furthermore , an analogue statement for magnetic systems is also introduced and proven : the stored magnetic field energy reaches the minimum value for superficial current distributions so that the magnetic vector potential points in the same direction as the surface current . this is the counterpart of thomson s theorem for the magnetic field . the result agrees with the fact that currents in superconductors are confined near the surface and indicates that the distinction between superconductors and hypothetical perfect conductors is fictitious .
simplified_kindergarten:
Thomson's rule talks about still charges.
These charges are only on the outside of special things.
All parts of the outside are the same.
This makes the power very small.

We show this rule is true.

We also have a similar rule for magnets.
We show this rule is true too.
The power in the magnet is as small as it can be.
This happens when power flows only on the outside.
And the magnet's push goes the same way as the power flow on the outside.

This is like Thomson's rule, but for magnets.

This matches what we know.
Power flows only on the outside of super-power things.
It means there is no real difference between super-power things and perfect power things we just think about.
simplified_primary_school:
Thomson's rule says that electric charges that are not moving inside materials that conduct electricity are only found on their surface. All parts of this surface have the same electric level, which makes the electric energy as small as it can be. In this paper, we show how to prove this rule using a special math method.

Also, we talk about and prove a similar rule for magnets. It says that the energy stored in a magnetic field becomes the smallest when electric currents flow only on the surface. This happens when a special magnetic pointer points the same way as the current on the surface. This is Thomson's rule, but for magnets.

This idea matches what we know: electricity in special materials called superconductors stays close to their surface. It also shows that there isn't a real difference between superconductors and imaginary perfect conductors.
simplified_secondary_school:
Thomson's theorem states that static (non-moving) electric charges in a conductor are found only on its outer surfaces. In this setup, the entire conductor and its surface have the same electrical potential, which results in the lowest possible electrostatic energy. In this work, we provide a proof for this theorem using a method called the variational principle.

It also suggests that there's no real difference between superconductors and theoretical 'perfect conductors'.

Middlesbrough head coach Aitor Karanka will watch Saturday's Sky Bet Championship match against Derby from the stands. The Football Association (FA) has given him a one-game touchline ban, effective immediately.

Karanka was also fined £2,000 after admitting an FA charge of improper conduct. This charge was for his behaviour during stoppage time at the end of Middlesbrough's 1-1 draw with Blackburn Rovers on November 29, 2014.

During that game, tempers rose after Blackburn's Rudy Gestede scored a controversial equaliser in the 95th minute. Karanka was accused of pushing fourth official David Coote at that time.

He had requested a personal hearing, and an independent FA commission confirmed the ban and fine on Wednesday.

simplified_secondary_school:
The term "Hatch Act" refers to two distinct US federal laws.

The **Hatch Act of 1887** was US federal legislation that established agricultural experiment stations.

The **Hatch Act of 1939** is US federal legislation that prohibits certain political activities for employees in the executive branch.

Related to these is the **Pendleton Civil Service Reform Act of 1883**, which established that positions within the federal government should be awarded based on merit.

original_text: Brovst is a town in North Jutland, Denmark. It is located in Jammerbugt Municipality, 16 km east of Fjerritslev, 14 km southwest of Aabybro and 2 km east and northeast of the villages of Ny Skovsgård and Skovsgård. Until 1 January 2007 Brovst was the seat of the former Brovst Municipality. History Brovst is first mentioned in 1458 as Brosth. simplified_secondary_school:
Brovst is a town in North Jutland, Denmark. It is located within Jammerbugt Municipality, 16 km east of Fjerritslev, 14 km southwest of Aabybro, and just 2 km east and northeast of the villages of Ny Skovsgård and Skovsgård. Until January 1, 2007, Brovst was the administrative center of the former Brovst Municipality.

The town's history dates back to 1458, when it was first mentioned as Brosth. A train station was built in Brovst in 1897, and it served as a stop on the Fjerritslev-Nørresundby railroad for its entire operational period. When the railroad closed down in 1969, Brovst Station also ceased operations.

Several notable people have connections to Brovst, including Hans Nielsen (born 1959), a speedway rider; Ane Halsboe-Jørgensen (born 1983), a politician and member of the Folketing (the Danish parliament); and Nicolaj Ritter (born 1992), a football player.

We do this by showing that the Gini coefficient – a measure of inequality often used by economists – has an @xmath0 mathematical relationship with both the Boltzmann and Fokker-Planck equations for the model.

The ideas we looked at include three about a special push called disjoining pressure, which comes from tiny pulls. We also looked at an idea for special liquids and an idea about where a liquid and a gas meet in a blurry way. We also looked at ideas about how liquids slide. This study examines how a contact line moves, comparing various models that scientists have suggested. We specifically look at the pressure and stress at the contact line in a basic example: a thin, two-dimensional droplet slowly spreading (quasistatic spreading) on a flat surface.

The models analyzed include three 'disjoining pressure models' based on van der Waals interactions, a model introduced for 'polar fluids,' and a 'liquid-gas diffuse-interface model.' We also investigate 'Navier-slip' and two 'nonlinear slip models.' For these slip models, three 'microscopic contact angle boundary conditions' are applied, with two of these conditions depending on the contact line's speed. The 'interface formation model' is also considered.

Under specific conditions (certain parameter regimes), it is shown that all of these models predict the same slow droplet spreading behavior.

original_text: many algorithms have been proposed for fitting network models with communities , but most of them do not scale well to large networks , and often fail on sparse networks . here we propose a new fast pseudo - likelihood method for fitting the stochastic block model for networks , as well as a variant that allows for an arbitrary degree distribution by conditioning on degrees . we show that the algorithms perform well under a range of settings , including on very sparse networks , and illustrate on the example of a network of political blogs . we also propose spectral clustering with perturbations , a method of independent interest , which works well on sparse networks where regular spectral clustering fails , and use it to provide an initial value for pseudo - likelihood . we prove that pseudo - likelihood provides consistent estimates of the communities under a mild condition on the starting value , for the case of a block model with two communities . , , +
simplified_kindergarten:
People made many ways to find friends in big webs. But most ways don't work for very big webs. They also break on webs with few lines.

We made a new fast way to find friends in webs. We also made a special way. This special way lets friends have many or few friends.

Our ways work well for many kinds of webs. They even work for webs with very few lines. We show this with a web of talk about politics.

We also made another way. It is called 'spectral clustering with wiggles'. This way is good on its own. It works well on webs with few lines. Other ways like it break there. We use it to start our first new way.

We show that our first new way finds the right friends. This is if we start it in a good spot. This is for webs with two groups of friends.
simplified_primary_school:
People have thought of many ways to find groups in networks. But most of these ways don't work well for very big networks. They also often don't work when networks don't have many connections. We made a new, quick way called "pseudo-likelihood" to find groups in networks. This way uses a special model called the "stochastic block model." We also made another version of this way. This other version lets each part of the network have any number of connections, by looking at how many connections each part has.

We show that our ways work well in many different situations. This includes networks that don't have many connections. We show this using an example of websites about politics that are connected.

We also made a special way called "spectral clustering with changes." This way is useful on its own. It works well when networks don't have many connections, even when the usual spectral clustering way doesn't work. We use this special way to get a good starting point for our pseudo-likelihood way.

We proved that our pseudo-likelihood way finds the groups correctly every time. This is true if we start it in a fairly good place, and when the network has two groups using the block model.
simplified_secondary_school: Many methods have been suggested for finding communities within network models. However, most of these methods don't work efficiently with very large networks and often fail when networks have few connections (are sparse). We introduce a new, fast method called "pseudo-likelihood" for analyzing networks using the "stochastic block model." We also present a variation of this method that can handle networks with any pattern of connections by considering the number of connections each point has. We demonstrate that these methods work well in many different situations, even with very sparse networks. We illustrate this using an example of a network of political blogs. We also introduce "spectral clustering with perturbations," a useful method in itself. This method works well on sparse networks where standard "spectral clustering" fails. We use it to provide an initial starting point for our "pseudo-likelihood" method. We prove that, for a block model with two communities, our "pseudo-likelihood" method accurately identifies the communities, provided the initial starting point meets a simple requirement.

original_text: (CNN) -- China's first aircraft carrier sailed out of the northeastern port of Dalian for sea trials Wednesday morning, the state-run news agency Xinhua reported. China has spent nearly a decade refurbishing the 67,000-ton, 300-meter (1,000-foot) ship, begun by the former Soviet Union but never completed. Xinhua quoted military sources as saying the trials would be brief, and the carrier would return to Dalian for further refits. China once renounced aircraft carriers as tools of imperialism, but has been developing a blue-water navy in recent years, along with air power to back it up. China's military modernization a cause for concern? It purchased the rusting, gutted shell of the Varyag, the Soviet-begun carrier now under way, from Ukraine in 1998. Only a handful of other nations operate aircraft carriers -- and only the United States, with 11, has more than two in service.

Some people worry about China's army getting stronger.

original_text: (CNN) -- Rescuers have yet to find any survivors or bodies Saturday after a landslide that buried 83 workers at a gold mine in the Tibet Autonomous Region, reported Xinhua, the Chinese state-run news agency. The likelihood of the miners' survival of the Friday landslide are slim, a government spokesperson was quoted in the Xinhua report. Rescue efforts are ongoing, despite being hindered by additional minor landslides and altitude sickness. The mining site is located at 4,600 meters, which is 15,092 feet. Cold temperatures falling to -3 degrees Celsius (26 degrees Fahrenheit) have hampered the sniffer dogs' sense of smell and their ability to trace the trapped miners, the news agency reported. The incident occurred Friday, about 6 a.m. in Maizhokunggar County, east of Lhasa, the regional capital. The landslide extended three kilometers (almost two miles) and was made up of about 2 million cubic meters (2.6 million cubic yards) of mud, rock and other debris, according to Xinhua. The victims, including two Tibetans, were workers from Tibet Huatailong Mining Development Co. Ltd., a subsidiary of the China National Gold Group Corp., the news agency said. CNN's Lonzo Cook contributed to this report .

The dogs could not find the workers.

The people who were covered were workers. They worked for a big company.

*   The Constitution of the Communist Party of China.
*   The Constitution of the Nationalist Party of China.

original_text: Baghdad, Iraq (CNN) -- A suicide bombing at an Iraqi military base north of Baghdad killed at least 10 people and wounded 25 others Monday, police said. Most of the dead and wounded were Iraqi soldiers, according to Baquba police, who said the truck bomber ran into a barracks. Police combed through the rubble of the building looking for victims. The attack took place in the town of Kanan about 10 kilometers (6 miles) south of Baquba in Diyala province, which is ethnically mixed. The province used to be an al Qaeda stronghold, especially during the peak of sectarian violence in the country between 2005 and 2007. On Monday afternoon, a string of roadside bombings targeted Baghdad communities. One civilian was killed and two others were wounded in western Baghdad's al-Amriya neighborhood after a roadside bomb blast, police said. Eight other people were wounded earlier Monday when three roadside bombs exploded in Baghdad neighborhoods, the interior ministry said.

original_text: The Recording Artists' Coalition (RAC) is an American music industry organization that represents recording artists, and attempts to defend their rights and interests. Compare and contrast with the RIAA, which represents the recording industry. In 2009, it became an element of the National Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences. Their most vocal and energetic actions have been in support of repealing laws which tie artists to contracts with record labels for seven years, and in successfully lobbying to repeal supposedly "technical revisions" to American copyright statutes which shifted ownership of some copyrights from artists to record labels. See also American Federation of Musicians References External links http://www.recordingartistscoalition.com/ http://www.recordingartistscoalition.com/artists.html Music organizations based in the United States

simplified_primary_school: The Recording Artists' Coalition, or RAC, is a group in America that works with music. It speaks for people who make music recordings and tries to keep their rights and what they want safe. It is different from the RIAA. The RIAA speaks for the companies that make music, not the artists. In 2009, it joined another group called the National Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences. The RAC has worked hard to change laws. They want to get rid of rules that make artists stay with music companies for seven years. They also worked to get rid of changes to America's copyright rules. These changes made music companies own some of the artists' work instead of the artists themselves.
simplified_secondary_school:
The Recording Artists' Coalition (RAC) is an American music industry organization that represents recording artists and works to defend their rights and interests. This is different from the RIAA, which represents the broader recording industry, including record labels. In 2009, the RAC became part of the National Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences.

The RAC's most active and vocal efforts have focused on two key areas. They have supported repealing laws that force artists into seven-year contracts with record labels. Additionally, they successfully lobbied to reverse what were called "technical revisions" to American copyright laws, which had transferred ownership of some copyrights from artists to record labels.
