original_text: Year 728 (DCCXXVIII) was a leap year starting on Thursday (link will display the full calendar) of the Julian calendar. The denomination 728 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Europe King Liutprand of the Lombards occupies all of the Exarchate of Ravenna. He advances towards Rome along the Via Cassia, and is met at the city of Sutri by Pope Gregory II, near the borders of the Duchy of Rome. Liutprand signs the Donation of Sutri, by which parts of Latium are given to the papacy (the first extension of papal territory in Italy). This marks the historical foundation of the Papal States. Births Abu Ubaidah, Muslim scholar of Arabic philology (d. 825) Du Huangchang, chancellor of the Tang Dynasty (or 729) Deaths Domnall mac Cellaig, king of Connacht (Ireland) Dúnchad mac Murchado, king of Leinster (Ireland) Hasan al-Basri, Arab theologian Ine, king of Wessex (approximate date) Jarir ibn Atiyah, Arab poet and satirist References

King Liutprand signed a paper. He gave land to the Pope. This was the first time the Pope got land in Italy. This started the Papal States.

Some people were born. Abu Ubaidah was born. He was a smart man. Du Huangchang was born. He was a leader.

Some people died. King Domnall died. He was a king in Ireland. King Dúnchad died. He was a king in Ireland. Hasan al-Basri died. He was a wise man. King Ine died. He was a king. Jarir ibn Atiyah died. He was a poet.

The year was 728. It was a leap year, which means it had an extra day. It started on a Thursday. People used the Julian calendar back then. People started calling this year 728 a long, long time ago. This was when the Anno Domini way of naming years became popular in Europe.

In Europe, a king named Liutprand, who was the king of the Lombards, took over a place called the Exarchate of Ravenna. King Liutprand then marched towards the city of Rome. He walked along a road called the Via Cassia. Near the city of Sutri, he met Pope Gregory II. Sutri was close to the edge of a place called the Duchy of Rome.

King Liutprand signed a special paper called the "Donation of Sutri". With this paper, he gave some land in a place called Latium to the Pope. This was the first time the Pope was given land in Italy. This was the start of what would later be called the Papal States.

A boy named Abu Ubaidah was born. He grew up to be a Muslim scholar who studied the Arabic language. He died in the year 825. Also born was Du Huangchang. He became an important leader, called a chancellor, in the Tang Dynasty. He might have been born in 729 instead.

Some important people died this year. Domnall mac Cellaig, who was a king in Ireland, in a place called Connacht, died. Dúnchad mac Murchado, another king in Ireland, from Leinster, also died. Hasan al-Basri, a wise Arab man who studied religion, passed away. King Ine of Wessex died around this time too. And Jarir ibn Atiyah, an Arab poet who wrote funny and critical poems, passed away.
simplified_secondary_school:
The year 728 was a leap year that began on a Thursday, according to the Julian calendar. This specific year number has been used since the early Middle Ages, when the Anno Domini (AD) system became the main way to name years in Europe.

In Europe, a significant event occurred when King Liutprand of the Lombards conquered the entire Exarchate of Ravenna. He then marched towards Rome along the Via Cassia road. At the city of Sutri, near the border of the Duchy of Rome, he was met by Pope Gregory II. Liutprand signed the Donation of Sutri, an agreement that gave parts of Latium to the papacy. This marked the first official extension of papal territory in Italy and is considered the historical foundation of the Papal States.

Notable births in 728 include Abu Ubaidah, a Muslim scholar known for his studies of Arabic language (who died in 825), and Du Huangchang, a chancellor of the Tang Dynasty in China (though some sources suggest he was born in 729).

Among those who died in 728 were Domnall mac Cellaig, the king of Connacht in Ireland; Dúnchad mac Murchado, the king of Leinster in Ireland; Hasan al-Basri, an Arab theologian; Ine, the king of Wessex (his death is estimated to be around this year); and Jarir ibn Atiyah, an Arab poet and satirist.

original_text: In Greek mythology the Perseides, "those born of Perseus" and Andromeda, are the members of the House of Perseus, descended, according to Valerius Flaccus through Perse and Perses. After the Greek Dark Ages, tradition recalled that Perseus and his descendants the Perseides had ruled Tiryns in Mycenaean times, while the allied branch descended from Perseus' great-uncle Proetus ruled in Argos. Perseus and Andromeda had seven sons: Perses, Alcaeus, Heleus, Mestor, Sthenelus, Electryon, and Cynurus, and two daughters, Gorgophone, and Autochthe. Perses was left in Aethiopia and was believed to have become an ancestor of the Persians. The other descendants ruled Mycenae from Electryon down to Eurystheus, after whom Atreus got the kingdom. The most renowned of the Perseides was Greece's greatest hero, Heracles son of Zeus and Alcmene, daughter of Electryon. Notes Characters in Greek mythology

Other children of Perseus ruled a town called Mycenae. Electryon was a king there. Then other kings ruled, like Eurystheus. After Eurystheus, Atreus became king.

simplified_secondary_school:
In Greek mythology, the Perseides are the members of the House of Perseus, meaning "those born of Perseus." They are the descendants of Perseus and Andromeda, with their lineage, according to Valerius Flaccus, tracing back through Perse and Perses.

Later traditions state that Perseus and his descendants, the Perseides, ruled Tiryns during Mycenaean times. At the same time, an allied family branch, descended from Perseus' great-uncle Proetus, ruled in Argos.

Perseus and Andromeda had seven sons: Perses, Alcaeus, Heleus, Mestor, Sthenelus, Electryon, and Cynurus. They also had two daughters: Gorgophone and Autochthe.

Before, other studies had to use different information for the top and bottom halves of the sky. This meant their information wasn't perfectly the same for the whole sky. Because of this, scientists got different answers when they tried to figure out how much everything in space is moving together. They also couldn't agree if this movement comes from inside or outside our local area of space. With our new study, and by making old information more accurate, we hope to find out exactly how much everything in space is moving together. This investigation examines a complete, full-sky sample of 297 sb@xmath0sc spiral galaxies, which have redshifts (a measure of how far away and fast they are moving) between 4500 and 7000. The survey was specifically designed to collect data that is consistent and accurately measured across both the Northern and Southern hemispheres of the sky.

Previous Tully-Fisher surveys within the Supergalactic Shell (a specific region of space) had to combine separate data sets from the Northern and Southern hemispheres. This meant they couldn't achieve truly uniform data across the entire sky. Analyses of the 'cosmological dipole' (the overall motion of the universe) and 'peculiar velocities' (the individual motions of galaxies) from these previous studies have produced conflicting claims. original_text: Tjoritja / West MacDonnell is a national park in the Northern Territory (Australia) due west of Alice Springs and 1234 km south of Darwin. It extends along the MacDonnell Ranges west of Alice Springs. The popular extended walk, the Larapinta Trail, runs east–west along the linear park, following the West MacDonnell Ranges. The park includes many tourist attractions along its 250 kilometre length including Ormiston Pound, the Ellery Creek Bighole, Glen Helen Gorge, Simpsons Gap, Standley Chasm, Mount Sonder, Serpentine Gorge, the Ochre Pits and Redbank Gorge. The Park is known as Tjoritja by the traditional owners of the land and is considered of great significance in the local Arrernte Aboriginal culture. It is home to several species of flora and fauna and is now utilised by people for a variety of recreational activities. Facilities at the park include swimming, camping, gas BBQ, bushwalking, caravan sites, etc. See also Protected areas of the Northern Territory References External links Official fact sheet and map National parks of the Northern Territory Protected areas established in 1992 1992 establishments in Australia

original_text: "Debout Congolais" (; ) is the national anthem of the Democratic Republic of the Congo. original_text: we construct in this article a new realization of quantum geometry , which is obtained by quantizing the recently - introduced flux formulation of loop quantum gravity . in this framework , the vacuum is peaked on flat connections , and states are built upon it by creating local curvature excitations . the inner product induces a discrete topology on the gauge group , which turns out to be an essential ingredient for the construction of a continuum limit hilbert space . this leads to a representation of the full holonomy - flux algebra of loop quantum gravity which is unitarily - inequivalent to the one based on the ashtekar isham lewandowski vacuum . it therefore provides a new notion of quantum geometry . we discuss how the spectra of geometric operators , including holonomy and area operators , are affected by this new quantization . in particular , we find that the area operator is bounded , and that there are two different ways in which the barbero immirzi parameter can be taken into account . the methods introduced in this work open up new possibilities for investigating further realizations of quantum geometry based on different vacua .

In this way, the vacuum is peaked on flat connections. States are built on it. They make local curvature excitations.

The inner product makes a discrete topology. This is on the gauge group. It is important to make a continuum limit Hilbert space.

This shows the holonomy-flux algebra of loop quantum gravity. It is not like the old one. The old one used the Ashtekar Isham Lewandowski vacuum.

So, it is a new quantum geometry.

We talk about how new quantization changes geometric operators. This includes holonomy and area operators.

The area operator is bounded. There are two ways to use the Barbero Immirzi parameter.

Our new ways help find more quantum geometry. These can use different vacua.
simplified_primary_school: In this paper, we made a new way to understand quantum geometry. We got it by making a new idea called the "flux way" of loop quantum gravity into a quantum theory. In this way, empty space is mostly flat. We can make other states by adding small curves or bumps to this flat space. A special math tool makes certain math groups have separate points, not smooth ones. This is very important for making a special math space that can describe things that look smooth, even if they are made of tiny pieces. This gives us a new way to show the math rules for "holonomy" and "flux" in loop quantum gravity. This new way is different from the usual way, which uses a different idea of empty space. So, it gives us a new idea of quantum geometry. We talk about how this new quantum way changes the possible values for things that measure geometry, like "holonomy" and "area." We found that the tool that measures area has a limit. Also, there are two different ways to use a special number called the "Barbero Immirzi parameter." The ways we used in this paper make it possible to look into other ways to understand quantum geometry, using different ideas of empty space.
simplified_secondary_school: This article introduces a new way to understand quantum geometry. We achieve this by applying quantum principles to the recently developed 'flux formulation' of loop quantum gravity. In this framework, the fundamental 'empty space' (vacuum) is centered on flat connections. Other states are then built by adding localized 'excitations' that represent curvature in space. The mathematical tool used to measure similarities between states (the inner product) gives the 'gauge group' a discrete structure. This discrete structure is vital for creating a 'Hilbert space' that can describe a continuous space, even though it's built from discrete parts. This approach provides a way to represent the complete 'holonomy-flux algebra' of loop quantum gravity. This representation is fundamentally different (unitarily inequivalent) from the one based on the traditional 'Ashtekar-Isham-Lewandowski vacuum'. Therefore, this work offers a new understanding of quantum geometry. We also explore how this new quantization affects the possible measured values (spectra) of geometric operators, including those for holonomy and area. Specifically, we find that the area operator has a maximum possible value, and there are two distinct ways to include the 'Barbero-Immirzi parameter'. The methods presented in this work open new avenues for exploring other versions of quantum geometry that are based on different 'empty space' (vacuum) states.

original_text: we develop a new general formulation to explore light - driven electron transport through a single - molecule device with multiple pathways . three individual systems are proposed including ( i ) a two - terminal molecular junction based on phenyl - acetylene macrocycle ( pam ) , ( ii ) pam with three terminals , and ( iii ) a parallel molecular circuit . the computations show that pam - based optoelectronic switches have robust large on - off ratios and weak - field operating conditions , which are not sensitive to asymmetric molecule - lead couplings . in addition , field - amplitude power laws for one- and two - photon assisted tunneling are evident in the computational results , and the laws can be proven by using perturbation analysis . for pam - based optoelectronic routers , we show that it is possible to manipulate the direction of electric current through the pam molecule by using a weak linearly polarized laser field . for parallel circuits made of molecular quantum dots , the condition of coherent destruction of tunneling is derived by using the rotating wave approximation and the high - frequency approximation .

We make a new way to see.
It shows how light moves tiny bits.
The tiny bits go through one tiny thing.
This tiny thing has many paths.

We show three kinds of tiny things.

Another is a PAM. It has three ends.
The last is a path for tiny bits. It goes side-by-side.

Our math shows this.
PAM can be a light switch.
It turns on and off very well.
It works with a small light push.
It does not care if the ends are not the same.

Also, we see rules.
These rules show how light helps tiny bits jump.
This happens with one light bit or two light bits.
We can show these rules are true with more math.

PAM can also guide light.
We show we can change where the tiny bits go.
They go through the PAM.
We use a small, straight light beam to do this.

For paths that go side-by-side.
These paths are made of tiny dots.
We find out when tiny bits stop jumping.
We use special math to find this out.
simplified_primary_school:
We found a new way to understand how light makes tiny bits called electrons move through a very small thing made of just one molecule. The electrons can go in many ways.

We looked at three different kinds of setups. One was a PAM molecule with two ends. Another was a PAM molecule with three ends. The third was a parallel path made of molecules.

Our numbers show that switches made from PAM that use light and electricity can turn on and off very strongly. They work with only a little bit of light. This works even if the molecule is not perfectly connected to its wires.

Also, our numbers clearly showed rules about how strong the light is for electrons jumping with one or two light particles. We can show these rules are true by looking closely at small changes.

For PAM routers that use light and electricity, we showed we can change where the electricity goes through the PAM molecule. We do this by using a weak laser light that shines in one direction.

For parallel paths made of very tiny molecule dots, we found out when electrons stop jumping in a special way. We did this by using two special ways of thinking about waves: the rotating wave idea and the high-speed wave idea.
simplified_secondary_school:
We developed a new general method to study how light makes electrons move through a single-molecule device that has multiple pathways. We looked at three specific setups: (i) a molecular connection with two ends, built using a molecule called phenyl-acetylene macrocycle (PAM); (ii) the same PAM molecule, but with three ends; and (iii) a molecular circuit where different parts are connected side-by-side.

Our calculations show that switches made from PAM, which use light and electricity, are very effective. They have strong "on-off" ratios (meaning a big difference between the on and off states) and work well even with weak light fields. Their performance isn't affected if the connection between the molecule and the leads (wires) is uneven.

Additionally, our results clearly show how the strength of the light field affects electron movement when one or two light particles (photons) help the electrons "tunnel" through a barrier. These relationships, called field-amplitude power laws, can be mathematically proven using a method called perturbation analysis.

For devices that direct electrical signals (like routers) made with PAM, we demonstrated that a weak laser beam, polarized in a straight line, can control the direction of electric current flowing through the PAM molecule.

Finally, for parallel circuits built from tiny molecular "quantum dots," we determined the specific conditions under which electrons stop tunneling altogether (a phenomenon called "coherent destruction of tunneling"). We found these conditions by using two mathematical simplifications: the rotating wave approximation and the high-frequency approximation.

original_text: Telesto is a moon of Saturn. It was discovered by Smith, Reitsema, Larson and Fountain in 1980 from ground-based observations, and was provisionally designated . In the following months, several other apparitions were observed: , , and . In 1983 it was officially named after Telesto of Greek mythology. It is also designated as or Tethys B. Telesto is co-orbital with Tethys, residing in Tethys' leading Lagrangian point (). This relationship was first identified by Seidelmann et al. in 1981. Another moon, Calypso, resides in the other (trailing) Lagrangian point of Tethys, 60 degrees in the other direction from Tethys. The Saturnian system has two additional trojan moons. Exploration The Cassini probe performed a distant flyby of Telesto on October 11, 2005. The resulting images show that its surface is surprisingly smooth, devoid of small impact craters. simplified_primary_school:
Telesto is a moon that goes around the planet Saturn.

Four people named Smith, Reitsema, Larson, and Fountain found Telesto in 1980. They saw it from Earth using telescopes. At first, they gave it a temporary name: S/1980 S 13. Later that year, people saw it again many times. They called these sightings S/1980 S 3, S/1980 S 9, and S/1980 S 10.

In 1983, it got its official name, Telesto. This name comes from an old Greek story. People also call it Saturn XIII or Tethys B.

Telesto shares its path around Saturn with another moon called Tethys. Telesto stays in a special spot in front of Tethys. This special spot is called Tethys' leading Lagrangian point (L4). Seidelmann and his team first figured this out in 1981. Another moon, Calypso, stays in a different special spot behind Tethys. This spot is called the trailing Lagrangian point. It is 60 degrees away from Tethys in the other direction. Saturn has two more moons like Telesto and Calypso, which are called trojan moons.

A space probe called Cassini flew past Telesto on October 11, 2005. It did not get very close. Pictures from Cassini showed that Telesto's surface is very smooth. It does not have many small holes made by rocks hitting it.
simplified_secondary_school:
Telesto is one of Saturn's moons. It was discovered in 1980 by Smith, Reitsema, Larson, and Fountain, who observed it from Earth using telescopes. At first, it was given the temporary name . Over the next few months, it was seen several more times, and these sightings were labeled , , and . In 1983, it was officially named Telesto, after a figure from Greek mythology. It is also known by the names or Tethys B.

Telesto shares an orbit with another moon, Tethys. It is located at Tethys' leading Lagrangian point, also known as . This special orbital relationship was first recognized by Seidelmann and his team in 1981. Another moon, Calypso, is located at Tethys' other Lagrangian point, which trails Tethys and is 60 degrees away in the opposite direction. The Saturn system has two more moons that are also considered 'trojan moons'.

Regarding exploration, the Cassini probe flew past Telesto on October 11, 2005, though it was a distant pass. Images taken during this flyby revealed that Telesto's surface is surprisingly smooth and lacks small impact craters.

original_text: In Mesopotamian myths, the alal was a kind of demon that, to tempt men, came out of the Underworld and took various forms, temptations that the inhabitants of Babylonia were able to reject by means of amulets. The Chaldean-Assyrian art represents these spirits in the form of horrible monsters, as in the bas-reliefs of the Palace of Ashurbanipal in Nineveh (now Iraq), today in the British Museum) and in small bronzes and clay tablets cooked in the shape of a cylinder, cone, or stamp. Generally these demons are seen as theriocephalou, with a human body and the head of a lion with open jaws, the ears of a dog and mane of a horse. The feet are frequently replaced by bird claws of prey. The goddess to which the alal obeyed was called Alat, and was the wife of Nergal, god of war, and sister of Astarte. References See also Pazuzu Lamashtu Mesopotamian mythology Demons
simplified_kindergarten:
Long ago, people told stories. They said the alal was a bad monster. It came from the world below. It wanted to trick people. It could look like many things. But people used special charms to stop it.

Old pictures show these monsters. They look very scary. You can see them on old wall carvings. You can also see them on small metal things. And on hard clay shapes. Some clay shapes look like tubes. Some look like pointy hats. Some look like stamps.

Most times, these monsters look like this: They have a body like a person. But they have a lion's head. Its mouth is open wide. They have dog ears. And hair like a horse. Their feet are like a bird's sharp claws.

The alal monster listened to a goddess. Her name was Alat. Alat was married to Nergal. Nergal was the god of war. Alat was also Astarte's sister.
simplified_primary_school:
Long ago, in stories from a place called Mesopotamia, there was a bad spirit called an alal. It wanted to trick people. It came from a dark place called the Underworld and could look like many different things. But people in Babylonia could stop the alal's tricks by using special charms called amulets.

Art from Chaldea and Assyria shows these spirits as scary monsters. You can see them in stone carvings from King Ashurbanipal's palace in Nineveh, which is now in Iraq. These carvings are now in the British Museum. The alal spirits are also shown on small metal statues and on baked clay pieces shaped like tubes, cones, or stamps.

Most of the time, these bad spirits look like a mix of an animal and a human. They have a human body, but a lion's head with its mouth open. They also have dog ears and hair like a horse's mane. Often, their feet are like the sharp claws of a bird that hunts other animals.

The alal spirits listened to a goddess named Alat. She was married to Nergal, who was the god of war. And she was Astarte's sister.
simplified_secondary_school:
In ancient Mesopotamian myths, the alal was a type of demon. These demons came from the Underworld and took different forms to tempt people. However, the people of Babylonia could ward off these temptations by using special amulets.

Chaldean-Assyrian art shows these spirits as terrifying monsters. You can see them in the bas-reliefs from the Palace of Ashurbanipal in Nineveh (a city in modern-day Iraq, with some art now in the British Museum). They also appear on small bronze figures and on baked clay tablets shaped like cylinders, cones, or stamps.

These demons are usually depicted as "theriocephalous," meaning they have a human body but an animal head. Specifically, they often have the head of a lion with open jaws, the ears of a dog, and the mane of a horse. Their feet are often replaced by the claws of a bird of prey.

The alal demons obeyed a goddess named Alat. She was the wife of Nergal, who was the god of war, and the sister of Astarte.

original_text: a growing number of observations reveal a subset of type ia supernovae undergoing circumstellar interaction ( sne ia - csm ) . we present unpublished archival @xmath0 data on sne ia - csm 2002ic and 2005gj obtained @xmath1 and 500 days post - discovery , respectively . both sne show evidence for late - time mid - infrared ( mid - ir ) emission from warm dust . the dust parameters are most consistent with a pre - existing dust shell that lies beyond the forward - shock radius , most likely radiatively heated by optical and x - ray emission continuously generated by late - time csm interaction . in the case of sn 2005gj , the mid - ir luminosity more than doubles after 1 year post - discovery . while we are not aware of any late - time optical - wavelength observations at these epochs , we attribute this rebrightening to renewed shock interaction with a dense circumstellar shell . +
simplified_kindergarten:
We see more and more big star explosions. Some of these explosions hit things around them. We call them SNe Ia-CSM.

We show old pictures of two explosions. They are called 2002ic and 2005gj. We took these pictures 200 days and 500 days after we found them.

Both explosions show a kind of light we cannot see. This light comes from warm dust. We see this light a long time after the explosions.

The dust looks like a cloud that was already there. It was outside the first big hit. Bright light and X-ray light made the dust warm. This light came from the explosion hitting things around it for a long time.

For explosion 2005gj, the warm dust light got much brighter. It was two times brighter after one year.

We did not see bright light from it then. We think it got brighter again because it hit a new, thick cloud of stuff around it.
simplified_primary_school: More and more, we see that some star explosions, called Type Ia supernovae, hit things around them. We call these "SNe Ia-CSM." We are showing past information that no one has shown before about two of these explosions, called SN 2002ic and SN 2005gj. We got this information at different times after we first found them. Both explosions show signs of warm dust giving off a special kind of light, like heat, a long time after they exploded. This dust seems to be an old cloud of dust that was already there, outside the first big push of the explosion. It probably got warm from the light we can see and X-ray light that kept coming from the explosion hitting things around it a long time later. For SN 2005gj, this special heat-light got more than twice as bright after one year from when we first saw it. We don't know if anyone looked at the normal light from these explosions at those times. But we think it got brighter again because the explosion hit a thick cloud of stuff around the star once more.
simplified_secondary_school: More and more observations show that some Type Ia supernovae (SNe Ia-CSM) interact with material surrounding them. We present new, previously unreleased mid-infrared data on SNe Ia-CSM 2002ic and 2005gj, collected 300 and 500 days after their initial discovery, respectively. Both supernovae show signs of mid-infrared (mid-IR) light coming from warm dust a long time after the explosion. The characteristics of this dust best match a pre-existing shell of dust located beyond the expanding shockwave. This dust was most likely heated by the optical and X-ray light continuously produced by the ongoing interaction between the supernova and the surrounding material. For SN 2005gj, the mid-IR brightness more than doubles over a year after its discovery. Although we don't have any observations of visible light from these times, we believe this increase in brightness is due to the shockwave hitting a dense shell of material around the star again.

original_text: we study a particle propagation on a circle in the presence of a point interaction . we show that the one - particle feynman kernel can be written into the sum of reflected and transmitted trajectories which are weighted by the elements of the @xmath0-th power of the scattering matrix evaluated on a line with a point interaction . as a by - product we find three - parameter family of trace formulae as a generalization of the poisson summation formula .
simplified_kindergarten:
We look at a tiny thing. It moves around a circle. There is a special spot.
We found out how the tiny thing moves. It is like adding up all the paths it can take. Some paths bounce back. Some paths go through. Each path has a special number. These numbers come from a math rule. This rule tells us how things bounce. It is for a straight line with a spot.
We also found new math rules. There are many of them. They are like a bigger version of an old math rule.
simplified_primary_school:
We look at how a tiny thing moves around a circle. There is a special spot on the circle that changes how it moves.
We found that how the tiny thing moves can be understood by adding up all the different paths it could take. Some paths bounce back, and some go through. Each path is given a special importance based on how the tiny thing changes direction at a special spot, like when it moves on a straight line.
Also, we found some new math rules. These rules are like a bigger version of a known math rule called the Poisson summation formula.
simplified_secondary_school: This research examines how a particle moves on a circle when it encounters a single point of interaction. We demonstrate that the Feynman kernel for a single particle can be expressed as the sum of its reflected and transmitted paths. Each of these paths is assigned a weight based on elements from the @xmath0-th power of the scattering matrix, which is calculated for a point interaction on a straight line. As a secondary outcome, we identified a family of trace formulas that depend on three parameters. These formulas serve as a generalization of the Poisson summation formula.

original_text: we present measurements of the frequency and electric field dependent conductivity of single walled carbon nanotube(swcnt ) networks of various densities . the ac conductivity as a function of frequency is consistent with the extended pair approximation model and increases with frequency above an onset frequency @xmath0 which varies over seven decades with a range of film thickness from sub - monolayer to 200 nm . the nonlinear electric field - dependent dc conductivity shows strong dependence on film thickness as well . measurement of the electric field dependence of the resistance r(e ) allows for the determination of a length scale @xmath1 possibly characterizing the distance between tube contacts , which is found to systematically decrease with increasing film thickness . the onset frequency @xmath0 of ac conductivity and the length scale @xmath1 of swcnt networks are found to be correlated , and a physically reasonable empirical formula relating them has been proposed . such studies will help the understanding of transport properties and benefit the applications of this material system .
simplified_kindergarten:
We looked at how electricity moves. It moves in tiny tubes. These tubes are called SWCNTs. They make a net. Some nets are thick. Some nets are thin. We saw how electricity moved when we changed how fast it wiggled. We also saw how it moved when we changed how strong we pushed it.

When electricity wiggles, it moves better if it wiggles faster. This starts after a special speed. We call this speed @xmath0. This speed @xmath0 changes a lot. It changes with how thick the net is. The net can be very thin. Or it can be thicker.

When electricity does not wiggle, it also moves in a special way. It changes a lot with how strong we push it. This also changes a lot with how thick the net is.

We measured how hard it is for electricity to move. This changes with how strong we push. This helps us find a special length. We call this length @xmath1. This length might be how far apart the tubes touch. This length @xmath1 gets smaller when the net gets thicker.

The special speed @xmath0 and the special length @xmath1 are linked. We found a good math rule that links them.

This work helps us know more about how electricity moves. This helps us use these tubes for new things.
simplified_primary_school:
We looked at how electricity moves through special tiny tubes called carbon nanotubes. We checked how this changes with how fast the electricity wiggles (frequency) and how strong the electricity is (electric field). We looked at different amounts of these tubes.

When the electricity wiggles (AC), it moves better as it wiggles faster. This starts happening after a certain wiggle speed, let's call it the "start speed." This "start speed" changes a lot, depending on how thick the layer of tubes is. The layers can be very, very thin or up to 200 nanometers thick. This matches a science idea called the "extended pair approximation model."

When the electricity flows steadily (DC), how well it moves also depends a lot on how thick the layer of tubes is, and it's not a simple straight line change.

By checking how hard it is for electricity to move (resistance) when the electric field changes, we can find a special distance. This distance might be how far apart the tubes touch each other. We found that this distance gets smaller as the layer of tubes gets thicker.

We saw that the "start speed" for wiggling electricity and the special distance between the tubes are connected. We even came up with a simple math rule that shows how they are linked.

Learning these things helps us understand how electricity moves through these tubes. This can help us use these tubes for many new things.
simplified_secondary_school:
We measured how the conductivity of single-walled carbon nanotube (SWCNT) networks changes based on their density, the frequency of the electric current, and the strength of the electric field. The alternating current (AC) conductivity changes with frequency in a way that matches the "extended pair approximation model." This conductivity increases as the frequency goes up, but only above a certain "onset frequency." This onset frequency changes significantly (over seven orders of magnitude) depending on how thick the SWCNT film is, ranging from less than a single layer to 200 nanometers thick. Similarly, the direct current (DC) conductivity, which also depends on the electric field in a complex way, is strongly affected by the film's thickness.

By measuring how resistance (R) changes with the electric field (E), we can determine a specific "length scale." This length scale likely represents the average distance between where the nanotubes touch each other. We found that this length scale consistently gets smaller as the film gets thicker. We discovered a connection between the onset frequency for AC conductivity and this length scale in the SWCNT networks, and we've suggested a practical formula that links these two values. These findings will help us better understand how electricity moves through these materials (their "transport properties") and will be useful for developing new applications for SWCNT networks.

original_text: in this paper we present a new approach to control variates for improving computational efficiency of ensemble monte carlo . we present the approach using simulation of paths of a time - dependent nonlinear stochastic equation . the core idea is to extract information at one or more nominal model parameters and use this information to gain estimation efficiency at neighboring parameters . this idea is the basis of a general strategy , called database monte carlo ( dbmc ) , for improving efficiency of monte carlo . in this paper we describe how this strategy can be implemented using the variance reduction technique of control variates ( cv ) . we show that , once an initial setup cost for extracting information is incurred , this approach can lead to significant gains in computational efficiency . the initial setup cost is justified in projects that require a large number of estimations or in those that are to be performed under real - time constraints . monte carlo , variance reduction , control variates s05.10.ln , 02.70.uu , 02.70.tt
simplified_kindergarten:
We show a new way for control variates. This helps ensemble Monte Carlo math go faster.
We show this new way by using pretend paths. These paths are for a math problem. This math problem changes with time. It has random parts.
The main idea is to get facts from some math numbers. These are our starting numbers. We use these facts. This helps us guess faster for other numbers that are close.
This idea helps make a big plan. It is called Database Monte Carlo (DBMC). It makes Monte Carlo math faster.
We show how to do this plan. We use control variates (CV). This helps make numbers less spread out.
We show there is a first cost. This cost is to get facts. After we pay this cost, our way makes math much faster.
The first cost is worth it. This is for jobs that need many, many guesses. Or for jobs that must be done very, very fast.
simplified_primary_school: In this paper, we show a new way to use a trick called 'control variates'. This helps computers work faster when they do something called 'ensemble Monte Carlo'. We show how this works by pretending to follow paths of a special math problem. This problem changes over time, is not simple, and has some random parts. The main idea is to get information from one or a few basic settings of our model. Then, we use what we learned to guess things faster and better for numbers that are close by. This idea helps a bigger plan called 'Database Monte Carlo' (DBMC). This plan makes Monte Carlo work better and faster. In this paper, we explain how this plan can be done using a trick called 'control variates' (CV). This trick helps make our results less spread out. We show that if we spend some time and effort at the start to get information, this way can make computers do the work much faster. Spending time at the start is worth it for big projects that need many guesses, or when we need to get answers very, very quickly.
simplified_secondary_school:
In this paper, we introduce a new method that uses "control variates" to make "ensemble Monte Carlo" calculations much faster. We show how this method works by simulating the paths of a complex equation that changes over time and involves randomness.

The main idea is to gather information from a few specific settings (called "nominal model parameters"). We then use this information to make estimations faster for similar settings nearby. This core idea is the basis of a broader strategy called "Database Monte Carlo" (DBMC), which aims to improve the overall efficiency of Monte Carlo simulations.

We explain how to implement this DBMC strategy using a technique called "control variates" (CV), which helps reduce the variability in results. We show that after an initial investment of time and effort to gather the necessary information, this approach can significantly speed up calculations. This initial cost is worthwhile for projects that require a large number of estimations or those that must be completed very quickly, such as in real-time situations.

original_text: Paper-and-pencil games or paper-and-pen games (or some variation on those terms) are games that can be played solely with paper and pencils (or other writing implements), usually without erasing. Some popular examples of pencil-and-paper games include Tic-tac-toe, Sprouts, Dots and Boxes, Hangman, M.A.S.H., Paper soccer, and Spellbinder. The term is unrelated to the use in role-playing games to differentiate tabletop games from role-playing video games. Board games where pieces are never moved or removed from the board once being played, particularly abstract strategy games like Gomoku and Connect Four, can also be played as pencil-and-paper games. External links A Zen Guide to Paper and Pen Games Pencil and Paper Games
simplified_kindergarten:
These are games. You play them with paper and pencils. Or pens. You usually do not erase.

Some games are Tic-tac-toe. Sprouts is one. Dots and Boxes. Hangman. M.A.S.H. Paper soccer. Spellbinder.

This name is not for other games. Not for games where you pretend. Not for video games.

Some board games are like this. You put pieces down. You do not move them. You do not take them off. Like Gomoku. Or Connect Four. You can play these with paper and pencils too.
simplified_primary_school: Paper-and-pencil games are games you play with just paper and a pencil or pen. You usually don't erase when you play. Some well-known paper-and-pencil games are Tic-tac-toe, Sprouts, Dots and Boxes, Hangman, M.A.S.H., Paper soccer, and Spellbinder. The name "paper-and-pencil games" is not used for role-playing games. In role-playing games, people use "paper-and-pencil" to talk about games played on a table, not on a computer. Some board games, where you put pieces down and don't move them, can also be played with paper and pencil. These include thinking games like Gomoku and Connect Four.
simplified_secondary_school:
Paper-and-pencil games (also known as paper-and-pen games) are games played using only paper and pencils or other writing tools, usually without erasing. Popular examples include Tic-tac-toe, Sprouts, Dots and Boxes, Hangman, M.A.S.H., Paper soccer, and Spellbinder.

It's important to note that this term is not related to how "paper-and-pencil" is used in role-playing games to distinguish physical tabletop games from role-playing video games.

Some board games can also be played as paper-and-pencil games. This applies particularly to abstract strategy games like Gomoku and Connect Four, where pieces, once placed, are never moved or removed from the board.

original_text: In Norse mythology Mundilfari (Old Norse: ; rendered variously Mundilfari, Mundilföri and Mundilfœri) (Old Norse, possibly "the one moving according to particular times") is the father of Sól, goddess associated with the Sun, and Máni, associated with the Moon. Mundilfari is attested in the Poetic Edda poem Vafþrúðnismál stanza 23, and in chapter 11 of the Prose Edda book Gylfaginning. Etymology The name appears in various forms in attestations for the figure, some of them significantly different, and various theories have been proposed for the name. John Lindow states that if the first element, mundil- is related to mund, meaning "period of time," then the name may be a kenning for the Moon, as Rudolf Simek theorizes. See also Hyperion (Titan) World Mill Notes References Lindow, John (2001). Norse Mythology: A Guide to the Gods, Heroes, Rituals, and Beliefs. Oxford University Press. Simek, Rudolf (2007) translated by Angela Hall. Dictionary of Northern Mythology. D.S. Brewer Characters in Norse mythology

A man named John Lindow says part of his name means "time."

simplified_primary_school:
In old Norse stories, there is a person named Mundilfari. His name was sometimes written as Mundilföri or Mundilfœri. His name might mean 'the one who moves at certain times'. He is the dad of Sól, who is the goddess of the Sun, and Máni, who is linked to the Moon.

We learn about Mundilfari in an old poem called Vafþrúðnismál, in part 23. He is also in an old book called Gylfaginning, in chapter 11.

People have different ideas about what his name means. One idea is that the first part of his name, 'mundil-', might be linked to the word 'mund', which means 'a period of time'. If this is true, then his name could be a special way to talk about the Moon. John Lindow and Rudolf Simek thought this.
simplified_secondary_school:
In Norse mythology, Mundilfari is the father of Sól, the goddess of the Sun, and Máni, the god of the Moon. His name appears in various forms, including Mundilföri and Mundilfœri, and may mean "the one moving according to particular times" in Old Norse.

Mundilfari is mentioned in the Poetic Edda, specifically in stanza 23 of the poem *Vafþrúðnismál*, and in chapter 11 of the Prose Edda book *Gylfaginning*.

The origin of Mundilfari's name is debated due to its significantly different forms in various texts. John Lindow suggests that if the first part of the name, "mundil-," is related to "mund" (meaning "period of time"), then the name could be a kenning, or poetic description, for the Moon. Rudolf Simek also supports this theory.

People still use this idea today to plan science tests. They also use it to check if what they find in tests matches the idea.

A very successful model, proposed thirty years ago by Field and Feynman, describes how initial quarks produce mesons in jets. This hadronization model is still used today for designing experiments and comparing with experimental data.

This study shows that the model's correlation function, which depends on the energies of two final particles (hadrons), has interesting properties. These energies are expressed as fractions of the initial quark's energy, labeled as @xmath0 and @xmath1.

Specifically, for some pairs of mesons, there are specific ranges of the @xmath1 variable (when @xmath0 is kept constant) where the correlation function is positive and surprisingly large.

Experimentally investigating these correlation functions in these specific regions is valuable for two reasons. First, it can help us understand subtle characteristics of quark jets. Second, it can help confirm the model's underlying physical assumptions and lead to more accurate values for its parameters.

original_text: A drawbridge mentality is the attitude of people who have migrated to a more exclusive or more "unspoilt" community and then campaign to preserve the tranquility of that community by opposing further inward migration by people or businesses and, possibly, any development or refurbishment, including plans put forward by those already located there. The term can imply a selfish attitude and can be taken as an insult by people who have strong affection for their home locality and wish to protect it from any changes. It is closely related to the NIMBY attitude. A drawbridge was historically the hinged bridge at a castle's gates providing entry across a defensive moat. Raising the drawbridge to a vertical position was therefore one's means by which intruders could be shut out of the castle. See also BANANA Isolationism I'm alright, Jack Lifeboat ethics Portcullis Precommitment Siege mentality Splendid Isolation Tragedy of the commons References Sustainability Political terminology

It helped people cross water to get in.
When the bridge went up high, it kept bad people out.

Then they try to keep that place quiet and peaceful. They do this by saying no to more people or shops moving in. Sometimes, they also say no to new buildings or fixing old ones, even if people who already live there want to do it. This idea can mean someone is being selfish. People who love their home and want to keep it safe from changes might feel hurt by this idea. It is a lot like the "NIMBY" way of thinking. A drawbridge was an old bridge that could move up and down at a castle's gate. It helped people get over the water around the castle. See also BANANA Isolationism I'm alright, Jack Lifeboat ethics Portcullis Precommitment Siege mentality Splendid Isolation Tragedy of the commons References Sustainability Political terminology
simplified_secondary_school:
A drawbridge mentality describes the attitude of people who have moved to a more exclusive or untouched community. Once settled, they then campaign to keep that community peaceful by opposing any further new residents or businesses. They might also oppose new construction or improvements, even if proposed by people already living there.

This term can suggest a selfish attitude. People who deeply care for their home area and want to protect it from changes might find this term insulting.

It is closely related to the NIMBY attitude.

Historically, a drawbridge was a hinged bridge at a castle's entrance, allowing passage over a defensive moat. Raising the drawbridge vertically was a way to shut out intruders from the castle. The term "drawbridge mentality" uses this idea to describe keeping others out of a community.

See also: BANANA, Isolationism, I'm alright, Jack, Lifeboat ethics, Portcullis, Precommitment, Siege mentality, Splendid Isolation, Tragedy of the commons.

simplified_kindergarten:
"Audio compression" can mean two things.

One is "audio compression (data)".
This makes sound files smaller.
It helps send sound.
Sometimes the sound stays the same.
Sometimes the sound changes a little.
We use it for music on CDs.
We use it for MP3 songs.
We use it for internet radio.

simplified_primary_school:
Audio compression can mean two different things.

One kind of audio compression makes sound files smaller. This helps send sounds, like music, on CDs, MP3s, or internet radio. Sometimes, when the file gets smaller, a little bit of the sound quality is lost. Other times, no sound quality is lost.

The other kind of audio compression changes how loud or quiet a sound is. It makes the very loud parts not so loud, and the very quiet parts a bit louder. This makes the sound more even. People also call this 'audio level compression'.
simplified_secondary_school:
Audio compression can refer to two different processes.

First, there's **audio data compression**. This process reduces the amount of digital information in a recorded sound wave. It can be **lossy**, meaning some quality is lost to make the file much smaller, or **lossless**, meaning no quality is lost but the file is still reduced. This type of compression is used for things like CD and MP3 encoding, and Internet radio, to make audio easier to transmit or store.

Second, there's **dynamic range compression**, also known as **audio level compression**. This reduces the **dynamic range** of an audio waveform. The dynamic range is the difference between its loudest and quietest parts.

These are notes for a class. The class was at the 18th Brazilian School of Probability. It happened from August 3 to 9, 2014, in Mambucaba.

We show how to solve these problems using two main ways. One way is called "energy solutions." The other way is called "paracontrolled distributions." This other way was found not long ago.

simplified_secondary_school: These notes are from a course taught at the 18th Brazilian School of Probability, which took place in Mambucaba from August 3rd to 9th, 2014. The course aims to introduce the fundamental challenges of nonlinear Partial Differential Equations (PDEs) that include random (stochastic) and irregular elements. How long a password is helps us understand the chance that someone might guess it. This chance depends on a few things:
- How long a password can be used before it needs to be changed.
- How many guesses bad people can try in a short time.

How safe a password is depends on the chance that someone might guess it while it's still being used.
simplified_secondary_school:
In telecommunications, the length of a password is a basic factor that directly affects how strong a password is against a "brute force attack." This type of attack involves trying many different password combinations until the correct one is found. Therefore, password length is very important for overall computer security.

One way password length is used is in a calculation that helps determine how likely a password is to be guessed during its active use. This calculation considers several elements:
*   **P:** The chance that a password can be guessed while it is still valid.
*   **L:** The longest time a password can be used to log into a system.
*   **R:** The number of guesses an attacker can make per unit of time.
*   **N:** The total number of unique passwords that could possibly be created (also called the 'password space').

Ultimately, how secure a password is depends on the probability that it could be guessed during the time it is being used.

We looked at many different speeds, and we saw that things don't always change in a simple way. We used a special computer method to see how fast protons make electrons jump around. We used another special way to figure out some important math problems for a moving particle inside the copper, which is like a neat pattern of atoms. It also changes how the copper atoms pull on each other, whether the protons move in a clear path or a messy path. What we found for messy paths matched what others found in real tests. When the protons were slow, we saw something new: the way they slowed down changed in a special, complicated way at certain speeds. We conducted a fundamental study, based on basic physical principles, to understand the 'electronic stopping power' of protons in copper. This refers to how much energy protons lose as they move through the material at various speeds, and our study included complex 'non-linear' effects.

To investigate this, we used advanced computer simulations, combining 'time-dependent density functional theory' with 'molecular dynamics.' These methods allowed us to study the 'electronic excitations' (when electrons in the copper get energized) caused by the energetic protons. Specifically, we employed a 'plane-wave pseudopotential scheme' to solve the complex 'time-dependent Kohn-Sham equations,' which describe how a moving proton interacts within the regular, repeating structure of the copper crystal.

The way electrons get excited and the material's 'band structure' (the specific energy levels available to electrons) are crucial. They determine the stopping power and also change the forces between atoms, whether the protons are moving smoothly through open channels in the crystal ('channeling') or hitting atoms directly ('off-channeling').

Our calculations for off-channeling protons closely match experimental results. At low speeds, we discovered a new 'crossover region' where the stopping power increases much faster than expected with velocity (specifically, with a power of @xmath1) within the velocity range @xmath2. We believe this unusual behavior is directly related to the unique 'electronic band structure' of copper. We can explain these findings using simple models of electron energy bands, which help connect two different ways the protons interact with the material. Ultimately, we found that the theoretical 'limit of electronic stopping' (@xmath3) is more complicated than simpler models suggest, largely because of the significant influence of the material's 'band structure' effects.

simplified_primary_school: Donald Trump is a famous businessman and TV star. He made a lot of news because he said he might want to be president in 2012. But on Monday, he said he will stay a businessman instead. Trump said in a message that he thought about it a lot. He decided not to try to become president. Trump often speaks very confidently. He said he would have won the first election and the main election. But he also said that being president is a very big job. You cannot do it if you are not all in. The head of the Republican party said, "It's his choice." Trump said in his message that business is what he loves to do most. He is not ready to stop being a businessman. When Trump was thinking about running for president, he asked President Barack Obama to show his full birth paper from Hawaii. Another person, Mike Huckabee, also said he would not run. Before this, President Obama had shown a shorter birth paper. This was the official record in Hawaii. But many news people talked about it a lot. This was partly because Trump kept asking if a longer birth paper was real. So, last month, the president showed the longer paper.
simplified_secondary_school:
Donald Trump, a real estate mogul and television celebrity, announced Monday that he will not run for president in 2012. Instead, he will remain a businessman. Trump had generated a lot of media attention by suggesting he might seek the presidency.

In a statement, Trump said, "After considerable deliberation and reflection, I have decided not to pursue the office of the presidency." He explained that business is his greatest passion and he is not ready to leave the private sector.

With his typical bravado, Trump maintained that he would have won both the Republican primary and the general election. However, he recognized that "running for public office cannot be done half-heartedly."

Trump's flirtation with running for the Republican nomination included his demand that President Barack Obama make public his full birth certificate from Hawaii. Obama had previously provided a shorter version, which serves as the public record in Hawaii. Amid the media glare on this issue, raised partly by Trump's repeated questioning of whether the so-called long version of the document existed, the president released it last month.

original_text: Fionn (, ) is a masculine given name in Irish and Scottish Gaelic. It is derived from a byname meaning "white" or "fair-haired". original_text: By . Simeon Gholam . Follow @@SimGholam . Borussia Dortmund fans are well-known for their impressive vocal displays during matches, and they proved their reputation once again as they joined in a superb rendition of You'll Never Walk Alone at Anfield on Sunday. The German club were on Merseyside for a pre-season friendly, but the 1,000+ fans who made the journey were determined to make themselves heard. Having taken the Anfield anthem to their hearts in recent years, the noise before the game was sensational, and it was captured in the ground by Borussia Dortmund's official Instagram account. Unfortunately for the travelling fans, however, the result did not match the support as Dortmund went down 4-0 to a rampant Liverpool attack. Daniel Sturridge opened the scoring after 10 minutes, before Dejan Lovren netted from a corner just a few minutes later. Phillippe Coutinho and Jordan Henderson then added to the scoreline after half time, but it did not dampen the German fans spirits, who sang right through until the end.

But the fans were sad. Their team did not win. Liverpool scored 4 goals. Dortmund scored 0 goals. Liverpool played very well.

Daniel scored first. It was after 10 minutes. Dejan scored next. He scored from a corner kick. It was a few minutes later.

Phillippe and Jordan scored more goals. This was after half time. But the German fans were still happy. They sang until the game ended.

original_text: Robert Pacho (August 1, 1911 – May 1, 1978) was an American professional boxer who competed from 1928 to 1941, twice challenging for the welterweight world title in 1939. After Bert Colima's career was over, Pacho was Mexican fans' most popular boxer. Early life Pacho broke into the professional boxing ranks while working as a farm mechanic in El Centro, California. Professional career Pacho fought many well known fighters during his career, including legends Barney Ross, and Henry Armstrong. His career lasted from 1929–1941 and his professional record was 78-67-15 with 37 Knockouts. His two bouts with Henry Armstrong were for the world Welterweight title, one held in Havana, Cuba. Pacho lost both by fourth round technical knockout. References External links American boxers of Mexican descent Boxers from Arizona Welterweight boxers 1911 births 1978 deaths American male boxers

After another boxer named Bert Colima stopped boxing, Pacho became the most liked boxer by fans in Mexico.

When he was young, Pacho started boxing professionally. At that time, he worked as a mechanic on a farm in El Centro, California.

As a professional boxer, Pacho fought many famous boxers. These included very famous boxers like Barney Ross and Henry Armstrong. His professional record, from 1929 to 1941, was 78 wins, 67 losses, and 15 draws. He knocked out his opponents 37 times.

He fought Henry Armstrong two times. These fights were to win the world welterweight title. One of these fights happened in Havana, Cuba. Pacho lost both of these fights in the fourth round by a technical knockout.
simplified_secondary_school:
Robert Pacho (August 1, 1911 – May 1, 1978) was an American professional boxer who competed from 1929 to 1941. During his career, he twice challenged for the welterweight world title in 1939. After Bert Colima's career ended, Pacho became the most popular boxer among Mexican fans.

Pacho began his professional boxing career while working as a farm mechanic in El Centro, California. He fought many well-known opponents, including boxing legends Barney Ross and Henry Armstrong. His two world welterweight title bouts were both against Henry Armstrong, with one of these fights taking place in Havana, Cuba. Pacho lost both title challenges by a fourth-round technical knockout.

His professional record was 78 wins, 67 losses, and 15 draws, with 37 knockouts.

original_text: The New York Saints are a former member of the National Lacrosse League. They played at the Nassau Coliseum in Uniondale, New York, from 1989 to 2003. They became an inactive team after the 2002–03 season and were officially defunct in 2006. The Saints had played in the Major Indoor Lacrosse League from 1987 to 1988 in New Jersey (at Brendan Byrne Arena) as the New Jersey Saints. The Saints won the 1988 MILL championship. Before becoming the New York Saints, the team played as the New Jersey Saints in the Major Indoor Lacrosse League from 1987 to 1988. During that time, they played at the Brendan Byrne Arena in New Jersey and won the MILL championship in 1988.

original_text: In criminology the term situational offender is used in several meanings, their common denominator being nontypical character of the offense in question for the person according to some criteria. General criminology Following the classical study of Martin R. Haskell and Lewis Yablonsky Criminology - Crime and Criminality (1974), a situational offender, as opposed to a career criminal, is a person who committed a crime under certain circumstances, but normally is not inclined to commit crimes and is unlikely to repeat the offense. Sex crimes In sex crimes, a situational sex offender is one whose offense is associated with situational sexual behavior, i.e., sexual behavior different from the person's usual habits. This term is in an opposition to the preferential offender, whose offense is associated with the person's preferential behavior. For example, a preferential child molester is exclusively involved with children, whereas the situational ones are normally engaged in sexual behavior within their peer group. References Criminology
simplified_kindergarten:
A 'situational offender' is a name.
It means a person did a crime.
But this crime is not like them.
It is not what they usually do.

Some people talk about this name.
A situational offender is someone who did a crime.
They did it because of how things were.
They do not usually do bad things.
They are not like a person who does crimes all the time.
They will probably not do this crime again.

For some bad sex acts, there is a 'situational sex offender'.
This means their bad act happened because of how things were.
They did something with sex that is not their usual way.

This is different from a 'preferential offender'.
A preferential offender does bad acts that they like to do.
It is their usual way of doing things.

For example, a preferential offender who hurts children only hurts children.
But a situational offender usually does sex acts with people their own age.
simplified_primary_school:
In the study of crime, a "situational offender" is a person who does something wrong that they don't normally do. This means the bad act is not typical for them.

Experts who study crime, like Martin R. Haskell and Lewis Yablonsky, say that a situational offender is different from a "career criminal" (someone who commits crimes all the time). A situational offender commits a crime because of certain things that happen around them. But they usually do not want to commit crimes, and they are not likely to do it again.

When talking about sex crimes, a "situational sex offender" is someone whose crime involves sexual behavior that is not their usual habit. This is different from a "preferential offender." A preferential offender's crime involves what they usually prefer to do. For example, a preferential child molester only harms children. But a situational one usually has sexual behavior with people their own age.
simplified_secondary_school:
In criminology, the term "situational offender" describes a person who commits a crime that is not typical for them, based on certain criteria.

In general criminology, following a classic 1974 study by Martin R. Haskell and Lewis Yablonsky, a situational offender is different from a career criminal. This is someone who commits a crime because of specific circumstances, but they are not usually inclined to commit crimes and are unlikely to repeat the offense.

When discussing sex crimes, a "situational sex offender" is someone whose offense involves sexual behavior that is different from their usual habits. This is in contrast to a "preferential offender," whose crime is linked to their usual or preferred sexual behavior. For example, a preferential child molester is exclusively involved with children. However, a situational offender normally engages in sexual behavior within their peer group, meaning their offense involving children is not their typical pattern.

It has the following genera: Balaeniceps †Goliathia (might belong in Balaeniceps) †Paludiavis Common characteristics A sharp hooked tip to the upper beak A furrow in the upper beak below the nasal openings An ossified septum Vestigial or absent expansor secundariorum muscle References Bird families Taxa named by Charles Lucien Bonaparte

The shoebill is the only type of bird from this group that is alive today. Its closest family member is the hamerkop. The hamerkop is part of a different bird family.

This group includes different kinds of birds: Balaeniceps, Goliathia, and Paludiavis. Goliathia and Paludiavis are not alive anymore. Goliathia might be the same as Balaeniceps.

These birds all share some common features. They have a sharp, hooked tip on their top beak. There is a groove on their top beak, below their nose holes. They have a bony wall inside their nose. They also have a muscle that is either very small or not there at all.
simplified_secondary_school:
Balaenicipitidae is a family of birds that belongs to the order Pelecaniformes, though it was historically classified under Ciconiiformes. The shoebill is the only living species in this family. Its closest relative is the hamerkop (Scopus umbretta), even though the hamerkop belongs to a different family.

This family includes several genera: Balaeniceps, and two extinct genera, †Goliathia and †Paludiavis. There is some discussion among scientists about whether †Goliathia should actually be grouped within Balaeniceps.

Members of this family share several distinct characteristics:
*   A sharp, hooked tip on the upper beak.
*   A groove (furrow) in the upper beak, located below the nasal openings.
*   An ossified (bony) septum.
*   A very small or missing expansor secundariorum muscle.

*   Melisende, who was the Queen of Jerusalem. She ruled the Kingdom of Jerusalem herself. She lived from 1105 to 1161.
*   Melisende, a Viscountess from Châteaudun. She died before the year 1040.
*   Melisende from Arsuf. She was a French noblewoman. She was born before 1177.
*   Melisende from Lusignan. She was a Princess of Antioch. She lived from 1200 to after 1249.
*   Melisende from Montlhéry. She was the grandmother of the Queen Melisende of Jerusalem.
*   Melisende from Piquigny. She might have been the mother of a man named Godfrey de Saint-Omer. She lived a very long time ago, in the 1000s and 1100s.

simplified_secondary_school:
Melisende was a popular French name in the Middle Ages and can refer to several historical figures:

*   **Melisende (1105–1161):** The ruling Queen of the Kingdom of Jerusalem.
*   **Melisende, Viscountess of Châteaudun:** She died before 1040.
*   **Melisende of Arsuf:** A French noblewoman born before 1177.
*   **Melisende of Lusignan (1200–after 1249):** A Princess of Antioch.
*   **Melisende of Montlhéry:** Grandmother of Queen Melisende of Jerusalem.
*   **Melisende of Piquigny:** Possibly the mother of Godfrey de Saint-Omer (11th–12th century).

For other individuals with similar names, see Melisande and Millicent.

original_text: Anab (Anav) is a city mentioned in the Bible. It was one of the cities in the mountains of Judah from which Joshua expelled the Anakim. History Anav was located at the site of today's Khirbet Anab. It lies among the hills, south-south-west of Hebron, in the West Bank. Anab is mentioned in Joshua 11:21 -- "And Joshua came at that time, and cut off the Anakim from the hill-country, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill-country of Judah, and from all the hill-country of Israel; Joshua utterly destroyed them with their cities." -- Jewish Publication Society translation (1917). Later, Joshua 15:50 lists Anab as one of a number of sites incorporated into the hilly parts of the territory of the Tribe of Judah. References Book of Joshua Hebrew Bible cities Anakim Archaeological sites in the West Bank Tribe of Judah

The Bible talks about Anab in Joshua 11:21. It says Joshua came. He made the Anakim leave the hills. He made them leave Hebron, Debir, and Anab. He made them leave all the Judah hills and all the Israel hills. Joshua broke their cities. He made the Anakim go away.
Later, Joshua 15:50 talks about Anab. It says Anab was one of the places in the hills of the Judah Tribe.

Long ago, Anab was where a place called Khirbet Anab is now. It is among hills. It is south-south-west of Hebron, in a place called the West Bank.

The Bible book of Joshua, chapter 11, verse 21, mentions Anab. It says: "And Joshua came at that time, and cut off the Anakim from the hill-country, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill-country of Judah, and from all the hill-country of Israel; Joshua utterly destroyed them with their cities." This is from a Bible translation made in 1917.

Later, in Joshua chapter 15, verse 50, Anab is listed. It was one of the places that became part of the hilly land belonging to the Tribe of Judah.
simplified_secondary_school:
Anab (also known as Anav) is a city mentioned in the Bible. It is located in the hills of the West Bank, south-south-west of Hebron, at the site of what is now called Khirbet Anab.

Historically, Anab was one of the cities in the mountains of Judah from which Joshua drove out the Anakim. The Book of Joshua, chapter 11, verse 21, describes this event: "And Joshua came at that time, and cut off the Anakim from the hill-country, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill-country of Judah, and from all the hill-country of Israel; Joshua utterly destroyed them with their cities." This translation is from the Jewish Publication Society (1917).

Later, Joshua 15:50 lists Anab as one of several places that became part of the hilly region belonging to the Tribe of Judah.

Potassium bromide, an anticonvulsant and sedative (most pharmacologic information is here) Sodium bromide, an anticonvulsant and sedative In photography Bromide paper, paper coated with an emulsion of silver bromide, used primarily for photographic prints In Japanese culture Bromide (Japanese culture), commercial photographic portraits of celebrities including geisha, singers, actors and sports-people In geography Bromide, Oklahoma, a small village in the southern central part of the state. Potassium bromide is a chemical. It helps people feel calm. It stops bad shakes.
Sodium bromide is another chemical. It also helps people feel calm. It stops bad shakes.

For pictures, there is bromide paper. This paper has a special silver chemical on it. We use it to make photos.

simplified_primary_school:
Bromide can mean different things.

Sometimes, when we talk, 'Bromide' is a saying that means a calm phrase, a common saying, or something very obvious. People use these words to make someone feel calm or sleepy.

In science, when we talk about chemicals, 'Bromide' is a tiny part of a chemical called bromine. It can also be in a type of salt that has only bromide in it. Sometimes, it's a common name for other chemicals that have bromine in a special way.
Potassium bromide is a medicine that can stop fits and make people calm or sleepy.
Sodium bromide is also a medicine that can stop fits and make people calm or sleepy.

In taking pictures, 'Bromide paper' is paper covered with a special mix that has silver bromide in it. People use it mostly to print photos.

In Japan, 'Bromide' means special photos sold of famous people. These can be geisha, singers, actors, and sports stars.

In places on a map, there is a small village called Bromide in the middle-south part of Oklahoma. There is also a place called Bromide Basin in Utah.
simplified_secondary_school:
The word 'bromide' has several different meanings, depending on the context.

In language, a 'bromide' is a phrase or expression that is a cliché or a platitude, often used to calm someone down or avoid a difficult topic, acting like a verbal sedative.

In chemistry, 'bromide' refers to the negatively charged ion of bromine. It can also mean any ionic salt where bromide is the only negatively charged ion, or, more generally, any covalent compound where bromine has an oxidation state of -1. Two important chemical compounds are potassium bromide and sodium bromide, both of which are used as anticonvulsants (to prevent seizures) and sedatives (to calm or induce sleep).

In photography, 'bromide paper' is a type of paper coated with a light-sensitive layer of silver bromide, mainly used for making photographic prints.

In Japanese culture, a 'bromide' refers to commercial photographs of celebrities, including geisha, singers, actors, and sports figures.

In geography, 'Bromide' is the name of a small village in south-central Oklahoma. There is also a 'Bromide Basin' in Utah.

original_text: (CNN) -- Investigators in the case of a missing Indiana University student hope forensic evidence will help them determine whether a decomposing body found in a creek Monday north of Indianapolis is connected with the disappearance of Lauren Spierer. Bloomington police are in contact with the Indianapolis Metropolitan police, the agency that recovered the body, but there is not enough information for police to "determine the identity or a basic description of the body," Lt. William Parker said in a statement. Parker hopes a pending forensic examination will provide more information, his statement said. CNN affiliates reported an autopsy on the female body will be performed Tuesday. Bloomington police have been searching for Spierer, 20, since June 2, when she was last seen leaving a sports bar in Bloomington after a night out with friends. In mid-June, police had found possible new clues to Spierer's disappearance -- images of a white pick-up truck caught on a security surveillance video. But they ruled out a connection and were back to square one. Officials have received thousands of tips but none so far have yielded anything concrete.

The smells from waste and spoiling food, as well as a lack of air conditioning, have made it a trip to remember.
