original_text: The year 618 BC was a year of the pre-Julian Roman calendar. In the Roman Empire, it was known as year 136 Ab urbe condita . The denomination 618 BC for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events King Qing of Zhou succeeds his father King Xiang of Zhou as ruler of the Chinese Zhou Dynasty. Births Deaths Duke Gong of Cao, ruler of the state of Cao References
simplified_kindergarten:
Year 618 BC was a year long ago.
In Rome, people called it year 136 Ab urbe condita.
Today, we call it 618 BC.
People started to use this name a long time ago.
This was when the Anno Domini calendar became the main way to name years.

King Qing of Zhou became the new king.
His father was King Xiang of Zhou.
King Qing ruled the Zhou land in China.

Duke Gong of Cao died.
He ruled the land of Cao.
simplified_primary_school:
The year 618 BC was a year on an old Roman calendar. In the Roman Empire, people called it year 136 Ab urbe condita. This means it was 136 years after the city of Rome was built.

People started calling it 618 BC a long time ago, in the early Middle Ages. This was when the Anno Domini calendar, which we use today, became the main way to name years in Europe.

In China, King Qing of Zhou became the new ruler after his father, King Xiang of Zhou.

Duke Gong of Cao, who ruled the state of Cao, is also noted for this year.
simplified_secondary_school:
The year 618 BC was part of the Roman calendar system used before the Julian calendar was introduced. In the Roman Empire, it was known as year 136 Ab urbe condita, which means 136 years since the founding of Rome. The name "618 BC" for this year began to be used in the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini (AD) calendar became the most common way to name years in Europe.

A significant event in 618 BC was King Qing of Zhou succeeding his father, King Xiang of Zhou, as the ruler of the Chinese Zhou Dynasty. Also during this year, Duke Gong of Cao, who was the ruler of the state of Cao, died.

original_text: we report on a concept inventory for special relativity : the development process , data analysis methods , and results from an introductory relativity class . the relativity concept inventory tests understanding of kinematic relativistic concepts . an unusual feature is confidence testing for each question . this can provide additional information ; for example high confidence correlated with incorrect answers suggests a misconception . a novel aspect of our data analysis is the use of monte carlo simulations to determine the significance of correlations . this approach is particularly useful for small sample sizes , such as ours . our results include a gender bias that was not present in other assessment , similar to that reported for the force concept inventory .
simplified_kindergarten:
We made a test about fast science ideas. We tell how we made it. We tell how we looked at the answers. We tell what we found from a science class.

This test checks what kids know about how things move very fast.

One special thing is, we ask how sure they are for every question. This helps us learn more. For example, if they are very sure but wrong, it means they might have a wrong idea.

One new way we looked at answers was using a special computer game. It helped us see if our findings were true. This way is good when not many kids take the test, like our test.

What we found shows that boys and girls answered differently. This was not seen in other tests. It was like what they found for another science test.
simplified_primary_school:
We are telling you about a test we made for special relativity. We will talk about how we made the test, how we looked at the answers, and what we found from a class learning about relativity. This test checks if people understand ideas about how things move very fast in relativity.

Something special about this test is that for each question, people say how sure they are about their answer. This can tell us more things. For example, if someone is very sure but gets the answer wrong, it means they might have a wrong idea.

A new way we looked at the answers was using special computer games called Monte Carlo simulations. This helped us see if connections between answers were important. This way of doing things is very helpful when not many people take the test, like in our case.

What we found includes that boys and girls answered differently. This was not seen in other tests, but it was like what they found for another test called the Force Concept Inventory.
simplified_secondary_school:
This report describes a new test, called a concept inventory, designed to check understanding of special relativity. We will discuss how we created the test, how we analyzed the results, and what we found when we used it with students in an introductory relativity class. This relativity concept inventory specifically tests students' understanding of concepts related to motion in special relativity.

An unusual feature of our test is that for each question, students also report how confident they are in their answer. This confidence information can give us extra insights. For instance, if a student is very confident but answers a question incorrectly, it often points to a deep misunderstanding, or 'misconception'.

A new part of how we analyzed our data involves using Monte Carlo simulations. These simulations help us figure out if the relationships we see between different pieces of data are truly meaningful or just random. This method is especially helpful when you have a small number of participants, like we did.

Our findings show a difference in performance between genders, which we call a gender bias. This bias was not seen in other types of assessments we used, but it is similar to what has been reported for another well-known test called the Force Concept Inventory.

original_text: The sixth suspect in the gang rape and killing of a 23-year-old woman is a minor and will be tried in juvenile court, police said Monday. The 17-year-old boy, whose name has not been released, was arrested by police in December and accused of taking part in the December 16 assault aboard a New Delhi bus. Police said the suspects attacked the woman and her male companion on a bus, robbed them and dumped them by the side of the road. The woman was badly injured in the attack. She died two weeks later after being flown to Singapore for treatment. The man survived. The five adult suspects are being tried in a "fast-track" court designed to bypass red tape. Hearings began last week, but details about what happens in court cannot be reported under a judge's ruling imposing restrictions on coverage of the case. The case has gripped India and led to calls for stricter laws on sexual assault and changes in cultural attitudes toward women in India, where most women have stories of sexual harassment and abuse on public transportation or on the streets, according to the Indian Council on Global Relations.

Police caught this boy in December. They say he helped hurt the woman on a bus in New Delhi. This happened on December 16.

Police said these people hurt the woman and a man on the bus. They took their things. They left them by the road.

The woman was very hurt. She died two weeks later. She went to another country to get help, but it was too late. The man lived.

Five grown-up people are also in court. Their court is a fast court. The court started last week. A judge said people cannot talk about what happens in court.

This story made many people in India sad. They want new rules to keep women safe. They want people to be kinder to women. A group said many women in India have bad stories about being on buses or outside.

So, this child will go to a special court for children. This child is a 17-year-old boy. We do not know his name. Police caught him in December. They say he was part of the attack on December 16. This happened on a bus in a city called New Delhi. Police said the people involved attacked the woman and her friend on the bus. They stole things from them. Then, they left them by the side of the road. The woman was badly hurt. She was flown to a country called Singapore to get help from doctors. But she died two weeks later. Her friend lived. The other five people involved are adults. They are going to a special court that works very fast. This is to make sure things happen quickly. The court meetings started last week. But a judge said that no one can share what happens inside the court. This case has made many people in India very upset. They want stronger rules to protect women from harm. They also want people to treat women better in India. A group called the Indian Council on Global Relations says that many women in India have been hurt or bothered on buses, trains, or streets.
simplified_secondary_school:
Police announced on Monday that the sixth suspect in the gang rape and killing of a 23-year-old woman is a minor. Because he is underage, he will be tried in a juvenile court. This 17-year-old boy, whose name has not been made public, was arrested by police in December. He is accused of being involved in the attack that happened on December 16 on a bus in New Delhi. Police stated that the suspects attacked the woman and her male friend on the bus, stole from them, and then left them by the side of the road. The woman was severely injured in the attack. She was flown to Singapore for medical treatment but died two weeks later. Her male companion survived.

The five adult suspects are being tried in a special "fast-track" court. This type of court is designed to speed up legal processes by avoiding unnecessary delays. Court hearings for the adult suspects began last week. However, a judge has ruled that details about what happens in court cannot be reported by the media.

This case has deeply affected India, leading to widespread demands for stronger laws against sexual assault and for changes in how women are viewed in Indian culture. According to the Indian Council on Global Relations, most women in India have experienced sexual harassment or abuse on public transportation or in public places.

original_text: Paul Alexandre René Janet (30 April 1823 – 4 October 1899) was a French philosopher and writer. Biography Born in Paris, he became professor of moral philosophy at Bourges (1845–1848) and Strasbourg (1848–1857), and of logic at the lycée Louis-le-Grand, Paris (1857–1864). In 1864 he was appointed to the chair of philosophy at the Sorbonne, and elected a member of the academy of moral and political sciences. He wrote widely on philosophy, politics and ethics, on idealistic lines: La Famille, Histoire de la philosophie dans l'antiquité et dans le temps moderne, Histoire de la science politique, Philosophie de la Revolution Française, etc. However, in the opinion of 1911 Encyclopædia Britannica, these writings are not characterised by much originality of thought. In philosophy he was a follower of Victor Cousin, and through him of G. W. F. Hegel. His principal work, Théorie de la morale, owes much to Immanuel Kant. Works The Materialism of the Present Day, a Critique of Dr. Büchner's System Notes References External links 1823 births 1899 deaths University of Bourges faculty University of Strasbourg faculty University of Paris faculty French philosophers French essayists École Normale Supérieure alumni French male essayists
simplified_kindergarten:
Paul Janet was a man.
He was born in 1823. He died in 1899.
He was from France.
He thought about big ideas. He wrote books.

He was born in Paris.
He became a teacher.
He taught about good and bad.
He taught about how to think.
He taught in many schools.

Later, he taught at a big school.
It was called the Sorbonne.
He joined a special club.
This club thought about good things.
It thought about how people live together.

He wrote many books.
He wrote about big ideas.
He wrote about how countries work.
He wrote about what is right and wrong.
He liked to think about perfect ideas.

One old book said his ideas were not always new.

He liked the ideas of Victor Cousin.
Victor Cousin liked the ideas of G. W. F. Hegel.
Paul Janet liked those ideas too.

His most important book was "Théorie de la morale."
He got many ideas for this book from Immanuel Kant.
He wrote other books too.
simplified_primary_school:
Paul Janet was born on April 30, 1823, and died on October 4, 1899. He was from France. He was a thinker and a writer.

He was born in Paris. He taught about right and wrong thinking at a school in Bourges from 1845 to 1848. Then he taught there in Strasbourg from 1848 to 1857. After that, he taught about how to think clearly at a school in Paris called Louis-le-Grand from 1857 to 1864.

In 1864, he got a special job teaching thinking at a big school called the Sorbonne. He also became a member of a group that studied right and wrong, and how countries are run.

He wrote many books about thinking, how countries are run, and what is right and wrong. He wrote about ideas that were very hopeful. Some of his books were called: "The Family," "History of Thinking in Old Times and Now," "History of How Countries Are Run," and "Thinking About the French Revolution."

But a very old book called the 1911 Encyclopædia Britannica said his ideas were not very new or special.

He followed the ideas of another thinker named Victor Cousin. Victor Cousin followed the ideas of G. W. F. Hegel. So, Paul Janet also followed Hegel's ideas.

His most important book was called "Theory of Morals." He learned a lot for this book from another thinker named Immanuel Kant.

He also wrote a book called "The Materialism of the Present Day," which looked closely at Dr. Büchner's ideas.
simplified_secondary_school:
Paul Alexandre René Janet (April 30, 1823 – October 4, 1899) was a French philosopher and writer, born in Paris.

He began his career as a professor of moral philosophy, teaching at Bourges from 1845 to 1848, and then at Strasbourg from 1848 to 1857. After that, he taught logic at the lycée Louis-le-Grand in Paris from 1857 to 1864. In 1864, he was appointed professor of philosophy at the Sorbonne and also became a member of the academy of moral and political sciences.

Janet wrote extensively on philosophy, politics, and ethics, often from an idealistic perspective. His many works include *La Famille*, *Histoire de la philosophie dans l'antiquité et dans le temps moderne*, *Histoire de la science politique*, and *Philosophie de la Revolution Française*. Another notable work was *The Materialism of the Present Day, a Critique of Dr. Büchner's System*. However, the 1911 Encyclopædia Britannica stated that his writings were not known for much originality of thought.

In philosophy, Janet followed Victor Cousin, who in turn was influenced by G. W. F. Hegel. His most important work, *Théorie de la morale*, was greatly influenced by Immanuel Kant.

original_text: Fallen Leaf Lake, once called Dead Lake, is a lake in Camas, Washington. Once home to Dead Lake Cemetery, its graves were moved to Camas Cemetery in 1984. This property had been owned by the local Camas paper mill for decades. Most recently, the mill was owned by the Georgia-Pacific Corporation, a subsidiary of Koch Industries, Inc. In 2011, the property surrounding Fallen Leaf Lake was purchased from Koch RP Holdings I, LLC by the City of Camas, Washington for use as a city park. References Camas, Washington Lakes of Clark County, Washington Lakes of Washington (state)
simplified_kindergarten:

A cemetery was here before. It was called Dead Lake Cemetery. The graves moved. They went to Camas Cemetery. This happened in 1984.

A paper mill owned this land. It was the Camas paper mill. They owned it for many years.

Later, Georgia-Pacific owned the mill. Georgia-Pacific was part of Koch Industries.

In 2011, the City of Camas bought the land. This land was around Fallen Leaf Lake. Koch owned the land then. Now it is a city park.

For many years, a paper factory in Camas owned the land around the lake. The paper factory was last owned by a company called Georgia-Pacific. This company is part of a bigger company called Koch Industries.

In 2011, the city of Camas bought the land around Fallen Leaf Lake. They bought it from a company called Koch RP Holdings I, LLC. The city wanted to make it a park for everyone to use.

Most recently, the mill belonged to the Georgia-Pacific Corporation, which is a subsidiary of Koch Industries, Inc. In 2011, the City of Camas, Washington, purchased the property surrounding Fallen Leaf Lake from Koch RP Holdings I, LLC to use as a city park.

original_text: In telecommunication, the Aeronautical Emergency Communications System Plan (AECS) provides for the operation of aeronautical communications stations, on a voluntary, organized basis, to provide the President and the Federal Government, as well as multiple heads of state and local governments, or their designated representatives, and the aeronautical industry with an expeditious means of communications during an emergency. References Title 47 of the Code of Federal Regulations Telecommunications in the United States Emergency communication
simplified_kindergarten:
AECS is a plan.
It helps planes talk.
It helps people talk to planes.
This is for when there is trouble.

People help with the plan. They want to help.
The plan is set up.
It helps many people talk fast.
It helps the President.
It helps other big leaders.
It helps city leaders.
It helps plane companies.
They can all talk fast when trouble happens.
simplified_primary_school: The Aeronautical Emergency Communications System Plan (AECS) is a way to send important messages quickly. It helps run special radio stations for airplanes. People volunteer to help with this plan, and it is well organized. Its job is to make sure the President, the country's government, leaders of states and towns (or people they choose), and airplane companies can talk fast during an emergency. You can find more information about this plan in a book called 'Title 47 of the Code of Federal Regulations.' It talks about sending messages in the United States during emergencies.
simplified_secondary_school:
The Aeronautical Emergency Communications System Plan (AECS) is a telecommunication plan. It allows aeronautical communication stations to operate voluntarily and in an organized way. During an emergency, the AECS provides a fast way for these groups to communicate:
*   The President and the Federal Government.
*   Leaders of state and local governments, or their chosen representatives.
*   The aviation industry.
This plan is detailed in Title 47 of the Code of Federal Regulations, specifically under sections related to Telecommunications in the United States and Emergency communication.

Corneille is a name that some people have. It can be a first name or a last name.

Here are some important people who have the name Corneille as their first name:

Corneille of Berghes lived a long time ago (around 1490 to 1560). He was a church leader and a ruler of a city called Liège.
Corneille of Burgundy lived a very long time ago (from 1420 to 1452). He was a son of Philip the Good, Duke of Burgundy, but his parents were not married.

original_text: (CNN) -- Rubber ducky, you ARE the one -- the chosen one, that is, for this year's induction into the National Toy Hall of Fame. The rubber duck shares the honor with the game of chess as a fellow 2013 inductee, announced today on the hall's website. Inductees are selected on the basis of several factors including icon-status, longevity and innovation. Rubber duck and chess squeaked and check mated past 10 other finalists, including Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, Pac-Man and My Little Pony to win this year's honor. Toys previously inducted into the Hall of Fame include Monopoly (1998), roller Skates (1999), jack-in-the-box (2005) and last year's inductees: Star Wars action figures and dominoes. The National Toy Hall of Fame is in Rochester, New York. Which toy got your vote? Tell us on CNN Living's Facebook page, or on Twitter @CNNLiving.

Dominoes got in last year too.

The Toy Hall of Fame is in Rochester, New York.

Other toys are already in the Hall of Fame. These include Monopoly (from 1998), roller skates (from 1999), and the jack-in-the-box (from 2005). Last year, Star Wars action figures and dominoes joined.

original_text: (CNN)Scarlett Johansson has become the go-to woman for action movies. Johansson's casting lit up social media on Monday because of the hardcore fan base from the original anime film from 1995, which was based on a manga series. The original film is considered a cyberpunk classic. It's about a future society where people have cyberbrains, allowing them to plug into computer networks. A police force is assigned specifically to track down hackers, including the movie's main character, Major Motoko Kusanagi. DreamWorks' Steven Spielberg has been wanting to adapt the movie for many years, and Rupert Sanders is attached to direct.

original_text: the success of density functional theory for the description of the adsorption of atoms on surfaces is well established , and based on recent calculations using gradient corrections , it has been shown that it also describes well the dissociative adsorption of molecules at surfaces admittedly however , the data base for reactions at surfaces is still somewhat small . in the present paper the power of density functional theory calculations is demonstrated by investigations for two different adsorption systems , namely , one with a strongly electropositive adsorbate [ na on al(111 ) ] and one with a strongly electronegative adsorbate [ o on ru(0001 ) ] . in each case , new hitherto not expected adsorbate phases have been predicted by the theory : for na on al(111 ) the stability of a `` four - layer '' surface alloy was identified while for o on ru(0001 ) it was predicted that the formation of a @xmath0-o adlayer should be possible which implies that the apparent saturation coverage of @xmath1 is due to kinetic hindering .
simplified_kindergarten:
A special idea helps us. It shows how tiny things stick to tops. We know this idea works well.

New math work uses this idea. It shows how bigger tiny things break. Then they stick to tops too.

But we do not have much to learn from yet. This is about things happening on tops.

Here, we show how good this special idea is. We look at two ways things stick.

One is like salt on metal. The other is like air on another metal.

For both, the idea showed us new ways for things to stick. We did not think of these ways before.

For salt on metal, it showed a strong mix. This mix has four layers on top.

For air on the other metal, it said a special air layer can form. This means when it looks like no more air can stick, it is just because it is hard for it to move and stick.
simplified_primary_school:
A special science idea called density functional theory is very good at explaining how tiny bits called atoms stick to surfaces. New studies show it also explains well how bigger bits called molecules break apart and stick to surfaces. But we don't have many examples of how things react on surfaces yet.

In this paper, scientists show how well density functional theory works by looking at two different ways things stick. One is when a bit (sodium, Na) really likes to give away its energy when it sticks to aluminum (Al). The other is when a bit (oxygen, O) really likes to take energy when it sticks to ruthenium (Ru).

For both examples, this science idea predicted new ways the bits could stick that no one expected. For sodium on aluminum, it found that a special 'four-layer' mix on the surface could stay put. And for oxygen on ruthenium, it said that a special layer of oxygen could form on the surface. This means that if only a certain amount of oxygen seems to stick, it's because it's hard for more to get there, not because there's no more room.
simplified_secondary_school:
Density functional theory (DFT) is known to be very successful at explaining how individual atoms stick to surfaces. More recent calculations, using an improved version of DFT called gradient corrections, have also shown that it accurately describes how molecules break apart and then stick to surfaces. Despite this, the amount of experimental data available for reactions on surfaces is still quite limited.

This paper demonstrates the power of DFT calculations by investigating two different systems where substances stick to surfaces. One system involves a strongly electropositive substance (sodium, Na) on an aluminum surface (Al(111)), meaning it readily gives up electrons. The other involves a strongly electronegative substance (oxygen, O) on a ruthenium surface (Ru(0001)), meaning it readily accepts electrons.

In both of these studies, the theory predicted new arrangements of the adsorbed substances that had not been expected before. For sodium on aluminum, DFT identified that a stable "four-layer" surface alloy could form, meaning the sodium atoms would mix into the top layers of the aluminum surface. For oxygen on ruthenium, it predicted that a specific type of oxygen layer, a @xmath0-o adlayer, should be possible. This prediction suggests that the maximum amount of oxygen that appears to stick to the surface (the apparent saturation coverage of @xmath1) is actually limited by how quickly the process can occur (kinetic hindering), rather than by the available space.

original_text: we present a fully automatic method for music classification , based only on compression of strings that represent the music pieces . the method uses no background knowledge about music whatsoever : it is completely general and can , without change , be used in different areas like linguistic classification and genomics . it is based on an ideal theory of the information content in individual objects ( kolmogorov complexity ) , information distance , and a universal similarity metric . experiments show that the method distinguishes reasonably well between various musical genres and can even cluster pieces by composer .
simplified_kindergarten:
We made a way to sort music. It works all by itself. It makes music words small.

It does not need to know about music. It can sort many things. You can use it for words. You can use it for other kinds of sorting.

It uses a big idea. It looks at what is inside things. It sees how things are different. It sees how things are the same.

We tried it. It can tell music types apart. It can group songs by who made them.
simplified_primary_school: We have a way to sort music all by itself. It just looks at how small you can make the computer code for each song. It doesn't need to know anything about music. It's a very broad idea. You can use it for other things too, without changing it, like sorting words or looking at DNA. It uses a special idea about how much 'stuff' is in each thing (called Kolmogorov complexity). It also looks at how different things are based on their information, and a way to measure how alike things are for everything. Tests show that this way can sort different types of music pretty well. It can even group songs by who wrote them.
simplified_secondary_school: We have developed a completely automatic method for classifying music. It works simply by compressing the digital information (called 'strings') that represents each piece of music. This method doesn't need any background knowledge about music at all. It's so versatile that it can be used without changes in other areas, such as classifying languages or genetic data. The method is built on an advanced theory about the amount of information in individual items, known as Kolmogorov complexity. It also uses concepts like 'information distance' (how different two pieces of information are) and a 'universal similarity metric' (a way to measure how similar things are that works for everything). Our experiments show that this method can quite accurately tell the difference between various musical genres and can even group songs by the same composer.

original_text: England have been caught up in a fresh team-sheet embarrassment. Just three days after sponsors Vauxhall tweeted a team-sheet which included players’ passport numbers, the line-ups ahead of Saturday’s game against Honduras named the suspended Raheem Sterling among the substitutes. Blunder: The suspended Raheem Sterling was named among the subs on the team-sheet . Sidelined: The injured Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain and Sterling sat out of the game against Honduras . Seeing red: Sterling was sent off during the 2-2 draw with Ecuador on Wednesday . The Liverpool forward was sent off during the 2-2 draw with Ecuador on Wednesday night, picking up a one-game ban to be served against the Hondurans. But the official team-sheet at the Sun Life Stadium in Miami included Sterling on the bench but had no place for Jack Wilshere, despite the Arsenal midfielder warming up on the pitch. Manager Roy Hodgson has indicated that Sterling would have started the game, hinting at his involvement for the World Cup opener against Italy next weekend. Passport numbers, incidentally, were not published on the team-sheet ahead of the Honduras game. Security breach: A picture of the England team-sheet against Ecuador was tweeted which contained the players' passport details .

But he could not play.
Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain was hurt.
He could not play either.

This was on Wednesday.
He had to leave that game.
So he could not play in the next game.
That game was against Honduras.

The real team list had Raheem Sterling.
But Jack Wilshere was not on it.
Jack was ready to play.

England had another problem with their team list.

Just a few days before, a team list for a game against Ecuador was shared online by mistake. It showed players' passport numbers.

Then, for Saturday's game against Honduras, the team list had another mistake. It showed Raheem Sterling as a player who could come on, but he was not allowed to play. Raheem Sterling had been sent off the field in a game against Ecuador on Wednesday. Because he was sent off, he was banned from playing in the next game against Honduras.

The official team list for the game against Honduras showed Raheem Sterling as a player who could come on. But it did not show Jack Wilshere, even though Jack was warming up on the field. Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain was also hurt and could not play in the game.

The team's manager, Roy Hodgson, said that Raheem Sterling would have started the game if he could play. This means Raheem might play in the first World Cup game against Italy next week. The team list for the Honduras game did not have passport numbers on it.
simplified_secondary_school: England has faced another embarrassing team-sheet mistake. Then, for Saturday's game against Honduras, the official line-up listed Raheem Sterling as a substitute, even though he was suspended. The Liverpool forward had received a one-game ban after being sent off during Wednesday night's 2-2 draw with Ecuador, meaning he was unable to play against Honduras. Both Sterling and the injured Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain sat out of the game. The official team-sheet at the Sun Life Stadium in Miami not only included Sterling on the bench but also surprisingly left out Arsenal midfielder Jack Wilshere, who was warming up on the pitch. Manager Roy Hodgson had indicated that Sterling would have started the game, suggesting his involvement in the World Cup opener against Italy next weekend. Importantly, the team-sheet for the Honduras game did not include any passport numbers.

original_text: By . Associated Press . Thirty coins stamped by the U.S. Mint in the 19th century to test designs and metals have sold at auction for more than a half-million dollars. Dallas-based Heritage Auctions had expected the coins to sell Thursday night for more than $300,000. Heritage spokesman Noah Fleisher says they sold for a total of $505,440. This image provided by Heritage Auctions shows one of the historic experimental coins from the collection of the late Texas businessman and philanthropist Harry W. Bass, Jr. that will be offered by Heritage Auctions at the 2014 World's Fair of Money on Aug. 7, 2014 near Chicago. (AP Photo/Heritage Auctions) The biggest price was $76,375 for an 1871 silver dollar struck in aluminum and believed to be unique. The coins from the collection of Dallas businessman and philanthropist Harry W. Bass Jr., who died in 1998 at age 71, were sold individually at the American Numismatic Association's World's Fair of Money near Chicago. The proceeds will go to the Harry W. Bass Jr. Foundation, which helps support organizations in areas such as education and the arts.

simplified_secondary_school:
Thirty experimental coins, made by the U.S. Mint in the 19th century to test new designs and metals, have sold at auction for over half a million dollars. Dallas-based Heritage Auctions had expected the coins to sell for more than $300,000. However, they sold for a total of $505,440, according to Heritage spokesman Noah Fleisher.

The most expensive coin was an 1871 silver dollar struck in aluminum, which sold for $76,375. This coin is believed to be unique.

These coins came from the collection of Harry W. Bass Jr., a Dallas businessman and philanthropist who passed away in 1998 at age 71. They were sold individually by Heritage Auctions at the American Numismatic Association's World's Fair of Money on August 7, 2014, near Chicago. The money raised from the sale will go to the Harry W. Bass Jr. Foundation, which supports organizations in fields like education and the arts.

The Greenlandic language is an Inuit-Yupik-Unangan language family member spoken by the people of Greenland. It has three main dialects: Kalaallisut (West Greenlandic), Inuktun (North Greenlandic), and Tunumiisut (East Greenlandic). original_text: Aarhus County or Århus County () is a former county of Denmark (Danish: amt) on the Jutland peninsula. It was created in 1970 by a merger of three counties: Århus, Randers and Skanderborg. The county was abolished effective 1 January 2007, when almost all of it merged into Region Midtjylland (i.e. Region Central Jutland). A very small portion was merged into Region Nordjylland (Region North Jutland). At the time of its abolishment, more than 20,000 people worked for the county. These were Århus, Randers, and Skanderborg. The county stopped existing on January 1, 2007. Almost all of it joined a new area called Region Midtjylland, which means Central Jutland Region. A very small part joined Region Nordjylland. This means North Jutland Region. When the county stopped existing, more than 20,000 people worked for it.
simplified_secondary_school:
Aarhus County, also known as Århus County, was a former administrative region in Denmark. Located on the Jutland peninsula, it was called an 'amt' in Danish. The county was created in 1970 by combining three smaller counties: Århus, Randers, and Skanderborg.

Aarhus County was abolished on January 1, 2007. Almost all of its territory merged into Region Midtjylland, which is also known as Region Central Jutland. A very small part of the county joined Region Nordjylland, or Region North Jutland. At the time it was dissolved, more than 20,000 people worked for Aarhus County.

original_text: In telecommunication, the term bit-stream transmission has the following meanings: 1. In bit-oriented systems, the transmission of bit strings. 2. In character-oriented systems, the transmission of bit streams that represent characters. In bit-stream transmission, the bits usually occur at fixed time intervals, start and stop signals are not used, and the bit patterns follow each other in sequence without interruption.
simplified_kindergarten:
"Bit-stream transmission" is how we send tiny bits of information.
It means sending many bits.
It also means sending bits that make letters or numbers.

The bits come at the same time.
There are no "start" or "stop" signs.
The bits just keep coming. They do not stop.
simplified_primary_school:
When computers send messages, the word "bit-stream transmission" means sending tiny pieces of information, called bits, in a steady flow.

This word has two meanings:
1. For computers that work with bits, it means sending groups of these bits.
2. For computers that work with letters and numbers, it means sending flows of bits that stand for those letters and numbers.

When bits are sent this way, they usually come at the same speed, one after the other. There are no special signals to say "start" or "stop." The groups of bits just keep coming, without any breaks.
simplified_secondary_school:
In telecommunication, "bit-stream transmission" refers to sending a continuous flow of bits. Specifically, it has two meanings:
1. In systems designed around bits (bit-oriented systems), it means sending sequences of bits, known as bit strings.
2. In systems designed around characters (character-oriented systems), it means sending streams of bits that represent characters.

During bit-stream transmission, bits usually appear at fixed time intervals. Start and stop signals are not used, and the patterns of bits follow each other continuously without any interruptions.

original_text: Neerabup National Park is a national park in the City of Wanneroo in Western Australia, situated approximately north of Perth. The park is found to the west of Wanneroo Road and is a long thin strip of bushland that is about in length. It contains no car parks and roads or any other facilities and attracts no entrance fee. The area protects part of an ancient Indigenous Australian migration route between Lake Joondalup and Loch McNess (in Yanchep National Park). This later became are well used stock route and then part of the Yaberoo Budjara Heritage Trail. The trail is based on the Yellalonga tribes movements between the lakes and highlights features of historical, aboriginal and natural significance. The park lies within the Northern Swan Coastal Plain Important Bird Area, so identified by BirdLife International because of its importance in supporting several thousand Short-billed Black Cockatoos during the non-breeding season. See also Protected areas of Western Australia References National parks of Western Australia Protected areas established in 1965 Important Bird Areas of Western Australia Neerabup, Western Australia

simplified_primary_school:
Neerabup National Park is a special park in Wanneroo City, Western Australia. It is north of Perth. The park is west of Wanneroo Road. It is a long, thin strip of bushland.

There are no car parks, roads, or other things like toilets or shops in the park. You do not have to pay to get in.

The park helps to protect a very old path. Aboriginal people used this path to travel between Lake Joondalup and Loch McNess. Loch McNess is in Yanchep National Park. Later, people used this path to move farm animals. Then it became part of a special walking path called the Yaberoo Budjara Heritage Trail. This walking path follows the way the Yellalonga tribe moved between the lakes. It shows important things about history, Aboriginal culture, and nature.

The park is in a special place for birds called the Northern Swan Coastal Plain Important Bird Area. BirdLife International said it was important. This is because many thousands of Short-billed Black Cockatoos live there when they are not having babies.
simplified_secondary_school:
Neerabup National Park is located in the City of Wanneroo, Western Australia, approximately north of Perth. It is a long, thin strip of bushland, about in length, found to the west of Wanneroo Road. The park has no facilities like car parks or roads, and there is no entrance fee.

This area protects part of an ancient Indigenous Australian migration route that ran between Lake Joondalup and Loch McNess (which is in Yanchep National Park). This route later became a well-used stock route and is now part of the Yaberoo Budjara Heritage Trail. The trail follows the movements of the Yellalonga tribe between the lakes and points out important historical, Aboriginal, and natural features.

Neerabup National Park is also part of the Northern Swan Coastal Plain Important Bird Area. BirdLife International identified this area as important because it supports several thousand Short-billed Black Cockatoos during their non-breeding season.

original_text: Hjo Municipality (Hjo kommun) is a municipality in Västra Götaland County in western Sweden. Its seat is located in the city of Hjo. The municipality was created in 1971, when the City of Hjo was amalgamated with a part of the rural municipality Värsås. In 1974 parts of Fröjered and Fågelås were added. Geography Geographically it is situated by the western shore of Lake Vättern. The large and fish-rich waters have been the main influence on the industry. The largest rivulet intersecting the municipality from Vättern is the Hjo River. Parts of it have been made a nature reserve. Partly this is due to the salmon (Salmo trutta) and the grayling (Thymallus thymallus) that occur natural in the rivulet, but have had hard times due to dams. References External links Hjo Municipality - Official site Three Wooden Towns - Requires some plugin Municipalities of Västra Götaland County Skaraborg
simplified_kindergarten:
Hjo is a place. It is in Västra Götaland. This is in west Sweden. The main town is Hjo.

Hjo started in 1971. Hjo town joined with part of Värsås. Then in 1974, parts of Fröjered and Fågelås came too.

It is by the west side of Lake Vättern. The big lake has many fish. This helped the jobs there.

The biggest little river goes through Hjo. It is called Hjo River. Some parts of the river are a special nature place.

Fish like salmon and grayling live in the small river. But big walls (dams) made it hard for them.
simplified_primary_school:
Hjo is a town area in western Sweden. It is in a bigger place called Västra Götaland County. The main town in this area is also called Hjo.

This town area started in 1971. Back then, the city of Hjo joined with part of a country area called Värsås. In 1974, parts of Fröjered and Fågelås also joined.

Hjo is next to the western side of a big lake called Vättern. This big lake has many fish. Because of the lake and its fish, many jobs in Hjo are about fishing or things related to the lake.

The biggest small river that flows through Hjo from Lake Vättern is called the Hjo River. Some parts of the Hjo River are now a special place to protect nature. This is because special fish like salmon and grayling live there naturally. But they have had problems because of walls, called dams, built in the river.
simplified_secondary_school:
Hjo Municipality (Hjo kommun) is a local government area in Västra Götaland County, western Sweden. Its main administrative office is located in the city of Hjo. The municipality was formed in 1971 when the City of Hjo merged with part of the rural municipality of Värsås. Later, in 1974, additional areas from Fröjered and Fågelås were added.

Geographically, Hjo Municipality is situated on the western shore of Lake Vättern. The lake's large, fish-rich waters have been the main influence on the industry. The Hjo River is the largest rivulet that flows through the municipality from Lake Vättern. Parts of the Hjo River have been made into a nature reserve. This is partly because salmon (Salmo trutta) and grayling (Thymallus thymallus) naturally live in the rivulet, but they have faced difficulties due to dams.

original_text: It’s a special occasion – but our birthdays are not always that happy. The odds of dying on that day are higher than average, according to a study of millions of death records. The excesses of partying plus accidents while travelling to celebrations are risk factors, warned researchers. Accident: New research suggests the excesses of partying plus accidents while travelling to celebrations are risk factors on birthdays . Suicides triggered by the ‘birthday blues’ may also be to blame, said academics at Chicago University. Researcher Pablo Peña said the phenomenon could be due to people ‘deviating from their usual behaviour’ on their birthday. Details of the demise of more than 25million people over 14 years were examined in the study. The data was recorded in the Death Master File, a list of all deaths in the US which is made public to prevent fraud. Nearly 73,266 people died on their birthday, nearly 7 per cent more than would be expected, reported the journal Social Science & Medicine. Death: Nearly 73,266 people died on their birthday, nearly 7 per cent more than would be expected, reported the journal Social Science & Medicine .

This is more than on other days.

A big study looked at many deaths.
It looked at 25 million people.
They died over 14 years.

People party a lot on their birthday.
They might have accidents.
Like when they go to parties.
These things can be bad.

Some people feel very sad on their birthday.
This is called "birthday blues."
Some sad people might hurt themselves.
Smart people at a big school said this.

A smart person named Pablo said this.
People do different things on their birthday.
They do not act like normal.
This might be why more people die.

About 73,266 people died on their birthday.
This is more than we thought.
It is 7 more out of 100 people.

simplified_primary_school:
Birthdays are special days. But sometimes, they are not very happy. A study looked at many death records. It found that more people die on their birthday than on other days.

Researchers said that too much partying can be a reason. Also, accidents when going to parties can be a reason. Experts at Chicago University also said that some people feel very sad on their birthday. This sadness can make them hurt themselves. A researcher named Pablo Peña said that people might act differently on their birthday. This could be why more people die.

The study looked at how more than 25 million people died. It looked at deaths over 14 years. This information came from a list called the Death Master File. This list has all the deaths in the US. It is public so people cannot cheat.

Almost 73,266 people died on their birthday. This is almost 7 more people out of every 100 than we would think. A science paper called Social Science & Medicine shared this news.
simplified_secondary_school: While birthdays are special occasions, a study of millions of death records shows that the risk of dying on your birthday is higher than average. Researchers warned that excessive partying and accidents while traveling to celebrations are risk factors. Suicides, possibly triggered by "birthday blues," may also be a cause, according to academics at Chicago University. Researcher Pablo Peña suggested this phenomenon could be due to people "deviating from their usual behavior" on their birthday. The study examined the deaths of over 25 million people over 14 years. This data was recorded in the Death Master File, a public list of all deaths in the US created to prevent fraud. The journal Social Science & Medicine reported that nearly 73,266 people died on their birthday, which is almost 7% more than would typically be expected.

simplified_kindergarten:
We learn about sending tiny things. We learn about how we measure them. We learn about how they can be squished.

We try this in good ways. We also try this in not-so-good ways.

We see how to make the sending work very well. We make what we send and what we get be very much alike. We do this with special helper tiny things.

We look closely at sending tiny squished things. We use special squished helper tiny things for this.

These kinds of tiny things are special. Some have more light. Some have less light.

When we use our special squished helper tiny things, we can try different ways to make things best. This gives us different good answers.

We can try two different ways to make things best. One way is to make the sending work very well. The other way is to make what we send and what we get be very much alike.

We look at which way is better. It depends on how much the tiny things are moved. It depends on how much they are squished. It also depends on if things work well or not so well.
simplified_primary_school:
We are studying how to send information, like a special kind of message, using something called 'quantum teleportation'. We also look at how we measure these messages and a special property called 'squeezing'. We study this problem using a special method called the Vaidman-Braunstein-Kimble protocol. We look at it when everything works perfectly and when it doesn't.

We found out how to make the teleportation work as well as it can. This means the message that comes out is very much like the message that went in. We do this by using special 'entangled resources' that are prepared in the best way.

To be more exact, we looked at sending a special kind of message called 'coherent squeezed states'. We used 'squeezed Bell states' as our special entangled resources. These special messages are a type called 'non-Gaussian states'. They include messages where tiny light particles (photons) are added or taken away, which are also 'squeezed states'.

This is different from using other types of entangled resources called 'Gaussian resources'. When we use our special 'non-Gaussian squeezed Bell resources', we can choose different ways to make things work best. These different ways give us different best results.

We can try two different ways to make things work best. One way makes the sent message as much like the original as possible. The other way makes the measurements of the message that comes out as close as possible to the measurements of the message that went in. We compare these two ways. We see how they work differently based on how much the original messages are 'displaced' and 'squeezed'. We also see how they work when everything is perfect or not perfect.
simplified_secondary_school:
We study the continuous-variable quantum teleportation of states, examining how measurements of these states vary (statistical moments of observables) and properties like squeezing. We investigate this process in both ideal and imperfect setups of the Vaidman-Braunstein-Kimble (VBK) protocol.

We show how to maximize the accuracy of teleportation (fidelity) and minimize the difference between the original and teleported states' variances by using specially optimized entangled resources. Specifically, we focus on teleporting coherent squeezed states, using squeezed Bell states as the entangled resources.

These squeezed Bell states are a type of non-Gaussian state, which includes examples like photon-added and photon-subtracted squeezed states. Unlike using entangled Gaussian resources, the use of entangled non-Gaussian squeezed Bell resources allows for different optimization procedures that lead to different results.

We can perform two separate optimization procedures: one aims to maximize the state teleportation fidelity, and the other aims to minimize the difference between the input and output quadrature variances. We compare these two procedures based on the displacement and squeezing of the input states, and on the working conditions in ideal and non-ideal setups.

original_text: The La Ferté-Alais Air Show is an annual meeting of aviation enthusiasts in La Ferté-Alais, France. It takes place during Pentecost at the Cerny airfield, which is owned and operated by the "Amicale Jean-Baptiste Salis" (AJ-BS). The airfield is also the site of a museum which sports an impressive number of pre-World War I up to present aircraft. The B-17 Flying Fortress The Pink Lady used in the motion picture Memphis Belle is a permanent project of the AJ-BS group. During the show, dozens of vintage aircraft are assembled from Europe and the US for display and flight demonstrations. The French air force usually demonstrates some new air capacity (paratroops, etc.) as well as overflight by the Patrouille de France, the French aerobatic team (similar to the Blue Angels, Thunderbirds in the US). Also, Airbus or other aircraft manufacturers will fly their latest commercial aircraft, such as the A-300 series of jetliners. References External links AJ-BS group official Site (french only) Air shows Aviation in France

Many old planes come to the show. They come from Europe and the US. People can look at the planes. The planes also fly in the sky.

The French army planes show new things. People jump from planes. A special team of French planes flies over. They are called Patrouille de France. They do cool tricks in the sky. They are like the Blue Angels in America.

Big plane companies also come. Airbus is one of them. They fly their newest big planes. Like the A-300 planes.

We are introducing new mathematical relationships, called "Hankel determinant identities," that describe the squares of these special polynomials. These relationships are expressed using another type of mathematical expression known as "Schur polynomials."

Using these new relationships, we investigate the 'roots' (the values that make the polynomials equal to zero) of the generalized Vorobev-Yablonski polynomials. We also provide mathematical formulas that describe the edges of the very organized patterns that have been seen in computer calculations, as noted in previous research.

original_text: An Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN) is a 10-character alphanumeric unique identifier assigned by Amazon.com and its partners for product identification within the Amazon organization. They were originated in 1996 by Rebecca Allen, an Amazon software engineer, when it became clear that Amazon was going to sell products other than books. The 10-character format of the ASIN was adopted so that Amazon databases and software, which were designed to expect a 10-character International Standard Book Number (ISBN) field, would not have to be changed to accommodate the new identification format. Usage and structure Each product sold on Amazon.com is given a unique ASIN. For books with a 10-digit International Standard Book Number (ISBN), the ASIN and the ISBN are the same. The Kindle edition of a book will not use its ISBN as the ASIN, although the electronic version of a book may have its own ISBN. The ASIN forms part of the URL of a product detail page on Amazon's website. References ASIN Book terminology Identifiers ja:Amazon.com#ASIN

original_text: Gehry Tower is a nine-story building constructed by architect Frank Gehry; it is located at the Steintor, Goethestraße 13a, in Hanover, Germany. The building was commissioned by the city-owned Hanover Transport Services (üstra), for whom Gehry also designed a bus stop in the city. Constructed of stainless steel, the tower is memorable for the noticeable twist in its outer façade on a ferroconcrete core, making optimal use of the relatively small piece of ground on which it is located. The tower is built from stainless steel and stands out because its outer wall, or façade, has a noticeable twist around a ferroconcrete core. This design cleverly makes the best use of the relatively small piece of land it sits on.

Like many of Gehry's projects, the tower was created using the most advanced technology available at the time. Gehry's office first built a 1:100 scale model. This model was then scanned and put into CAD (Computer-Aided Design) software. This allowed them to calculate the exact dimensions for each individual part, all of which vary in size and shape.

Construction of the Gehry Tower began in 1999 and cost 8.5 million Deutsche Mark. The building officially opened on June 28, 2001.

original_text: in a remarkable paper from 1852 , gotthilf heinrich ludwig hagen measured and explained two fundamental aspects of granular matter : the first effect is the saturation of pressure with depth in a static granular system confined by silo walls generally known as the _ janssen effect_. the second part of his paper describes the dynamics observed during the flow out of the container today often called the _ beverloo law _ and forms the foundation of the _ hourglass theory_. the following is a translation of the original german paper from 1852 .
simplified_kindergarten:
A man named Gotthilf Hagen wrote a paper in 1852. He studied two big things about sand and dirt.

The first thing was how much sand pushes down when it is deep. This happens when sand sits still in a tall box. We call this the Janssen effect.

The second thing in his paper was about how sand moves when it falls out of a box. We now call this the Beverloo law. This helps us understand how sand falls in an hourglass.
simplified_primary_school:

The first thing he found was about how sand pushes down. When sand is not moving inside a tall box, the pushing down stops getting stronger as you go deeper. People often call this the Janssen Effect.

In the second part of his writing, he told about how sand moves when it pours out of a box. Today, this is often called the Beverloo Law. This idea also helps us understand how hourglasses work.

This writing is a copy of the first German writing from 1852.
simplified_secondary_school: In a significant paper from 1852, Gotthilf Heinrich Ludwig Hagen measured and explained two key aspects of granular matter. The first effect he described is how pressure in a static granular system, such as grain confined by silo walls, reaches a maximum and doesn't continue to increase with depth. This is generally known as the Janssen effect. The second part of his paper details the movement observed when granular material flows out of a container, which is now often called the Beverloo law and forms the basis of the hourglass theory.

original_text: In the Integrated Services Digital Network (ISDN), a high-speed communication channel comprising multiple aggregated low-speed channels to accommodate bandwidth-intensive applications such as file transfer, videoconferencing, and high-quality audio. The channels, once bonded, remain so end-to-end, from transmitter to receiver, through the ISDN network. The feature is known variously as multirate ISDN, Nx64, channel aggregation, and bonding. People call this many names. Like multirate ISDN. Or Nx64. Or channel aggregation. Or bonding.

This fast way to send information is called an H channel. It is made when many smaller B channels are joined together. A B channel sends information at 64 kbit/s. H channels are used for things that need more speed than one B channel can give. They are joined in a special way called PRA or PRI.

Once these channels are joined, they stay joined all the way from the start to the end, through the whole ISDN network.

H0 is 384 kbit/s. It is made of 6 B channels.
H10 is 1472 kbit/s. It is made of 23 B channels.

simplified_secondary_school:
In the Integrated Services Digital Network (ISDN), an H channel is a high-speed communication channel designed for applications that need a lot of bandwidth, such as transferring large files, videoconferencing, or high-quality audio.

An H channel is created by combining multiple smaller "bearer B channels." Each B channel transmits data at 64 kbit/s. H channels are used when a single B channel isn't fast enough for an application's needs. These B channels are bonded together within a primary rate access (PRA) or primary rate interface (PRI) frame.

Once these channels are bonded to form an H channel, they remain connected as one unit for the entire communication, from the sender to the receiver, across the ISDN network. This feature is also known as multirate ISDN, Nx64, channel aggregation, or bonding.

Here are some examples of H channels and their speeds:
*   H0 = 384 kbit/s (made of 6 B channels)
*   H10 = 1472 kbit/s (made of 23 B channels)
*   H11 = 1536 kbit/s (made of 24 B channels)
*   H12 = 1920 kbit/s (made of 30 B channels) – This specific channel is used only for international E-carrier systems.

This research investigates "radiative corrections" – small adjustments to physics calculations – that arise from particle pair production during electron-positron annihilation. We just changed the "pull" around it.
The tiny fast bits get much more power. This happens when the "pull" gets smaller.
This makes the light look very bright. It is just like we saw.
It gets cool very fast. This happens when tiny bits bump into other light bits.
This makes the light get dimmer. It is just like we saw.
The "pull" does not have much power. It has much less power than the tiny bits and the light bits.

This does not fit another guess. That guess says the light comes from a slow change in power.
simplified_primary_school:
In December 2013, scientists saw a very quick and bright flash from something called 3c 279. They saw it using a tool called Fermi-LAT. They are using an idea to understand this flash. This idea says that tiny particles called electrons got a lot of energy very quickly because of messy, swirling movements.

Our computer test, which changes over time, shows that we can make the very strong light and the uneven way the light changed look the same. We do this just by changing how strong the magnetic field was, compared to when things were calm. The most energy the electrons can have gets much, much bigger when the magnetic field gets weaker. This makes the light (photons) very strong, just like what scientists saw.

The electrons cool down very fast because they bump into other light particles from outside. This explains why the flash of light faded away. The amount of energy in the magnetic field that we figured out is much smaller than the amount of energy in the electrons and in the light particles. simplified_secondary_school: A model where electrons are randomly accelerated by turbulence was used to explain the very short and bright flare from 3c 279, which was detected by the Fermi-LAT telescope in December 2013. Our simulation, which tracks changes over time, showed that we could successfully explain the very energetic spectrum and the asymmetric shape of the light curve simply by changing the magnetic field strength from what it was during the object's steady period. When the magnetic field becomes weaker, the maximum energy of the electrons greatly increases. This produces the energetic light (photons) that we observed. The electrons cool down quickly because they lose energy by scattering off surrounding light particles (photons). This rapid cooling explains why the light curve fades away. Our calculations show that the energy stored in the magnetic field is much smaller than the energy stored in the electrons and in the light (photons). A weak magnetic field and the rapid changes in brightness do not support a model where the jet is accelerated by slowly releasing energy from its electromagnetic field.

original_text: A mafic (portmanteau of "magnesium" and "ferric") mineral or rock is a silicate mineral or igneous rock rich in magnesium and iron. Most mafic minerals are dark in color, and common rock-forming mafic minerals include olivine, pyroxene, amphibole, and biotite. Common mafic rocks include basalt, diabase and gabbro. Mafic rocks often also contain calcium-rich varieties of plagioclase feldspar. Mafic materials can also be described as ferromagnesian. Chemically, mafic rocks are enriched in iron, magnesium and calcium and typically dark in color. In contrast, the felsic rocks are typically light in color and enriched in aluminium and silicon along with potassium and sodium. The mafic rocks also typically have a higher density than felsic rocks. The term roughly corresponds to the older basic rock class. Mafic lava, before cooling, has a low viscosity, in comparison with felsic lava, due to the lower silica content in mafic magma. Water and other volatiles can more easily and gradually escape from mafic lava. As a result, eruptions of volcanoes made of mafic lavas are less explosively violent than felsic-lava eruptions. They use it to guess how special bouncing events usually happen in tiny paths for electrons and light. They do this using a special math idea. We show how this bouncing theory works in an easy way. We check if its guesses are right by comparing them to another bouncing model where we already know the answers. We also use it to see how a tiny wave-like particle bounces off a jumbled surface. simplified_secondary_school:
A scattering theory, first developed many years ago to understand how atomic nuclei scatter particles, has become a powerful tool. It helps scientists study universality in scattering processes that involve open quantum systems with underlying classically chaotic dynamics. More recently, this theory has been applied to predict the statistical properties of scattering resonances in mesoscopic electron waveguides and electromagnetic waveguides, using random matrix theory.

In this work, we provide a simple explanation of this scattering theory. We then compare its predictions to those from an exactly solvable scattering model. We also use it to study how a particle wave scatters from a random potential. This method may prove useful in distinguishing the effects of chaos from the effects of disorder in real scattering processes.

original_text: Tombaatar is a mammal genus that existed during the Mongolian Upper Cretaceous period. It co-existed with some of the late dinosaurs. This animal was a member of the extinct order Multituberculata, within the suborder Cimolodonta and family Djadochtatheriidae. The genus Tombaatar was named by Rougier G.W., Novacek M. and Dashzeveg D. in 1997. The species Tombaatar sabuli is known from remains found in the Upper Cretaceous Djadokhta Formation of Ukhaa Tolgod, Mongolia. Tombaatar was a relatively large multituberculate. The skull is about 6 cm in length. References Rougier, Novacek & Dashzeveg (1997), "A new multituberculate from the late Cretaceous locality Ukhaa Tolgod, Mongolia: Considerations on multituberculate interrelationships", American Museum Novitates 3191, p. 1-26. Kielan-Jaworowska, Z. & Hurum, J.H. (2001), "Phylogeny and Systematics of multituberculate mammals", Paleontology 44, p. 389-429. Much of this information has been derived from Mesozoic Mammals: Djadochtatherioidea, an Internet directory. Cimolodonts Cretaceous mammals Extinct mammals of Asia Prehistoric mammal genera

original_text: (CNN) -- Tenants of apartments in Pacifica, California, were under evacuation Thursday after erosion threatened the cliff on which their building sits, a city official said. Authorities have been watching the seaside building for years and were waiting for the erosion to reach a 12-foot safety zone behind it, said Doug Rider, a building official for Pacifica. The problem has reached that zone, he said, so officials notified the building's management and tenants began evacuating. The building is stable, so tenants are able to remove belongings from the apartments, Rider said. Police, fire and other local authorities are assisting residents, along with the city's Building Department, he said. All residents of the 12-unit building must be out by 5 p.m. PT Thursday, he told CNN affiliate KGO-TV in San Francisco, California. KGO reported a large chunk of land fell from the cliff into the Pacific Ocean about 5:30 a.m. PT Thursday. Two nearby buildings also face questions about their structural reliability but are not being evacuated, the station reported. Pacifica is about 15 miles southwest of San Francisco. CNN's Sara Pratley contributed to this report.

original_text: Tartar sauce (French: sauce tartare; spelled tartare sauce in the UK, Ireland, New Zealand, Australia, Fiji, India, Pakistan, South Africa, and other parts of the Commonwealth) is a condiment made of mayonnaise, chopped pickles, capers and herbs such as tarragon and dill. Tartar sauce can also be enhanced with the addition of other varieties of herbs, lemon juice, or olives. It is most often served as a condiment with seafood dishes such as fried fish and fried oysters. Composition Tartar sauce is based on either mayonnaise (egg yolk, mustard or vinegar, bitartrate, oil) or aioli (olive oil, garlic), with certain other ingredients added. In the UK, recipes typically add to the base capers, gherkins, lemon juice, and dill. US recipes may include chopped dill pickles, onions (or chives), and fresh parsley. Chopped hard-boiled eggs or olives are sometimes added, as may be Dijon mustard and cocktail onions. Paul Bocuse described sauce tartare explicitly as a sauce remoulade, See also List of common dips Remoulade Steak tartare Tarator References External links An explanation of the name's origin, from The Straight Dope Tartar sauce and steak A definition at Allrecipes.com Sauces Mayonnaise Tatar cuisine French cuisine

original_text: (CNN)Eight crew members are missing after a cargo vessel overturned off the coast of Scotland, the Royal National Lifeboat Institution said late Saturday. The search for the crew was suspended Sunday night with no sign of them, the Maritime & Coastguard Agency said. A search operation that included a helicopter, coast guard rescue teams and a fixed-wing aircraft was unsuccessful, the agency said. The coast guard in Shetland was notified at 2:30 p.m. that the hull of the Cemfjord was found 11 miles east of Pentland Skerries, the RNLI said. Photographs showed the vessel in a vertical position, one end jutting from the water. Meanwhile, 25 crew members were airlifted from a boat that ran aground off the coast of southern England, below Southampton, the Maritime & Coastguard Agency reported. Helicopters lifted the crew from the car carrier, which listed dramatically to one side. The ship ran aground on Bramble Bank near the Isle of Wight.

Photographs showed the vessel in a vertical position, with one end sticking out of the water. The Maritime & Coastguard Agency reported that helicopters airlifted the crew from the ship, which was leaning dramatically to one side. The vessel had become stuck on Bramble Bank, close to the Isle of Wight.

original_text: the evolving galaxy is considered as a system of baryonic fragments embedded into the static dark nonbaryonic ( dh ) and baryonic ( bh ) halo and subjected to gravitational and viscous interactions . although the chemical evolution of each separate fragment is treated in the frame of one zone close box model with instantaneous recycling , its star formation ( sf ) activity is a function of mean local gas density and , therefore , is strongly influenced by other interacting fragments . in spite of its simplicity this model provides a realistic description of the process of galaxy formation and evolution over the hubble timescale .
simplified_kindergarten:
A galaxy changes. It has many small pieces. These pieces are inside a big, still cloud. The cloud has dark parts and other parts. The pieces pull on each other. They also rub against each other.

What each piece is made of changes. It uses its old parts again quickly. How many stars a piece makes depends on the gas around it. Other pieces that pull on it change how it makes stars.

This simple way of looking at things is good. It shows how galaxies are made. It shows how they change. This is for a very, very long time.
simplified_primary_school: A changing galaxy is thought of as many small parts of regular matter. These parts are inside a big, still cloud. This cloud has dark matter and regular matter. The parts pull on each other and rub against each other. Even though how the stuff inside each small piece changes is looked at using a simple model (where things change and get reused quickly), how many stars are made in a piece depends on how much gas is packed together in that spot. This means other pieces that are pulling and rubbing really affect it. Even though this way of thinking is simple, it shows what really happens when galaxies are made and change. It works for a very, very long time, as long as the universe has been around.
simplified_secondary_school: An evolving galaxy is considered a system of baryonic fragments. These fragments are embedded within a static halo that contains both dark nonbaryonic (DH) and baryonic (BH) material, and they are subjected to gravitational and viscous interactions. Even though the chemical evolution of each individual fragment is described using a 'one zone close box model with instantaneous recycling', its star formation (SF) activity depends on the average local gas density. Consequently, its SF activity is greatly influenced by other interacting fragments. Despite its simplicity, this model provides a realistic description of how galaxies form and evolve over the Hubble timescale.

original_text: to examine a recently proposed hypothesis that silicon nanoparticles are the source of extended red emission ( ere ) in the interstellar medium , we performed a detailed modeling of the mean galactic extinction in the presence of silicon nanoparticles . for this goal we used the appropriate optical constants of nanosized si , essentially different from those of bulk si due to quantum confinement . it was found that a dust mixture of silicon nanoparticles , bare graphite grains , silicate core - organic refractory mantle grains and three - layer silicate - water ice - organic refractory grains works well in explaining the extinction and , in addition , results in the acceptable fractions of uv / visible photons absorbed by silicon nanoparticles : 0.0710.081 . since these fractions barely agree with the fraction of uv / visible photons needed to excite the observed ere , we conclude that the intrinsic photon conversion efficiency of the photoluminescence by silicon nanoparticles must be near 100% , if they are the source of the ere .
simplified_kindergarten:
We want to check an idea.
The idea is that tiny silicon bits make a red light in space.
This red light is called ERE.
We looked at how light gets blocked in space when these tiny silicon bits are there.
We used special numbers for the tiny silicon bits.
These numbers are not like numbers for big silicon.
This is because the bits are so small.
We found that a mix of space dust worked well.
This dust had tiny silicon bits and other kinds of dusty bits.
This mix helped us understand how light gets blocked.
The tiny silicon bits took in some light.
They took in a small part of the UV and visible light.
It was about 7 to 8 parts out of 100.
This amount of light taken in is almost enough to make the ERE light we see.
So, if the tiny silicon bits make the ERE light, they must be very good at it.
Almost all the light they take in must turn into ERE light.
simplified_primary_school:
We wanted to check a new idea. This idea says that tiny silicon bits make a long red glow in space between stars. So, we made a careful computer model. This model looked at how much light from our galaxy gets blocked when these tiny silicon bits are around.

To do this, we used special numbers for how light acts with tiny silicon. These numbers are very different from regular, bigger silicon because the tiny bits are so small.

We found that a mix of different dust bits worked well to explain how light gets blocked. This mix included tiny silicon bits, plain graphite bits, bits with a hard rock center and a tough carbon coating, and bits with three layers: rock, water ice, and a tough carbon coating. This mix also showed that the tiny silicon bits took in a good amount of UV and visible light, between 0.07 and 0.08.

But this amount of light taken in is just barely enough to make the red glow we see. So, we think that if tiny silicon bits really make the red glow, they must be almost perfect at turning light into their own glow.
simplified_secondary_school:
We investigated a recent idea that tiny silicon particles (silicon nanoparticles) cause a glow called extended red emission (ERE) in the space between stars. To do this, we carefully modeled how much light is blocked (galactic extinction) when these silicon nanoparticles are present. For our model, we used the specific light-absorbing properties (optical constants) of nanosized silicon. These properties are very different from those of larger pieces of silicon because of a quantum effect called quantum confinement.

Our findings showed that a mixture of different types of dust particles successfully explained the observed light blocking. This mixture included silicon nanoparticles, plain graphite particles, particles with a silicate core and an outer layer of organic material, and three-layered particles made of silicate, water ice, and organic material. This combination also meant that silicon nanoparticles absorbed an acceptable amount of ultraviolet (UV) and visible light photons, specifically between 7.1% and 8.1% of them.

However, the amount of UV and visible light photons absorbed by silicon nanoparticles only just matches the amount needed to create the ERE we observe. Therefore, if silicon nanoparticles are indeed the cause of ERE, they must be extremely efficient at converting the light they absorb into the light they emit (a process called photoluminescence), with an efficiency close to 100%.

original_text: In telecommunication, equivalent pulse code modulation (PCM) noise is the amount of noise power on a frequency-division multiplexing (FDM) or wire communication channel necessary to approximate the same judgment of speech quality created by quantization noise in a PCM channel. Note 1: The speech quality judgment is based on comparative tests. Note 2: Generally, 33.5 dBrnC ±2.5 dB is considered the approximate equivalent PCM noise of a 7-bit PCM system. References Multiplexing Noise (electronics)
simplified_kindergarten:
Talking far away can have noise.
There is a special noise. It is called PCM noise.
Imagine noise on a phone line.
We want the phone line noise to make talking sound the same as the special PCM noise.
People listen to both. They compare how they sound.
That amount of phone line noise is called equivalent PCM noise.
For a 7-bit talking system, this noise is usually 33.5.
It can be a little more or a little less.
simplified_primary_school:
When people talk on phones, sometimes there is a bad sound, like a hiss. We call this 'noise'.

'Equivalent PCM noise' is a way to measure how much noise is on a regular phone line (or a line that sends many calls at once). This amount of noise makes the call sound just as good (or bad) as a call from a special digital phone system (called PCM) that has its own kind of noise.

People decide how good the sound is by listening to and comparing many phone calls.

Usually, for a special digital phone system (called a 7-bit PCM system), the amount of noise that sounds like 'equivalent PCM noise' is about 33.5 units. It can be a little more or a little less, around 2.5 units.
simplified_secondary_school: In telecommunication, equivalent pulse code modulation (PCM) noise is the amount of noise power on a frequency-division multiplexing (FDM) channel or a wire communication channel that is needed to produce the same judgment of speech quality as the quantization noise created in a PCM channel. This judgment of speech quality is based on comparative tests. For example, in a 7-bit PCM system, the approximate equivalent PCM noise is generally considered to be 33.5 dBrnC, with a possible variation of plus or minus 2.5 dB.
